options.C: Negotiate payment schedules and exception-handling procedures with the acquirer
options.D: Accept delivery of products and render payment in accordance with the agreement

correct_answer_text: Develop and maintain strategic plans, policies, and procedures to meet the needs of potential acquirer organizations; identify opportunities

question: In the case of a mass-produced commercial product or service where no specific acquirer has yet been identified, which function typically represents the acquirer and establishes stakeholder expectations?

options.A: The Quality Management function within the supplier organization
options.B: A marketing, or similar, function within the supplier organization
options.C: The Portfolio Management function within the supplier organization
options.D: An independent regulatory body external to both parties

correct_answer_text: A marketing, or similar, function within the supplier organization

question: Which of the following is listed as a Typical Output of the Supply process in Figure 2.14?

options.A: Acquired system or system element
options.B: Supplied system or system element
options.C: Acquisition agreement
options.D: Validated system

correct_answer_text: Supplied system or system element

question: Which item is listed among the Typical Inputs to the Supply process in Figure 2.14?

options.A: Supply strategy/approach
options.B: Supply response
options.C: Variance/deviation/waiver request
options.D: Supplied system or system element

correct_answer_text: Variance/deviation/waiver request

question: Which of the following appears as a Typical Output (not a Typical Input) of the Supply process per Figure 2.14?

options.A: Project status report/dashboard
options.B: Project decision gate/review result
options.C: Organization strategic plan
options.D: Supply report

correct_answer_text: Supply report

question: Under the "Establish and maintain an agreement" activity of the Supply process, what must be ensured when the agreement is initially established?

options.A: That the supplier alone defines all functional and performance requirements
options.B: An understanding of expectations, including acceptance criteria
options.C: That payment is rendered up front to lock in agreed pricing
options.D: That the agreement is converted from informal to a formal written contract

correct_answer_text: An understanding of expectations, including acceptance criteria

question: A supplier identifies changes that affect the existing agreement with the acquirer. According to the "Establish and maintain an agreement" activity, what should the supplier do next?

options.A: Halt all work until a new agreement is signed from scratch
options.B: Identify the necessary changes, evaluate the related impacts on the agreement, and update the agreement with the acquirer as necessary
options.C: Defer the changes until the final review of performance at agreement closure
options.D: Issue a unilateral variance and proceed without consulting the acquirer

correct_answer_text: Identify the necessary changes, evaluate the related impacts on the agreement, and update the agreement with the acquirer as necessary

question: Under "Execute the agreement," who in the supplier organization holds final approval authority for deliveries made to the acquirer?

options.A: The acquirer's contracting officer
options.B: An external regulator approved by both parties
options.C: The Supply process manager who manages the related activities including relationship building and interaction with organization management
options.D: The quality assurance auditor independent of the Supply process

correct_answer_text: The Supply process manager who manages the related activities including relationship building and interaction with organization management

question: Per the "Execute the agreement" activity, what must the supplier carefully evaluate to identify risks and issues, progress toward mitigation, and adequacy of progress toward delivery?

options.A: The acquirer's organizational strategic plan
options.B: The terms of the agreement, along with cost and schedule performance and potential undesirable outcomes for the organization
options.C: The supplier's marketing white papers and capability brochures
options.D: The Portfolio Management closure records of prior projects

correct_answer_text: The terms of the agreement, along with cost and schedule performance and potential undesirable outcomes for the organization

question: According to the "Deliver and support the product or service" activity, what is typically true about supplier obligations after the final transfer of products and/or services?

options.A: All supplier obligations terminate at the moment of acceptance
options.B: A support agreement is often ongoing after the transfer of products and/or services
options.C: The supplier is required to assume operational ownership of the system
options.D: The supplier must immediately disband the project team upon delivery

correct_answer_text: A support agreement is often ongoing after the transfer of products and/or services

question: When a Supply process cycle concludes, what action does the supplier take before the agreement is retired?

options.A: Issue a final supply payment to the acquirer in accordance with the agreement
options.B: Conduct a final review of performance to extract lessons learned for continued process performance
options.C: Transfer ownership of the project back to the marketing function
options.D: Initiate disposal of the supplied system on behalf of the acquirer

correct_answer_text: Conduct a final review of performance to extract lessons learned for continued process performance

question: In the Acquisition / Supply "two sides of the same coin" relationship, which item is an output of the Acquisition process that becomes a Typical Input to the Supply process?

options.A: Supply response
options.B: Supply agreement
options.C: Request for supply
options.D: Supplied system or system element

correct_answer_text: Request for supply

question: The Supply Process Elaboration notes that agreements span a wide range. Which of the following is NOT an example of the range of formality or financial structure described?

options.A: A written contract with cost plus fixed fee and penalties for late delivery
options.B: A verbal agreement based on informal understanding
options.C: A fixed-price contract with incentives for early delivery
options.D: An automatically self-executing smart contract with no human signatories

correct_answer_text: An automatically self-executing smart contract with no human signatories

question: According to the "common approaches and tips" for the Supply process, why should personnel who will be responsible for agreement execution participate in evaluating and responding to the acquirer's request?

options.A: It transfers contractual liability away from the supplier's executive leadership
options.B: It reduces the start-up time once the project is initiated, which helps recapture the cost of writing the response
options.C: It eliminates the need for the Portfolio Management process to close the agreement
options.D: It satisfies a mandatory ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 staffing requirement

correct_answer_text: It reduces the start-up time once the project is initiated, which helps recapture the cost of writing the response

question: Per the Supply process "common approaches and tips," what is the consequence of NOT making a critical assessment of the organization's ability to execute the agreement before committing to it?

options.A: The acquirer will automatically be entitled to withdraw the request for supply
options.B: The Supply process will be re-categorized as an Acquisition process
options.C: High risk of failure and associated costs, delivery delays, and increased resource commitments will reflect negatively on the reputation of the entire organization
options.D: Portfolio Management will mandate that the supplier disband the project team

correct_answer_text: High risk of failure and associated costs, delivery delays, and increased resource commitments will reflect negatively on the reputation of the entire organization

question: A supplier's project team has just finished evaluating an acquirer's request and is preparing to commit to delivering the product. Which Supply process activity is being performed?

options.A: Prepare for the supply
options.B: Respond to a request for supply of products or services
options.C: Execute the agreement
options.D: Deliver and support the product or service

correct_answer_text: Respond to a request for supply of products or services

question: A supplier is starting the project work and invoking the other handbook processes after the agreement is signed. Which Supply process activity is being performed?

options.A: Prepare for the supply
options.B: Establish and maintain an agreement
options.C: Execute the agreement
options.D: Deliver and support the product or service

correct_answer_text: Execute the agreement

question: Categorize each item in the Supply Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.14).

items.text: Respond to a request for supply of products or services
Deliver and support the product or service

question: According to ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288, the Organizational Project-Enabling Processes are primarily concerned with what?

options.A: Defining detailed technical requirements for the system-of-interest
options.B: Providing the resources needed to enable the project to meet the needs and expectations of the organization's stakeholders
options.C: Executing the technical processes that transform stakeholder needs into a verified system
options.D: Managing the acquirer-supplier contractual agreement throughout the supply chain

correct_answer_text: Providing the resources needed to enable the project to meet the needs and expectations of the organization's stakeholders

question: How many Organizational Project-Enabling Processes are identified by ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288?

options.A: Four
options.B: Five
options.C: Six
options.D: Eight

correct_answer_text: Six

question: Which of the following is NOT one of the six Organizational Project-Enabling Processes identified by ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288?

options.A: Life Cycle Model Management
options.B: Portfolio Management
options.C: Project Planning
options.D: Knowledge Management

correct_answer_text: Project Planning

question: At what level are the Organizational Project-Enabling Processes typically concerned with management and improvement of the organization's undertaking?

options.A: Tactical and task-execution level
options.B: Strategic level
options.C: Operational level confined to a single project
options.D: System-element level within a single life cycle stage

correct_answer_text: Strategic level

question: What is the stated purpose of the Life Cycle Model Management process per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288?

options.A: To plan, execute, and control project activities within the schedule and budget baselines
options.B: To define, maintain, and help ensure availability of policies, life cycle processes, life cycle models, and procedures for use by the organization with respect to the scope of ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288
options.C: To provide infrastructure and services to projects to support organization and project objectives throughout the life cycle
options.D: To initiate, sustain, and terminate investments in the organization's projects

correct_answer_text: To define, maintain, and help ensure availability of policies, life cycle processes, life cycle models, and procedures for use by the organization with respect to the scope of ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288

question: The Life Cycle Model Management process establishes and maintains a set of policies and procedures at what level of the organization?

options.A: At the individual project level, customized to each project's tailoring decisions
options.B: At the organization level that supports the organization's ability to acquire and supply products and services
options.C: At the SE working group level, scoped to specific technical disciplines
options.D: At the system-element supplier level to align subcontractor practices

correct_answer_text: At the organization level that supports the organization's ability to acquire and supply products and services

question: Per the description of the Life Cycle Model Management process, the resulting organization policies and procedures are still subject to what action by projects?

options.A: Approval by the customer before they may be used
options.B: Tailoring by projects (see Section 4.1)
options.C: Replacement by project-specific processes that override organizational guidance
options.D: Certification under ISO 9001 before they may be applied

correct_answer_text: Tailoring by projects (see Section 4.1)

question: Per Figure 2.15, which of the following is a typical INPUT to the Life Cycle Model Management process?

options.A: Life cycle models
options.B: Organization processes
options.C: Organization strategic plan
options.D: Life cycle model management report

correct_answer_text: Organization strategic plan

question: Per Figure 2.15, which of the following is identified as a typical OUTPUT (rather than an input) of the Life Cycle Model Management process?

options.A: Organization strategic plan
options.B: Project portfolio
options.C: Quality management corrective action
options.D: Life cycle model management strategy/approach

correct_answer_text: Life cycle model management strategy/approach

question: Per Figure 2.15, which of the following is shown as a typical INPUT to the Life Cycle Model Management process?

options.A: Organization lessons learned
options.B: Organizational measurement data
options.C: Quality management evaluation report
options.D: Life cycle models

correct_answer_text: Quality management evaluation report

question: Per Figure 2.15, the three high-level activities of the Life Cycle Model Management process are best summarized as:

options.A: Plan the project, execute the project, and close the project
options.B: Establish the life cycle processes, assess the life cycle processes, and improve the process
options.C: Acquire resources, deploy resources, and audit resources
options.D: Define requirements, allocate requirements, and verify requirements

correct_answer_text: Establish the life cycle processes, assess the life cycle processes, and improve the process

question: Under the 'Establish the life cycle processes' activity, which of the following is explicitly listed as a sub-task?

options.A: Negotiate the terms of the supply agreement with the acquirer
options.B: Define roles, responsibilities, accountabilities, and authorities to enable the implementation of the life cycle processes
options.C: Allocate cost and schedule reserves to individual projects
options.D: Conduct technical reviews for each project's system-of-interest

correct_answer_text: Define roles, responsibilities, accountabilities, and authorities to enable the implementation of the life cycle processes

question: Which sub-task explicitly belongs to the 'Establish the life cycle processes' activity of the Life Cycle Model Management process?

options.A: Establish entrance and exit criteria for decision gates
options.B: Prioritize and implement the identified improvement opportunities
options.C: Use assessments and reviews of the life cycle models' performance to confirm adequacy and effectiveness
options.D: Communicate with all relevant organizations regarding the creation of and changes in the life cycle model management guideline

correct_answer_text: Establish entrance and exit criteria for decision gates

question: Identifying opportunities to improve the organization's life cycle model management guidelines based on individual project assessments, individual feedback, metrics, and changes in the organization strategic plan is part of which Life Cycle Model Management activity?

options.A: Establish the life cycle processes
options.B: Assess the life cycle processes
options.C: Improve the process
options.D: Tailor the project's process

correct_answer_text: Assess the life cycle processes

question: Within the 'Improve the process' activity, which of the following sub-tasks is explicitly identified?

options.A: Establish tailoring guidance for projects
options.B: Define an appropriate set of life cycle models that are comprised of stages
options.C: Prioritize and implement the identified improvement opportunities
options.D: Establish process performance metrics to assess effectiveness and efficiency

correct_answer_text: Prioritize and implement the identified improvement opportunities

question: A project manager asks why the organization-level Life Cycle Model Management process produces life cycle models and tailoring guidance instead of letting each project define its own from scratch. Which response best reflects the handbook's rationale?

options.A: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 forbids projects from selecting their own life cycle stages
options.B: Project Planning is technically prohibited from selecting a life cycle model
options.C: Organization-wide processes provide repeatable/predictable performance across projects, leverage proven practices, enable process improvement, and reduce reinvention at project startup
options.D: Only the Quality Management process is permitted to define life cycle models

correct_answer_text: Organization-wide processes provide repeatable/predictable performance across projects, leverage proven practices, enable process improvement, and reduce reinvention at project startup

question: Which of the following best distinguishes the organization-level Life Cycle Model Management process from the project-level selection and execution of a life cycle model?

options.A: Life Cycle Model Management defines and maintains the set of organization policies, processes, and life cycle models, while projects tailor and apply them to a specific system-of-interest
options.B: Life Cycle Model Management performs technical verification of each project's system, while projects only document the results
options.C: Life Cycle Model Management is performed by the acquirer, while life cycle execution is performed by the supplier under an agreement
options.D: Life Cycle Model Management is a project-level process, while Project Planning is the corresponding organization-level process

correct_answer_text: Life Cycle Model Management defines and maintains the set of organization policies, processes, and life cycle models, while projects tailor and apply them to a specific system-of-interest

question: Which 'common approaches and tips' recommendation aligns specifically with the Life Cycle Model Management process?

options.A: Negotiate fixed price, cost plus fixed fee, or incentive terms with the acquirer
options.B: Develop alternative life cycle models based on the type, scope, complexity, and risk of a project to decrease the need for tailoring by engineering and project organizations
options.C: Allocate physical workspace, communications, and IT services to the project team
options.D: Define the staffing plan and skills inventory for the organization's personnel

correct_answer_text: Develop alternative life cycle models based on the type, scope, complexity, and risk of a project to decrease the need for tailoring by engineering and project organizations

question: Per the handbook, SE benchmarking in the context of Life Cycle Model Management is best described as:

options.A: Verifying that the system-of-interest meets all stakeholder requirements before delivery
options.B: Measuring an individual engineer's productivity against organizational targets
options.C: Comparing an organization's system life cycle processes and practices to those of other entities considered good performers, or to industry standards or good practices, to generate ideas for process improvement
options.D: Auditing each project's compliance with the contract baseline

correct_answer_text: Comparing an organization's system life cycle processes and practices to those of other entities considered good performers, or to industry standards or good practices, to generate ideas for process improvement

question: Per Figure 2.15, which of the following is listed as a typical OUTPUT of the Life Cycle Model Management process?

options.A: Project portfolio
options.B: Organization tailoring strategy/approach
options.C: Organization processes
options.D: Quality management evaluation report

correct_answer_text: Organization processes

question: The handbook notes that the organization-level focus of Life Cycle Model Management is intended to address what scope?

options.A: General business management objectives across the entire enterprise, regardless of SE relevance
options.B: The capabilities of an organization relevant to enabling the system life cycle, though it may sometimes overlap with general business management
options.C: Only the technical processes that produce the system-of-interest
options.D: Only the acquisition and supply contractual interfaces with external customers

correct_answer_text: The capabilities of an organization relevant to enabling the system life cycle, though it may sometimes overlap with general business management

question: Which statement best characterizes the relationship between organizational policies produced by Life Cycle Model Management and the life cycle models used on individual projects?

options.A: Organizational policies and procedures are immutable and may not be adapted by individual projects
options.B: Organizational policies provide integrated life cycle models needed to meet strategic plans, policies, goals, and objectives for all projects and life cycle stages, and are then subject to tailoring by projects
options.C: Each project independently invents its own life cycle model and only retroactively documents it for the organization
options.D: The acquirer mandates the project's life cycle model, bypassing the organization's policies

correct_answer_text: Organizational policies provide integrated life cycle models needed to meet strategic plans, policies, goals, and objectives for all projects and life cycle stages, and are then subject to tailoring by projects

question: Within Section 2.3.3, the handbook clarifies that the Organizational Project-Enabling Processes are intentionally scoped to:

options.A: Manage general business management objectives across the entire enterprise, regardless of life cycle relevance
options.B: The capabilities of an organization relevant to enabling the system life cycle, even though those capabilities sometimes overlap with general business management
options.C: Execute the technical work of producing the system-of-interest
options.D: Replace project-level processes with mandatory enterprise-wide procedures that cannot be tailored

correct_answer_text: The capabilities of an organization relevant to enabling the system life cycle, even though those capabilities sometimes overlap with general business management

question: An organization wants to update its standard set of life cycle models because individual project assessments and metrics have revealed recurring issues. Which Life Cycle Model Management activity covers this work of recognizing the issues and identifying improvement opportunities?

options.A: Establish the life cycle processes
options.B: Assess the life cycle processes
options.C: Improve the process
options.D: Project Planning

correct_answer_text: Assess the life cycle processes

question: After improvement opportunities for the organization's life cycle model management guidelines have been prioritized and implemented, which sub-task within the 'Improve the process' activity must still be carried out?

options.A: Establish entrance and exit criteria for decision gates
options.B: Define roles, responsibilities, accountabilities, and authorities for the life cycle processes
options.C: Communicate with all relevant organizations regarding the creation of and changes in the life cycle model management guideline
options.D: Negotiate the supply agreement with the acquirer

correct_answer_text: Communicate with all relevant organizations regarding the creation of and changes in the life cycle model management guideline

question: Per Figure 2.15, which of the following is identified as a typical INPUT to the Life Cycle Model Management process rather than an output?

options.A: Life cycle models
options.B: Organization procedures
options.C: Organization tailoring strategy/approach
options.D: Life cycle model management report

correct_answer_text: Organization tailoring strategy/approach

question: Per Figure 2.15, which of the following is identified as a typical INPUT to the Life Cycle Model Management process?

options.A: Organization lessons learned
options.B: Organizational measurement data
options.C: Project portfolio
options.D: Life cycle model management records/artifacts

correct_answer_text: Project portfolio

question: Per Figure 2.15, which of the following is a typical OUTPUT of the Life Cycle Model Management process?

options.A: Quality management corrective action
options.B: Organization strategic plan
options.C: Project portfolio
options.D: Organizational measurement data

correct_answer_text: Organizational measurement data

question: The Quality Management evaluation report and Quality Management corrective action appear in Figure 2.15 as inputs to the Life Cycle Model Management process. What does this cross-process linkage indicate?

options.A: Quality Management is a sub-activity of Life Cycle Model Management
options.B: Findings from the Quality Management process feed the assessment and improvement of the organization's life cycle processes and models
options.C: Quality Management replaces the Life Cycle Model Management process for ISO 9001 certified organizations
options.D: Only Quality Management may authorize tailoring of organizational policies

correct_answer_text: Findings from the Quality Management process feed the assessment and improvement of the organization's life cycle processes and models

question: Establishing process performance metrics to assess effectiveness and efficiency of the life cycle processes is a sub-task of which Life Cycle Model Management activity?

options.A: Establish the life cycle processes
options.B: Assess the life cycle processes
options.C: Improve the process
options.D: Portfolio Management

correct_answer_text: Establish the life cycle processes

question: Per the handbook, the 'Assess the life cycle processes' activity uses assessments and reviews of life cycle models' performance for what specific purpose?

options.A: To verify the system-of-interest against stakeholder requirements
options.B: To confirm the adequacy and effectiveness of the Life Cycle Model Management process
options.C: To negotiate cost and schedule baselines with the acquirer
options.D: To allocate human resources across the organization's project portfolio

correct_answer_text: To confirm the adequacy and effectiveness of the Life Cycle Model Management process

question: Among the Life Cycle Model Management activities, where does the responsibility for defining an appropriate set of life cycle models comprised of stages reside?

options.A: Establish the life cycle processes
options.B: Assess the life cycle processes
options.C: Improve the process
options.D: Decision Management

correct_answer_text: Establish the life cycle processes

question: Per the handbook's 'common approaches and tips,' on what should the organization's life cycle policies and procedures be based?

options.A: Only on the latest ISO 9001 audit findings
options.B: On each individual project manager's preferences
options.C: On an organization-level strategic and business area plan that provides a comprehensive understanding of the organization's goals, objectives, stakeholders, competitors, future business, and technology trends
options.D: Exclusively on the acquirer's contractual requirements

correct_answer_text: On an organization-level strategic and business area plan that provides a comprehensive understanding of the organization's goals, objectives, stakeholders, competitors, future business, and technology trends

question: Per the handbook's common approaches and tips for Life Cycle Model Management, where should compliance with organizational policies and procedures be checked?

options.A: Only at project closeout via the final acceptance report
options.B: Included as part of the business decision gate criteria
options.C: Only when the acquirer requests a compliance audit
options.D: Only as part of the Quality Management corrective action process

correct_answer_text: Included as part of the business decision gate criteria

question: The handbook recommends that 'Identify opportunities to improve the organization life cycle model management guidelines' be performed on what basis?

options.A: Only when a project formally requests a deviation
options.B: Only at the end of a contractual reporting period
options.C: On a continuing basis, driven by individual project assessments, individual feedback, metrics, and changes in the organization strategic plan
options.D: Only when triggered by an external regulator

correct_answer_text: On a continuing basis, driven by individual project assessments, individual feedback, metrics, and changes in the organization strategic plan

question: Per the handbook, which artifact is the principal output of Life Cycle Model Management that defines, for projects, the entrance and exit criteria for decision gates and the standard set of life cycle stages they must apply?

options.A: Project portfolio
options.B: Quality management evaluation report
options.C: Organization processes (including life cycle models and tailoring guidance)
options.D: Acquirer agreement

correct_answer_text: Organization processes (including life cycle models and tailoring guidance)

question: Categorize each item in the Life Cycle Model Management Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.15).
Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 as cited in the SE Handbook, what is the purpose of the Infrastructure Management process?

options.A: To initiate and sustain necessary, sufficient, and suitable projects to meet the strategic objectives of the organization
options.B: To provide the infrastructure and services to projects to support organization and project objectives throughout the life cycle
options.C: To define, maintain, and assure availability of skilled personnel needed to conduct organization projects
options.D: To establish the life cycle models used by the organization and to deploy them across active projects

correct_answer_text: To provide the infrastructure and services to projects to support organization and project objectives throughout the life cycle

question: Within the ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 process group structure, the Infrastructure Management process is classified as which type of process?

options.A: A technical management process applied within each project
options.B: A technical process applied to a single System of Interest
options.C: An organizational project-enabling process foundational to all SE process management and improvement
options.D: An agreement process executed between acquirer and supplier

correct_answer_text: An organizational project-enabling process foundational to all SE process management and improvement

question: According to the SE Handbook, which examples does the Infrastructure Management process explicitly identify as infrastructure elements to be defined?

options.A: Facilities, tools, hardware, software, services, and standards
options.B: Funding, budget, schedule, and earned value baselines
options.C: Project staff, contractors, training plans, and reward systems
options.D: Stakeholder requirements, system requirements, and architecture artifacts

correct_answer_text: Facilities, tools, hardware, software, services, and standards

question: Per Figure 2.16 (IPO diagram), which of the following is listed as a typical INPUT to the Infrastructure Management process?

options.A: Infrastructure management strategy/approach
options.B: Organization infrastructure needs
options.C: Project infrastructure
options.D: Infrastructure management report

correct_answer_text: Organization infrastructure needs

question: Per Figure 2.16 (IPO diagram), which of the following is a typical OUTPUT of the Infrastructure Management process?

options.A: Organization strategic plan
options.B: Project infrastructure needs
options.C: Infrastructure management strategy/approach
options.D: Organization infrastructure needs

correct_answer_text: Infrastructure management strategy/approach

question: The SE Handbook organizes the Infrastructure Management activities into two top-level activity groups. What are they?

options.A: Plan the infrastructure and Audit the infrastructure
options.B: Acquire the infrastructure and Dispose of the infrastructure
options.C: Establish the infrastructure and Maintain the infrastructure
options.D: Allocate the infrastructure and Retire the infrastructure

correct_answer_text: Establish the infrastructure and Maintain the infrastructure

question: An SE practitioner is asked to determine the specific resources needed by a single project to meet that project's objectives. According to the SE Handbook, which role and process is responsible for this work?

options.A: The Infrastructure Management process, because it owns all resource decisions in the organization
options.B: Project planners under the Project Planning process determine the resources needed by the project; Infrastructure Management then makes the organization infrastructure aware of those needs and schedules resources to be in place
options.C: The Portfolio Management process, because all resource decisions are financial in nature
options.D: The Human Resource Management process, because resources are equivalent to staffing

correct_answer_text: Project planners under the Project Planning process determine the resources needed by the project; Infrastructure Management then makes the organization infrastructure aware of those needs and schedules resources to be in place

question: The Infrastructure Management process addresses non-human resources except for financial resources. Per the handbook, which process explicitly addresses financial resources?

options.A: Quality Management process
options.B: Life Cycle Model Management process
options.C: Portfolio Management process
options.D: Knowledge Management process

correct_answer_text: Portfolio Management process

question: Per the SE Handbook, human resources are explicitly excluded from the Infrastructure Management process because they are addressed under which process?

options.A: Project Assessment and Control process
options.B: Human Resource Management process
options.C: Acquisition process
options.D: Measurement process

correct_answer_text: Human Resource Management process

question: Which activity does the SE Handbook list under "Establish the infrastructure" for the Infrastructure Management process?

options.A: Continue to assess whether the project infrastructure needs are met
options.B: Allocate infrastructure resources and services to support all projects
options.C: Conduct inventory management to include enumeration, lists, and storage to establish ownership, accessibility, and expectations
options.D: Perform cost analysis toward the cost of infrastructure management

correct_answer_text: Conduct inventory management to include enumeration, lists, and storage to establish ownership, accessibility, and expectations

question: Which activity does the SE Handbook list under "Maintain the infrastructure" for the Infrastructure Management process?

options.A: Define, gather, and negotiate infrastructure resource needs with the organization and projects
options.B: Define infrastructure elements (e.g., facilities, tools, hardware, software, services, and standards)
options.C: Identify, obtain, and provide the infrastructure resources and services to ensure organization goals and objectives are met
options.D: Evaluate infrastructure management alternatives through analysis of alternatives

correct_answer_text: Evaluate infrastructure management alternatives through analysis of alternatives

question: Beyond physical infrastructure, the SE Handbook states the Infrastructure Management process also includes which additional infrastructures?

options.A: Financial and contractual infrastructures
options.B: Political and process improvement infrastructures
options.C: Marketing and customer-relationship infrastructures
options.D: Training and reward infrastructures

correct_answer_text: Political and process improvement infrastructures

question: Per the SE Handbook's "Common approaches and tips," for organizations of approximately what size is computer-based equipment tracking, facilities allocation, and a resource management information system specifically recommended?

options.A: Organizations with over 10 people
options.B: Organizations with over 50 people
options.C: Organizations with over 250 people
options.D: Organizations with over 1,000 people

correct_answer_text: Organizations with over 50 people

question: Per the Infrastructure Management Process Maturity discussion, what is the primary focus of this process?

options.A: The construction of the infrastructure
options.B: The actual day-to-day use of the infrastructure by operators
options.C: The establishment and deployment of infrastructure rather than the construction or actual use of the infrastructure
options.D: The disposal and retirement of obsolete infrastructure elements

correct_answer_text: The establishment and deployment of infrastructure rather than the construction or actual use of the infrastructure

question: Per Figure 2.16, in addition to 'Organization strategic plan' and 'Organization infrastructure needs,' which of the following is also listed as a typical INPUT to the Infrastructure Management process?

options.A: Project infrastructure needs
options.B: Project infrastructure
options.C: Infrastructure management report
options.D: Infrastructure management records/artifacts

correct_answer_text: Project infrastructure needs

question: Per Figure 2.16, which of the following pairs are BOTH listed as typical OUTPUTS of the Infrastructure Management process?

options.A: Organization strategic plan AND Project infrastructure needs
options.B: Organization infrastructure AND Project infrastructure
options.C: Organization infrastructure needs AND Project infrastructure needs
options.D: Project authorization AND Project direction

correct_answer_text: Organization infrastructure AND Project infrastructure

question: An organization's SE leadership wants to roll out a new model-based engineering toolset that will be shared across multiple projects. Per the SE Handbook, this kind of organization-wide provisioning of tools is most properly accomplished under which process?

options.A: Project Planning, because each project must plan its own tool acquisition independently
options.B: Infrastructure Management, which establishes and maintains shared organization infrastructure such as tools and services
options.C: Life Cycle Model Management, because tools are part of the SE life cycle model
options.D: Knowledge Management, because the tool captures engineering knowledge

correct_answer_text: Infrastructure Management, which establishes and maintains shared organization infrastructure such as tools and services

question: Per the SE Handbook's Infrastructure Management Concepts, the infrastructure management organization is described as collecting needs, negotiating to remove conflicts, and providing the enabling organization infrastructure. Why is this provisioning typically centralized at the organization level rather than performed per-project?

options.A: Because ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 prohibits projects from owning any resources
options.B: Because efficient use of resources is achieved by exploiting opportunities to share enabling systems or to use a common system element on more than one project
options.C: Because projects lack the authority to define their own infrastructure requirements
options.D: Because the Portfolio Management process owns all project execution decisions

correct_answer_text: Because efficient use of resources is achieved by exploiting opportunities to share enabling systems or to use a common system element on more than one project

question: Per the SE Handbook, the Infrastructure Management process establishes which type of infrastructure that is NOT strictly physical?

options.A: Financial infrastructure used to allocate funding to active projects
options.B: Human-resource infrastructure used to define skills inventories
options.C: Political and process improvement infrastructures, in addition to the physical infrastructure
options.D: Contractual infrastructure used for acquirer-supplier agreements

correct_answer_text: Political and process improvement infrastructures, in addition to the physical infrastructure

question: A new project requests a specialized environmental test chamber that is not currently part of the organization's infrastructure. According to the SE Handbook's Infrastructure Management Concepts, what is the proper division of responsibility?

options.A: The project planners determine the resource needed; Infrastructure Management is made aware of that need and schedules the resource to be in place when requested
options.B: Infrastructure Management determines whether the project really needs the chamber and may refuse the request
options.C: Portfolio Management decides whether the chamber is acquired because all resource decisions are financial
options.D: Human Resource Management acquires the chamber as part of the labor pool

correct_answer_text: The project planners determine the resource needed; Infrastructure Management is made aware of that need and schedules the resource to be in place when requested

question: Per the SE Handbook, which activity is part of 'Establish the infrastructure' (NOT 'Maintain the infrastructure')?

options.A: Allocate infrastructure resources and services to support all projects
options.B: Evaluate the condition of the infrastructure
options.C: Define infrastructure requirements
options.D: Provide change control for the infrastructure management

correct_answer_text: Define infrastructure requirements

question: Per the SE Handbook, which activity is part of 'Maintain the infrastructure' (NOT 'Establish the infrastructure')?

options.A: Define infrastructure elements (e.g., facilities, tools, hardware, software, services, and standards)
options.B: Conduct inventory management to include enumeration, lists, and storage
options.C: Identify and provide improvements or changes to the infrastructure resources as the project requirements change
options.D: Define, gather, and negotiate infrastructure resource needs with the organization and projects

correct_answer_text: Identify and provide improvements or changes to the infrastructure resources as the project requirements change

question: Per the SE Handbook's 'Common approaches and tips' for the Infrastructure Management process, what specific computer-based capability is recommended to maintain, track, allocate, and improve resources for present and future organization needs?

options.A: A configuration management database (CMDB) compliant with ITIL
options.B: A resource management information system with enabling support systems and services
options.C: An earned value management (EVM) system
options.D: A product line engineering (PLE) variability model

correct_answer_text: A resource management information system with enabling support systems and services

question: Per the SE Handbook, what kind of physical and human factors does it specifically recommend that infrastructure management attend to?

options.A: Ambient noise level and computer access to specific tools and applications
options.B: Travel-and-expense policies and per diem rates
options.C: Stockholder equity and capital structure
options.D: Acquisition and supply contract terms

correct_answer_text: Ambient noise level and computer access to specific tools and applications

question: Per the SE Handbook, the Infrastructure Management process addresses non-human, non-financial resources. Financial resources are handled by Portfolio Management and human resources by Human Resource Management. Which of the following best describes how Infrastructure Management still interacts with the financial concern?

options.A: Infrastructure Management sets the organization's annual budget and approves all expenditures
options.B: Infrastructure Management performs cost analysis toward the cost of infrastructure management, and resource costs are factored into investment decisions
options.C: Infrastructure Management directly allocates funding to each project's general ledger account
options.D: Infrastructure Management cannot consider cost; only Portfolio Management may discuss money

correct_answer_text: Infrastructure Management performs cost analysis toward the cost of infrastructure management, and resource costs are factored into investment decisions

question: Per the SE Handbook, qualified resources used by the Infrastructure Management process may be obtained how, in accordance with the investment strategy?

options.A: Only by direct purchase and capitalization
options.B: Only by internal development under the Life Cycle Model Management process
options.C: By leasing (insourced or outsourced) or by licensing
options.D: Only via supplier agreements under the Acquisition process

correct_answer_text: By leasing (insourced or outsourced) or by licensing

question: Per the SE Handbook, what does Infrastructure Management rely on heavily in order to balance efficiency and robustness when deploying resources?

options.A: Forecasts into the future of the demand and supply of various resources
options.B: Earned value management variance reports from individual projects
options.C: Risk burn-down charts maintained by the Risk Management process
options.D: Strategic objectives published only by the Portfolio Management process

correct_answer_text: Forecasts into the future of the demand and supply of various resources

question: Categorize each item in the Infrastructure Management Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.16).
Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 [6.2.3.1], what is the purpose of the Portfolio Management process?

options.A: To provide the organization with the necessary human resources and maintain their competencies consistent with strategic needs
options.B: To initiate and sustain necessary, sufficient, and suitable projects to meet the strategic objectives of the organization
options.C: To define, maintain, and assure the availability of policies, life cycle processes, and procedures used by the organization
options.D: To create and maintain the infrastructure (facilities, tools, communications, and information technology assets) needed to support the organization and its projects

correct_answer_text: To initiate and sustain necessary, sufficient, and suitable projects to meet the strategic objectives of the organization

question: Beyond initiating and sustaining projects, what additional role does Portfolio Management serve with respect to external parties such as parent organizations, investors/funding sources, and governance bodies?

options.A: It negotiates supplier agreements on behalf of the projects within the portfolio
options.B: It executes detailed cost-accounting reconciliations for each project's general ledger
options.C: It provides organizational output regarding the set of projects, systems, and technical investments of the organization to those external stakeholders
options.D: It collects employee competency data and reports workforce metrics for compliance audits

correct_answer_text: It provides organizational output regarding the set of projects, systems, and technical investments of the organization to those external stakeholders

question: According to Section 2.3.3.3, which of the following is NOT one of the criteria used in periodic assessments to determine whether a project justifies continued investment?

options.A: Contributes to the organization strategy and progresses toward established goals
options.B: Complies with project directives from the organization and is conducted according to an approved plan
options.C: Provides a service or product that is still needed and providing acceptable investment returns
options.D: Maintains the lowest engineering labor rate among all active projects in the portfolio

correct_answer_text: Maintains the lowest engineering labor rate among all active projects in the portfolio

question: Per Figure 2.17 (IPO for Portfolio Management), which item is a typical INPUT to the Portfolio Management process?

options.A: Project authorization
options.B: Project portfolio
options.C: Organization strategic plan
options.D: Portfolio management report

correct_answer_text: Organization strategic plan

question: Per Figure 2.17, which item is identified as a typical OUTPUT (rather than an input) of the Portfolio Management process?

options.A: Business plan
options.B: Project authorization
options.C: Project authorization request
options.D: Variance/deviation/waiver request

correct_answer_text: Project authorization

question: Figure 2.17 lists the three top-level Activities of the Portfolio Management process as which of the following?

options.A: Plan the project; assess and control the project; manage configuration
options.B: Define and authorize projects; evaluate the portfolio of projects; terminate projects
options.C: Acquire products; supply products; manage agreements
options.D: Identify stakeholders; elicit needs; define requirements

correct_answer_text: Define and authorize projects; evaluate the portfolio of projects; terminate projects

question: Within the activity 'Define and authorize projects,' which sub-task is explicitly identified in Section 2.3.3.3?

options.A: Develop the system architecture and allocate functions to physical elements
options.B: Establish project scope, define project management accountabilities and authorities, and identify expected project outcomes
options.C: Perform verification and validation of delivered system elements
options.D: Negotiate individual employee compensation packages for project staff

correct_answer_text: Establish project scope, define project management accountabilities and authorities, and identify expected project outcomes

question: Under the 'Evaluate the portfolio of projects' activity, what action does Portfolio Management take when an ongoing project's evaluation does not support continuation as planned?

options.A: Automatically converts the project into a program with expanded scope
options.B: Transfers the project to the Quality Management process for re-baselining
options.C: Provides direction and supporting actions for continuation or redirection, as applicable for successful completion
options.D: Reassigns the project to an external supplier through the Acquisition process

correct_answer_text: Provides direction and supporting actions for continuation or redirection, as applicable for successful completion

question: Section 2.3.3.3 identifies a 'Terminate projects' activity. What does this activity entail?

options.A: Closing, cancelling, or suspending projects that are completed or designated for termination
options.B: Issuing final pay and benefits to project personnel before reassignment
options.C: Disposing of physical hardware according to environmental regulations
options.D: Archiving configuration items into the organization's knowledge repository

correct_answer_text: Closing, cancelling, or suspending projects that are completed or designated for termination

question: Per the common approaches and tips in Section 2.3.3.3, when should project initiation occur as a formal milestone?

options.A: As soon as a candidate opportunity is identified, to reserve a place in the portfolio
options.B: Only after a contract has been awarded to an external supplier
options.C: Only after all resources are in place as identified in the project plan
options.D: Whenever organizational fiscal-year budgeting cycles dictate

correct_answer_text: Only after all resources are in place as identified in the project plan

question: How does Section 2.3.3.3 recommend that investment opportunities be prioritized?

options.A: By the seniority of the project sponsor proposing the opportunity
options.B: Based on measurable criteria such that projects can be objectively evaluated against a threshold of acceptable performance
options.C: Strictly by the lowest projected cost, regardless of strategic fit
options.D: On a first-come, first-served basis to encourage rapid response

correct_answer_text: Based on measurable criteria such that projects can be objectively evaluated against a threshold of acceptable performance

question: Section 2.3.3.3 directs that ongoing project evaluations should include risk assessments. What action is recommended for projects whose disadvantages or risks to the organization outweigh the investment?

options.A: Transfer them to a different program within the same portfolio
options.B: Increase their funding to mitigate the identified risks
options.C: Cancel or suspend them
options.D: Continue them unchanged while monitoring the risks

correct_answer_text: Cancel or suspend them

question: When should the Portfolio Management process recommend use of a product line engineering approach (Section 4.2.4)?

options.A: Whenever a single bespoke system is being built for a unique customer
options.B: When stakeholders need the same or similar systems (e.g., common features) with some customizations (e.g., variants)
options.C: Only when terminating projects to consolidate their assets
options.D: Exclusively for research-and-development pilot studies

correct_answer_text: When stakeholders need the same or similar systems (e.g., common features) with some customizations (e.g., variants)

question: According to Section 2.3.3.3, business cases for potential projects are evaluated for cost-benefit and business need before approval. How are business cases treated as a project matures?

options.A: They are finalized at project initiation and never revisited
options.B: They are reviewed at each decision gate, resulting in reverification or restatement of the business case
options.C: They are merged with the systems engineering management plan (SEMP) and discarded
options.D: They are transferred to the Quality Management process for ongoing maintenance

correct_answer_text: They are reviewed at each decision gate, resulting in reverification or restatement of the business case

question: Figure 2.18 illustrates requirements across the portfolio, program, and project domains. Per Section 2.3.3.3, at the portfolio level, where do requirements typically start?

options.A: As detailed verification specifications tied to test cases
options.B: As element-level interface control documents between subsystems
options.C: As a high-level view associated with investment or business opportunities at the concept or portfolio level
options.D: As supplier statements of work issued through the Acquisition process

correct_answer_text: As a high-level view associated with investment or business opportunities at the concept or portfolio level

question: Per Section 2.3.3.3's common approaches and tips, what is recommended for managing the synergies between active projects in the organization portfolio?

options.A: Consolidate all active projects into a single project to eliminate interface management
options.B: Establish organizational coordination mechanisms to manage the synergies between active projects in the portfolio
options.C: Delegate inter-project coordination entirely to each individual project manager without portfolio-level oversight
options.D: Defer coordination until issues arise across project interfaces

correct_answer_text: Establish organizational coordination mechanisms to manage the synergies between active projects in the portfolio

question: Per the PMI (2017) distinction cited in Section 2.3.3.3, what is the focus of portfolio management compared to program or project management?

options.A: Portfolio management focuses on 'doing work right,' while program/project management focuses on 'doing the right work'
options.B: Portfolio management focuses on 'doing the right work,' while program/project management is more concerned with 'doing work right'
options.C: Both portfolio and program management focus on schedule adherence above all else
options.D: Portfolio management is concerned exclusively with supplier negotiations, while project management addresses internal scope

correct_answer_text: Portfolio management focuses on 'doing the right work,' while program/project management is more concerned with 'doing work right'

question: Per Section 2.3.3.3, how are projects typically organized within an organization's portfolio when multiple projects share a set of strategic objectives?

options.A: Projects with defined scope are organized into programs focused on a set of objectives that are part of the organization's strategic plan
options.B: Each project is administered as an entirely independent portfolio of one
options.C: Projects are merged into a single super-project to consolidate management overhead
options.D: Projects are grouped solely by physical co-location of their engineering teams

correct_answer_text: Projects with defined scope are organized into programs focused on a set of objectives that are part of the organization's strategic plan

question: Within the 'Define and authorize projects' activity, what is the first sub-task identified in Section 2.3.3.3 for identifying candidate projects?

options.A: Issue requests for proposals to qualified external suppliers
options.B: Obtain business area plans and organization strategic plans, and use the strategic objectives to identify candidate projects to fulfill them
options.C: Allocate detailed funding to selected projects before any candidate identification
options.D: Authorize project execution prior to evaluating investment opportunities

correct_answer_text: Obtain business area plans and organization strategic plans, and use the strategic objectives to identify candidate projects to fulfill them

question: An organization has seven candidate projects competing for funding but can only sponsor three this year. Which Portfolio Management activity governs deciding which projects to fund?

options.A: Terminate projects
options.B: Evaluate the portfolio of projects
options.C: Define and authorize projects (specifically identify, assess, prioritize, and select investment opportunities consistent with the strategic plan)
options.D: Project governance reporting and reviews

correct_answer_text: Define and authorize projects (specifically identify, assess, prioritize, and select investment opportunities consistent with the strategic plan)

question: A portfolio manager observes that an ongoing project's risks have grown to outweigh the potential investment return, while another project is simply completed. Which Portfolio Management activity addresses BOTH situations?

options.A: Define and authorize projects
options.B: Evaluate the portfolio of projects (only)
options.C: Terminate projects: close, cancel, or suspend projects that are completed or designated for termination
options.D: Establish project governance

correct_answer_text: Terminate projects: close, cancel, or suspend projects that are completed or designated for termination

question: Per Section 2.3.3.3, what makes prioritization criteria for investment opportunities effective for objective evaluation?

options.A: They are kept confidential to avoid politicizing the decision
options.B: They are measurable, so that projects can be objectively evaluated against a threshold of acceptable performance
options.C: They are weighted equally so no single criterion dominates
options.D: They are revisited only at the end of each fiscal year

correct_answer_text: They are measurable, so that projects can be objectively evaluated against a threshold of acceptable performance

question: Per the Acquisition (2.3.2.1) and Supply (2.3.2.2) processes, how is the agreement formally closed at the end of a project?

options.A: The Acquisition or Supply process itself closes the agreement directly through its 'retire' sub-task with no other process involvement
options.B: The Quality Management process closes the agreement after a final audit
options.C: The project is closed through the Portfolio Management process, which manages the full set of projects (and systems) of the organization; when the project is closed, action is taken to close the agreement
options.D: Agreements are closed by the Human Resource Management process after reassigning personnel

correct_answer_text: The project is closed through the Portfolio Management process, which manages the full set of projects (and systems) of the organization; when the project is closed, action is taken to close the agreement

question: Per Figure 2.18, the relationship between portfolio, program, project, and product/service/result depicts which top-down flow?

options.A: Product, service, or result drives Project, which drives Program, which drives Portfolio strategic plan
options.B: Portfolio strategic plan -> Portfolio Requirements -> Program -> Project -> Product, Service, or Result (each meets the upstream requirements)
options.C: Portfolio and project requirements are derived independently with no hierarchical relationship
options.D: Portfolio Requirements bypass programs and feed directly into product element designs

correct_answer_text: Portfolio strategic plan -> Portfolio Requirements -> Program -> Project -> Product, Service, or Result (each meets the upstream requirements)

question: Per Section 2.3.3.3, before a project can be authorized for execution, what condition must be in place per the common approaches and tips?

options.A: Initial concept sketches are sufficient; formal resource commitment occurs later
options.B: Initiation should be a formal milestone that does not occur until all resources are in place as identified in the project plan
options.C: The project must already have completed verification of the SoI
options.D: Only the project sponsor's signature is required to authorize execution

correct_answer_text: Initiation should be a formal milestone that does not occur until all resources are in place as identified in the project plan

question: Per Figure 2.17, which of the following are listed as Controls (rather than Inputs, Outputs, or Enablers) for the Portfolio Management process?

options.A: Project authorization request and Variance/deviation/waiver request
options.B: Project authorization and Project direction
options.C: Portfolio management strategy/approach and Organization infrastructure needs
options.D: Organization lessons learned and Portfolio management records/artifacts

correct_answer_text: Portfolio management strategy/approach and Organization infrastructure needs

question: Per Section 2.3.3.3 Elaboration, how are investment opportunities evaluated when an organization cannot pursue them all?

options.A: Each opportunity is evaluated in isolation without reference to the existing portfolio
options.B: Opportunities are evaluated against the portfolio of existing agreements and ongoing projects, taking into consideration the attainability of the stakeholders' requirements
options.C: Opportunities are deferred until the next strategic-planning cycle regardless of urgency
options.D: Opportunities are accepted in the order they are received, regardless of strategic fit

correct_answer_text: Opportunities are evaluated against the portfolio of existing agreements and ongoing projects, taking into consideration the attainability of the stakeholders' requirements

question: Per Section 2.3.3.3, what is the stated goal of using a product line engineering approach (Section 4.2.4) at the portfolio level?

options.A: To eliminate variability between customers by enforcing a single fixed configuration
options.B: To manage a product line as one product definition with planned variants, as opposed to multiple separate products managed individually, thereby streamlining and simplifying the management effort
options.C: To force every project in the portfolio to use the same supplier
options.D: To replace systems engineering management plans with product roadmaps

correct_answer_text: To manage a product line as one product definition with planned variants, as opposed to multiple separate products managed individually, thereby streamlining and simplifying the management effort

question: Per Section 2.3.3.3, how can large-project business cases be validated to prove objectives can be met prior to committing large amounts of resources to full-scale engineering and manufacturing development?

options.A: By relying solely on supplier marketing materials and analogies to past projects
options.B: By using sophisticated engineering models, or even prototypes of key system elements, to demonstrate the business case can be met and the system will work as envisioned
options.C: By skipping validation entirely and proceeding directly to detailed design
options.D: By transferring business-case responsibility to the Human Resource Management process

correct_answer_text: By using sophisticated engineering models, or even prototypes of key system elements, to demonstrate the business case can be met and the system will work as envisioned

question: Per Section 2.3.3.3, how does the SCOPE of Portfolio Management compare with that of an individual project?

options.A: Both have identical scope limited to the system of interest
options.B: Portfolio scope is narrowly internal to one project; project scope is enterprise-wide
options.C: At the portfolio level the scope is extensive with consideration external to the organization and internal across the organization's enterprise; at the project end the focus is internal to the project with consideration for the context of the product/service/result
options.D: Portfolio scope considers only financials, while project scope considers only technical work

correct_answer_text: At the portfolio level the scope is extensive with consideration external to the organization and internal across the organization's enterprise; at the project end the focus is internal to the project with consideration for the context of the product/service/result

question: Categorize each item in the Portfolio Management Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.17).
According to ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 as cited in the handbook, what is the purpose of the Human Resource Management process?

options.A: To allocate financial resources, tools, and information technology consistent with project budgets
options.B: To provide the organization with necessary human resources and to maintain their competencies, consistent with strategic needs
options.C: To staff a specific project with qualified personnel for the duration of the project life cycle
options.D: To negotiate labor contracts and resolve interpersonal conflicts within project teams

correct_answer_text: To provide the organization with necessary human resources and to maintain their competencies, consistent with strategic needs

question: How does the handbook distinguish the scope of Human Resource Management from Project Planning's staffing activities?

options.A: Human Resource Management is project-level day-to-day staffing while Project Planning is enterprise-level competency planning
options.B: Human Resource Management is the same process as Project Planning, just executed by HR personnel
options.C: Human Resource Management operates at the organization level across the portfolio of projects, while Project Planning anticipates the resources needed for a particular project
options.D: Human Resource Management only handles training, while all hiring is performed inside Project Planning

correct_answer_text: Human Resource Management operates at the organization level across the portfolio of projects, while Project Planning anticipates the resources needed for a particular project

question: According to the IPO diagram (Figure 2.19), which of the following is a typical INPUT to the Human Resource Management process?

options.A: Qualified personnel
options.B: Human resource management strategy/approach
options.C: Human resource management records/artifacts
options.D: Project human resource needs

correct_answer_text: Project human resource needs

question: Per Figure 2.19, which item is a typical OUTPUT of the Human Resource Management process rather than an input?

options.A: Organization strategic plan
options.B: Project portfolio
options.C: Qualified personnel
options.D: Project human resource needs

correct_answer_text: Qualified personnel

question: The IPO diagram (Figure 2.19) groups the Human Resource Management activities into which three categories?

options.A: Plan resources, execute resources, control resources
options.B: Identify skills; develop skills; acquire and provide skills
options.C: Hire, train, retain
options.D: Inventory skills, assign personnel, evaluate performance

correct_answer_text: Identify skills; develop skills; acquire and provide skills

question: Within the "Identify skills" activity, what is the purpose of recording the skills of existing personnel?

options.A: To establish a skills inventory used to evaluate needs against available personnel
options.B: To create a public roster of employees for marketing purposes
options.C: To rank employees against one another for promotion decisions
options.D: To replace the need for the organization's strategic plan

correct_answer_text: To establish a skills inventory used to evaluate needs against available personnel

question: Which two INCOSE resources does the handbook explicitly recommend for identifying systems engineering skills as part of the "Identify skills" activity?

options.A: The INCOSE SE Handbook and the INCOSE Vision document
options.B: The Systems Engineering Body of Knowledge (SEBoK) and PMBOK Guide
options.C: The Systems Engineering Competency Framework (SECF) and the Systems Engineering Competency Assessment Guide (SECAG)
options.D: The Professional Development Portal and the Career Development Plan template

correct_answer_text: The Systems Engineering Competency Framework (SECF) and the Systems Engineering Competency Assessment Guide (SECAG)

question: Within the "Develop skills" activity, what is the documented role of training, education, and mentoring?

options.A: To replace hiring and acquisition activities entirely
options.B: To close identified gaps of project personnel against required skills
options.C: To certify personnel for ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 compliance
options.D: To establish project budgets for human resource allocation

correct_answer_text: To close identified gaps of project personnel against required skills

question: Which activity is listed under "Acquire and provide skills" in the Human Resource Management process?

options.A: Establish a strategy/approach for skills development
options.B: Review current and anticipated projects to determine skill needs across the portfolio
options.C: Create succession plans to ensure flow of skill development is sustained into the future
options.D: Train or hire qualified personnel when gaps indicate skill needs cannot be met with existing personnel

correct_answer_text: Train or hire qualified personnel when gaps indicate skill needs cannot be met with existing personnel

question: What does the handbook identify as the purpose of creating succession plans within "Develop skills"?

options.A: To document who replaces a project manager at the end of each project phase
options.B: To ensure that the desired skill set and flow of skill development through the organization is sustained into the future
options.C: To assign personnel to specific job roles for the next fiscal year
options.D: To negotiate salary progression with senior employees

correct_answer_text: To ensure that the desired skill set and flow of skill development through the organization is sustained into the future

question: Under "Develop and Manage Competencies," which activity does the handbook list?

options.A: Assign personnel to projects based on personnel development and project needs
options.B: Maintain organization-level competency definitions and frameworks
options.C: Provide training to project personnel to close identified skill gaps
options.D: Maintain a skilled personnel pool to staff ongoing projects

correct_answer_text: Maintain organization-level competency definitions and frameworks

question: Why does the handbook explicitly exclude tools, databases, communication systems, and IT from the Human Resource Management process?

options.A: Because those nonhuman resources are addressed under the Infrastructure Management process
options.B: Because those resources are addressed under the Portfolio Management process
options.C: Because those resources are addressed under the Quality Management process
options.D: Because those resources are out of scope of the system life cycle entirely

correct_answer_text: Because those nonhuman resources are addressed under the Infrastructure Management process

question: According to the "Common approaches and tips," what does the handbook warn against doing with project personnel, especially those with specialized skills?

options.A: Assigning them to apprentice and mentoring programs
options.B: Allowing them to take vacations during the project
options.C: Overcommitting them across projects
options.D: Including them in succession plans

correct_answer_text: Overcommitting them across projects

question: Per the Elaboration on Human Resource Management Concepts, what is identified as the primary objective of the process?

options.A: To minimize the cost of labor across all projects in the portfolio
options.B: To provide a pool of qualified personnel to the organization
options.C: To assign every employee a career development plan
options.D: To eliminate competition for scarce talent among project managers

correct_answer_text: To provide a pool of qualified personnel to the organization

question: A systems engineering organization has three concurrently running projects, each requiring two certified senior systems engineers, but only four such engineers are currently on staff. Per the Human Resource Management process activities in Section 2.3.3.4, which activity step most directly addresses this gap?

options.A: Under "Identify skills," record the skills of existing personnel into a skills inventory and stop there
options.B: Under "Acquire and provide skills," train or hire qualified personnel when gaps indicate skill needs cannot be met with existing personnel
options.C: Under "Develop and Manage Competencies," create job role definitions related to competencies required
options.D: Under Project Planning, increase the project budget so each project can hire two senior engineers independently

correct_answer_text: Under "Acquire and provide skills," train or hire qualified personnel when gaps indicate skill needs cannot be met with existing personnel

question: Per the handbook's distinction between Human Resource Management and Project Planning, which statement best characterizes their relationship in the staffing flow?

options.A: Project planners determine project resource needs by anticipating current and future needs; the Human Resource Management process provides the mechanisms whereby organization management is made aware of those needs and schedules personnel to be in place when requested
options.B: Project Planning provides qualified personnel directly; Human Resource Management only tracks records of assignments after the fact
options.C: Human Resource Management determines the project resource needs; Project Planning sources and hires the personnel
options.D: Both processes independently hire and assign personnel without coordination

correct_answer_text: Project planners determine project resource needs by anticipating current and future needs; the Human Resource Management process provides the mechanisms whereby organization management is made aware of those needs and schedules personnel to be in place when requested

question: According to the IPO diagram (Figure 2.19), which of the following is the complete set of typical INPUTS to the Human Resource Management process?

options.A: Organization strategic plan; project portfolio; project human resource needs
options.B: Skills inventory; career development plans; succession plans
options.C: Qualified personnel; human resource management report; human resource management records/artifacts
options.D: Human resource management strategy/approach; identify skills; develop skills

correct_answer_text: Organization strategic plan; project portfolio; project human resource needs

question: Per Figure 2.19, which of the following sets correctly lists the typical OUTPUTS of the Human Resource Management process?

options.A: Organization strategic plan; project portfolio; project human resource needs
options.B: Human resource management strategy/approach; qualified personnel; human resource management report; human resource management records/artifacts
options.C: Identify skills; develop skills; acquire and provide skills
options.D: Skills inventory; succession plans; career development plans

correct_answer_text: Human resource management strategy/approach; qualified personnel; human resource management report; human resource management records/artifacts

question: Within the "Identify skills" activity, after recording the skills of existing personnel and reviewing current and anticipated projects, what is the next step the handbook prescribes?

options.A: Immediately initiate a hiring campaign to expand the pool of qualified personnel
options.B: Evaluate skill needs against available personnel with the prerequisite skills to determine if training, hiring, or other skill acquisition activities are indicated
options.C: Create succession plans to ensure the desired skill set is sustained into the future
options.D: Assign personnel to projects based on personnel development and project needs

correct_answer_text: Evaluate skill needs against available personnel with the prerequisite skills to determine if training, hiring, or other skill acquisition activities are indicated

question: Per the handbook's "Common approaches and tips," which INCOSE resources are described as comprehensive resources of skills that can be used to develop career development plans?

options.A: SEBoK and the INCOSE Vision document
options.B: INCOSE SECF and SECAG
options.C: PMBOK Guide and ISO 9001
options.D: The INCOSE Professional Development Portal and the INCOSE Handbook

correct_answer_text: INCOSE SECF and SECAG

question: The handbook recommends which INCOSE resource specifically as a tool to help identify training and development resources to match desired skills, abilities, and behaviors?

options.A: INCOSE Systems Engineering Vision
options.B: INCOSE Competency Working Group charter
options.C: INCOSE Professional Development Portal
options.D: INCOSE Insight magazine

correct_answer_text: INCOSE Professional Development Portal

question: Within the "Develop and Manage Competencies" activity, which of the following is NOT one of the bullets the handbook lists?

options.A: Create and maintain job role definitions related to competencies required
