options.B: Identify organization competency gaps
options.C: Align organization competencies with strategic objectives
options.D: Train or hire qualified personnel when gaps cannot be met with existing personnel

correct_answer_text: Train or hire qualified personnel when gaps cannot be met with existing personnel

question: Per the "Elaboration on Human Resource Management Concepts," what kind of balance does the handbook say human resource management should find?

options.A: A balance between training and hiring
options.B: A balance between efficiency and robustness
options.C: A balance between project schedule and project budget
options.D: A balance between competency frameworks and succession plans

correct_answer_text: A balance between efficiency and robustness

question: Per the Elaboration, what does the handbook say human resource management relies heavily on?

options.A: Annual performance review cycles
options.B: Forecasts into the future of the demand and supply of various resources
options.C: Single-source talent acquisition through outsourcing
options.D: Centralized hiring authority within the Quality Management process

correct_answer_text: Forecasts into the future of the demand and supply of various resources

question: Per the "Common approaches and tips," what does the handbook recommend doing with the slack time at the beginning of a project?

options.A: Reassign personnel to other ongoing projects to maximize utilization
options.B: Provide training to ensure necessary skills
options.C: Conduct hiring interviews for the next project
options.D: Update the organization's succession plans

correct_answer_text: Provide training to ensure necessary skills

question: Per the "Common approaches and tips," how does the handbook describe the kinds of items that should appear in the organization's pipeline for obtaining necessary expertise?

options.A: A pipeline of candidates, training provisions, consultants, temporary outsourcing, and reassignments
options.B: Only permanent full-time employees hired through formal hiring processes
options.C: Only internal personnel cross-trained between projects
options.D: Only external consultants on fixed-term contracts

correct_answer_text: A pipeline of candidates, training provisions, consultants, temporary outsourcing, and reassignments

question: Per the Elaboration, what challenge does the handbook say project managers face with respect to the larger organization pool?

options.A: They must directly hire from outside the organization to bypass the pool
options.B: They compete for scarce talent and must balance access to needed experts with stability in the project team and its tacit knowledge and project memory
options.C: They must contract all specialized work to external vendors due to limited internal supply
options.D: They are prohibited from sharing personnel between projects

correct_answer_text: They compete for scarce talent and must balance access to needed experts with stability in the project team and its tacit knowledge and project memory

question: Per the Elaboration on Human Resource Management Concepts, why does the handbook say today's projects depend on optimally multidisciplinary teams?

options.A: Because multidisciplinary teams are cheaper to staff than single-discipline teams
options.B: Because multidisciplinary teams reduce the need for succession planning
options.C: Because such teams can resolve project issues quickly through direct communication, shortening the decision-making cycle and producing improved decisions through early multidisciplinary perspectives
options.D: Because multidisciplinary teams eliminate the need for human resource forecasts

correct_answer_text: Because such teams can resolve project issues quickly through direct communication, shortening the decision-making cycle and producing improved decisions through early multidisciplinary perspectives

question: Categorize each item in the Human Resource Management Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.19).
According to ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 as cited in the SE Handbook, what is the purpose of the Quality Management process?

options.A: To verify that delivered system elements conform to specified design requirements through inspection and test
options.B: To assure that products, services, and implementations of the Quality Management process meet organizational and project quality objectives and achieve customer satisfaction
options.C: To identify, capture, and disseminate knowledge across the organization so that quality lessons learned can be re-applied
options.D: To plan, monitor, and control project-level quality activities so that work products satisfy contractual acceptance criteria

correct_answer_text: To assure that products, services, and implementations of the Quality Management process meet organizational and project quality objectives and achieve customer satisfaction

question: Per the Description in Section 2.3.3.5, what is identified as the 'overarching process' for achieving quality goals in an organization?

options.A: The Quality Assurance (QA) process and its supporting inspections and audits
options.B: The Quality Control (QC) process and its associated Statistical Process Control techniques
options.C: The Quality Management (QM) process and its supporting methods, values, and subprocesses
options.D: The Measurement process and its associated cost-of-quality calculations

correct_answer_text: The Quality Management (QM) process and its supporting methods, values, and subprocesses

question: The SE Handbook states that the QM process makes visible the goals of the organization to achieve customer satisfaction when it is properly communicated through which mechanisms?

options.A: Verification and validation records
options.B: Policy and procedure
options.C: Configuration baselines and change requests
options.D: Risk registers and decision logs

correct_answer_text: Policy and procedure

question: According to the IPO diagram in Figure 2.20, which of the following is a typical INPUT to the Quality Management process?

options.A: Quality management strategy/approach
options.B: Quality management system
options.C: Quality assurance report
options.D: Quality management corrective action

correct_answer_text: Quality assurance report

question: Which of the following is listed in Figure 2.20 as a typical OUTPUT of the Quality Management process (and not as an input)?

options.A: Customer satisfaction inputs
options.B: Quality assurance strategy/approach
options.C: Organization strategic plan
options.D: Quality management system

correct_answer_text: Quality management system

question: Which of the following is one of the three top-level activities of the Quality Management process as listed in Section 2.3.3.5?

options.A: Perform quality management corrective and preventive action
options.B: Conduct statistical process control sampling on production lots
options.C: Execute project-level inspections and acceptance tests
options.D: Identify and capture tacit knowledge from quality experts

correct_answer_text: Perform quality management corrective and preventive action

question: Under the 'Plan quality management' activity, which task is explicitly identified in Section 2.3.3.5?

options.A: Recommend appropriate corrective action when indicated
options.B: Evaluate project assessments
options.C: Assess customer satisfaction against compliance with requirements
options.D: Establish QM guidelines—policies, standards, and procedures

correct_answer_text: Establish QM guidelines—policies, standards, and procedures

question: Under the 'Assess quality management' activity, the SE Handbook lists which of the following tasks?

options.A: Establish organization and project QM responsibilities and authorities
options.B: Assess customer satisfaction against compliance with requirements and objectives
options.C: Maintain open communications within the organization and with stakeholders
options.D: Identify, assess, and prioritize quality guidelines consistent with the organization strategic plan

correct_answer_text: Assess customer satisfaction against compliance with requirements and objectives

question: The 'Common Approaches and Tips' for QM state that management's commitment to quality is reflected primarily by what?

options.A: Sign-off on every quality assurance report by the executive team
options.B: Mandatory weekly quality status meetings with all project teams
options.C: Integration of QM principles in the strategic planning and budgeting of the organization, and allocation of educational resources
options.D: Public publication of defect metrics and customer complaint data

correct_answer_text: Integration of QM principles in the strategic planning and budgeting of the organization, and allocation of educational resources

question: Philip Crosby's 'Four Absolutes of Quality' referenced in Section 2.3.3.5 are the definition of quality, the system of quality, the standard for quality, and what?

options.A: The price of conformance
options.B: The Zero Defects Attitude
options.C: The method for measuring quality
options.D: Constancy of purpose

correct_answer_text: The method for measuring quality

question: Per Crosby's Four Absolutes as described in the SE Handbook, the 'system' of quality is best characterized as what?

options.A: Prevention
options.B: Inspection
options.C: Conformance to specifications
options.D: Continuous improvement

correct_answer_text: Prevention

question: The SE Handbook contrasts Quality Assurance (QA) and Quality Control (QC) as the two QM prevention methods. Which statement most accurately captures this distinction?

options.A: QA removes defective outputs after they occur; QC builds quality in by failure testing and inspecting incoming materials
options.B: QA 'puts good things into our processes' so they conform to requirements; QC 'takes bad things out of our processes after they occur' to prevent defects from reaching stakeholders
options.C: QA is the responsibility of the executive team only; QC is the responsibility of production-line workers only
options.D: QA is a statistical sampling method; QC is a documentation method

correct_answer_text: QA 'puts good things into our processes' so they conform to requirements; QC 'takes bad things out of our processes after they occur' to prevent defects from reaching stakeholders

question: How does the SE Handbook distinguish Quality Management (QM) from Quality Assurance (QA)?

options.A: QM and QA are synonymous and used interchangeably throughout the handbook
options.B: QM is the inspection of finished products, while QA plans those inspections
options.C: QM is the overarching organizational process establishing policies, standards, goals, and culture; QA is one of the QM prevention methods that 'puts good things into our processes' to conform to requirements
options.D: QM is a project-level process executed by engineers; QA is an external auditor function performed by customers

correct_answer_text: QM is the overarching organizational process establishing policies, standards, goals, and culture; QA is one of the QM prevention methods that 'puts good things into our processes' to conform to requirements

question: The SE Handbook defines a Zero Defects Attitude (ZDA) as which of the following?

options.A: A statistical certification that a process produces zero defects over a defined sampling interval
options.B: A 'heart attitude' and commitment to make each stakeholder's experience as close to what was promised as possible, not an expectation of perfection
options.C: A contractual warranty that the supplier will replace any defective unit at no cost to the customer
options.D: An audit standard requiring proof of zero non-conformances before product release

correct_answer_text: A 'heart attitude' and commitment to make each stakeholder's experience as close to what was promised as possible, not an expectation of perfection

question: According to Crosby as cited in Section 2.3.3.5, what is 'the method for measuring quality'?

options.A: The defect density per thousand lines of code or units produced
options.B: The customer satisfaction score from post-delivery surveys
options.C: The price of non-conformance (also called the 'cost of poor quality')
options.D: The first-pass yield of a statistically controlled production process

correct_answer_text: The price of non-conformance (also called the 'cost of poor quality')

question: Per Crosby's principle described in the SE Handbook, when does 'Quality is Free'?

options.A: When defect rates fall below the statistical process control upper limit
options.B: When the cost of doing things right is equal to or less than the price of non-conformance
options.C: When customer satisfaction surveys exceed a 95% threshold
options.D: When the organization eliminates all rework and warranty expenses

correct_answer_text: When the cost of doing things right is equal to or less than the price of non-conformance

question: Which technique does the SE Handbook identify as a Quality Control monitoring approach that uses real-time, random sampling to test a fraction of output for variances within critical tolerances?

options.A: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA)
options.B: Statistical Process Control (SPC)
options.C: Design of Experiments (DOE)
options.D: Reliability Engineering

correct_answer_text: Statistical Process Control (SPC)

question: In Table 2.3 'Eight Attributes of a Quality Management Culture,' which attribute is described as 'a measure of our mastery of planning and budgeting disciplines and how effectively we apply them to create viable work processes'?

options.A: Vocational Certainty
options.B: Administrative Consistency
options.C: Process Quality
options.D: Executive Credibility

correct_answer_text: Process Quality

question: Per Table 2.3, the attribute 'Administrative Consistency' in a QM Culture is described as which of the following?

options.A: A measure of our faithfulness to our career agenda and disciplined skill development
options.B: A measure of our attention to details—carefully listening to customers to identify and conform to their requirements and assure customer satisfaction
options.C: A measure of our sincerity and skill with people, gaining reliable influence
options.D: A measure of mutual respect, accountability, and professionalism in a work culture

correct_answer_text: A measure of our attention to details—carefully listening to customers to identify and conform to their requirements and assure customer satisfaction

question: In Table 2.3, which Eight-Attributes element is described as 'a measure of our trustworthiness in practical matters—what we turn to when we want things to work right, run on time, and be there when needed'?

options.A: Personal Authenticity
options.B: Ethical Dependability
options.C: Executive Credibility
options.D: Zero Defects Attitude

correct_answer_text: Ethical Dependability

question: Per Table 2.3, the 'Create a Keeping the Promise Culture' attribute is best described as which of the following?

options.A: A measure of an organization's commitment to formal contract close-out and warranty fulfillment
options.B: A measure of mutual respect, accountability, and professionalism in a work culture—the practiced values of effective QM cultures
options.C: A measure of executive sincerity expressed through people-management skill
options.D: A measure of consistency with customers and coworkers that makes exceptional service feel normal

correct_answer_text: A measure of mutual respect, accountability, and professionalism in a work culture—the practiced values of effective QM cultures

question: Who is credited in Section 2.3.3.5 with defining the 'Eight Attributes of a Quality Management Culture' presented in Table 2.3?

options.A: W. Edwards Deming
options.B: Philip Crosby
options.C: Joseph Juran
options.D: Kennedy (2005)

correct_answer_text: Kennedy (2005)

question: Figure 2.21 'QM Values and Skills Integration' depicts a QM culture as the result of integrating which two principal elements?

options.A: Quality Assurance reports and Quality Control inspections
options.B: Skills that are supported by values, and values that are expressed through skills—producing a 'Keeping the Promise' work culture
options.C: Statistical Process Control and Zero Defects metrics
options.D: Organizational policies and supplier agreements

correct_answer_text: Skills that are supported by values, and values that are expressed through skills—producing a 'Keeping the Promise' work culture

question: Per the Elaboration in Section 2.3.3.5, Deming's '14 Points' emphasize creating 'constancy of purpose for improving products and services,' supported by what?

options.A: Aggressive defect-quota enforcement and supplier penalties
options.B: A vigorous program of education and self-improvement for everyone
options.C: Mandatory annual third-party quality audits
options.D: Bonus incentives tied to zero-defect production runs

correct_answer_text: A vigorous program of education and self-improvement for everyone

question: Per Crosby's First Absolute as presented in Section 2.3.3.5, the 'definition of quality' is best stated as which of the following?

options.A: Conformance to internal engineering specifications and tolerances
options.B: Meeting the stakeholder's requirements, needs, and expectations
options.C: Fitness for use as judged by an independent quality auditor
options.D: The absence of measurable defects in the delivered product

correct_answer_text: Meeting the stakeholder's requirements, needs, and expectations

question: Per Crosby's Four Absolutes as cited in Section 2.3.3.5, the 'standard for quality' is which of the following?

options.A: Six Sigma (3.4 defects per million opportunities)
options.B: ISO 9001 certification
options.C: Zero Defects (ZD)
options.D: Statistical Process Control within 3-sigma limits

correct_answer_text: Zero Defects (ZD)

question: Section 2.3.3.5 explicitly distinguishes the 'tracking of defects' (Zero Defects, ZD) from a more fundamental human term. Which statement best captures that distinction?

options.A: ZD is an individual mindset, while ZDA is a numerical defect-tracking measure
options.B: ZD is the numerical count tracked via feedback loops; the Zero Defects Attitude (ZDA) is a 'heart attitude' committed to keeping promises, not an expectation of perfection
options.C: ZD applies only to manufacturing; ZDA applies only to services
options.D: ZD and ZDA are interchangeable terms used by Crosby and Deming respectively

correct_answer_text: ZD is the numerical count tracked via feedback loops; the Zero Defects Attitude (ZDA) is a 'heart attitude' committed to keeping promises, not an expectation of perfection

question: According to Section 2.3.3.5, where did Quality Assurance (QA) originate and what was it originally referred to as?

options.A: Automotive industry, originally called 'Total Quality Management'
options.B: Aerospace industry, originally called 'reliability engineering'
options.C: Pharmaceutical industry, originally called 'Good Manufacturing Practice'
options.D: Software industry, originally called 'verification and validation'

correct_answer_text: Aerospace industry, originally called 'reliability engineering'

question: Per Section 2.3.3.5, Quality Control (QC) consists of which set of activities?

options.A: Failure testing and pre-inspecting batches of materials before use
options.B: Infusing processes with reliable human resources, policies, procedures, and training
options.C: Checking, monitoring, and inspecting for defects, plus removal, replacement, or rework of defective outcomes
options.D: Establishing strategic plans, budgets, and educational resources for the organization

correct_answer_text: Checking, monitoring, and inspecting for defects, plus removal, replacement, or rework of defective outcomes

question: Per Table 2.3, the 'Vocational Certainty' attribute of a QM culture is best characterized as which of the following?

options.A: A measure of our faithfulness to our career agenda; being disciplined about developing skills and talents and acquiring earned confidence
options.B: A measure of our commitment to keep our promises and prevent defects from reaching customers
options.C: A measure of our mastery of planning and budgeting disciplines to create viable work processes
options.D: A measure of our sincerity and skill with people to gain reliable influence

correct_answer_text: A measure of our faithfulness to our career agenda; being disciplined about developing skills and talents and acquiring earned confidence

question: In Table 2.3, the 'Executive Credibility' attribute of a QM culture is described as which of the following?

options.A: A measure of trustworthiness in practical matters—what we turn to when we want things to work right
options.B: A measure of attention to details and conforming to customer requirements
options.C: A measure of resolve to be consistent with customers and coworkers so that exceptional service feels normal
options.D: A measure of sincerity and skill with people; sincerity comes from the heart, but skills can be sharpened to gain reliable influence

correct_answer_text: A measure of sincerity and skill with people; sincerity comes from the heart, but skills can be sharpened to gain reliable influence

question: Per Table 2.3, the 'Personal Authenticity' attribute of a QM culture is best described as which of the following?

options.A: A measure of resolve to be consistent with our customers and coworkers; authentic QM cultures work diligently to make exceptional service feel normal
options.B: A measure of our commitment to initiate systems that prevent defects from reaching our customers
options.C: A measure of mastery of planning and budgeting disciplines applied to work processes
options.D: A measure of mutual respect, accountability, and professionalism in a work culture

correct_answer_text: A measure of resolve to be consistent with our customers and coworkers; authentic QM cultures work diligently to make exceptional service feel normal

question: Section 2.3.3.5 explains the 'cost of quality' calculation. Which statement most accurately captures its composition?

options.A: It is the sum of all defects multiplied by the average unit warranty cost
options.B: It is determined by comparing the 'price of non-conformance' (rework, defects, warranties) with the 'price of conformance' (improving processes and applying preventive methods), measured in both financial and human values
options.C: It is the variance between budgeted and actual quality department expenditures
options.D: It is a single financial metric equal to the price of non-conformance only

correct_answer_text: It is determined by comparing the 'price of non-conformance' (rework, defects, warranties) with the 'price of conformance' (improving processes and applying preventive methods), measured in both financial and human values

question: Under the 'Plan quality management' activity in Section 2.3.3.5, in addition to establishing QM guidelines, the handbook explicitly directs the organization to establish QM goals and objectives that include what?

options.A: Statistical Process Control thresholds and sampling plans
options.B: Supplier quality agreements and incoming-inspection criteria
options.C: QM Culture emphasis
options.D: Defect-tracking dashboards and customer-complaint metrics

correct_answer_text: QM Culture emphasis

question: Under the 'Perform quality management corrective action and preventive action' activity in Section 2.3.3.5, which task is explicitly listed (in addition to recommending appropriate action when indicated)?

options.A: Continuously improve the QM guidelines
options.B: Maintain open communications within the organization and with stakeholders
options.C: Identify, assess, and prioritize quality guidelines consistent with the organization strategic plan
options.D: Assess customer satisfaction against compliance with requirements and objectives

correct_answer_text: Maintain open communications within the organization and with stakeholders

question: An organization wants to establish a sustainable QM culture. Per the Description and Common Approaches in Section 2.3.3.5, which combination of conditions does the handbook identify as essential to that outcome?

options.A: Frequent third-party ISO audits and aggressive defect-quota enforcement
options.B: A work culture disciplined in QM foundational principles, integration of QM principles into the organization's strategic planning and budgeting, and allocation of educational resources
options.C: Public dashboards of defect metrics and weekly executive sign-off on QA reports
options.D: Replacement of QC inspections with statistically certified zero-defect production runs

correct_answer_text: A work culture disciplined in QM foundational principles, integration of QM principles into the organization's strategic planning and budgeting, and allocation of educational resources

question: Per Figure 2.20, which item is listed as a typical OUTPUT of the Quality Management process (distinct from the QA evaluation report which is an input)?

options.A: Quality assurance strategy/approach
options.B: Customer satisfaction inputs
options.C: Quality management evaluation report
options.D: Organization strategic plan

correct_answer_text: Quality management evaluation report

question: Per the Elaboration on QM Culture in Section 2.3.3.5, what enables SE practitioners to 'evaluate prevention options and make continuous, incremental improvements'?

options.A: Certification as an ASQ-Certified Quality Engineer or Six Sigma Black Belt
options.B: Sufficient process knowledge and a QM knowledge base
options.C: Authority to halt production whenever a non-conformance is observed
options.D: Direct reporting access to the Chief Quality Officer of the organization

correct_answer_text: Sufficient process knowledge and a QM knowledge base

question: Categorize each item in the Quality Management Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.20).

items.text: Perform quality management corrective and preventive action

question: From Table 2.3, drag each defining behavior or descriptor to the QM Culture attribute it characterizes.
categories: Create a Keeping the Promise Culture

items.text: A measure of our commitment to keep our promises and to initiate systems with the goal of preventing defects from reaching our customers.
A measure of our faithfulness to our career agenda; disciplined about developing skills and talents and acquiring earned confidence.
A measure of our mastery of planning and budgeting disciplines and how effectively we apply them to create viable work processes.
A measure of our attention to details; carefully listen to the customer to identify and conform to their requirements and assure customer satisfaction.
A measure of our sincerity and skill with people; sincerity comes naturally from the heart, but skills can be sharpened and improved to gain reliable influence.
A measure of our resolve to be consistent with our customers and coworkers; authentic QM cultures work diligently to make exceptional service feel normal.
A measure of our trustworthiness in practical matters; what we turn to when we want things to work right, run on time, and be there when needed.
A measure of the mutual respect, accountability, and professionalism in a work culture; the practiced values of effective QM cultures.
items.category: Create a Keeping the Promise Culture

question: Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 as cited in the SE Handbook, what is the purpose of the Knowledge Management process?

options.A: To manage information items, data, and records generated within a single project across its life cycle
options.B: To create the capability and assets that enable the organization to exploit opportunities to re-apply existing knowledge
options.C: To plan, schedule, and control the technical activities of a project through measurable indicators
options.D: To allocate human resources and infrastructure to specific projects based on competency needs

correct_answer_text: To create the capability and assets that enable the organization to exploit opportunities to re-apply existing knowledge

question: Which statement best distinguishes Knowledge Management (KM) from Information Management (IM) as presented in the SE Handbook?

options.A: KM is project-level and manages records within a single project; IM is organization-level and retains assets across projects
options.B: KM and IM are synonymous and produce the same outputs at the organizational level
options.C: KM is viewed from an organizational project-enabling perspective to support reuse across projects; IM manages project-level information items and records
options.D: KM applies only to tacit knowledge while IM applies only to explicit knowledge

correct_answer_text: KM is viewed from an organizational project-enabling perspective to support reuse across projects; IM manages project-level information items and records

question: How does the SE Handbook characterize the difference between explicit and tacit knowledge within an organization?

options.A: Explicit knowledge is captured in training, processes, practices, methods, policies, and procedures, while tacit knowledge is embodied in individuals or teams and requires specialized techniques to capture
options.B: Explicit knowledge is held by individuals through experience, while tacit knowledge resides in organizational documents
options.C: Explicit knowledge is unconscious and informal, while tacit knowledge is always documented in formal artifacts
options.D: Explicit and tacit knowledge are managed identically because both can be captured directly into any KM repository

correct_answer_text: Explicit knowledge is captured in training, processes, practices, methods, policies, and procedures, while tacit knowledge is embodied in individuals or teams and requires specialized techniques to capture

question: According to Figure 2.22, which of the following is a typical INPUT to the Knowledge Management process?

options.A: Knowledge management strategy/approach
options.B: Knowledge management system
options.C: Reused system or system element
options.D: Organization lessons learned

correct_answer_text: Organization lessons learned

question: Per the Figure 2.22 IPO diagram, which of the following is a typical OUTPUT of the Knowledge Management process?

options.A: Project lessons learned
options.B: Organization strategic plan
options.C: Knowledge management strategy/approach
options.D: Records/artifacts (as inputs to be captured)

correct_answer_text: Knowledge management strategy/approach

question: Which of the following is identified as one of the four high-level activities of the Knowledge Management process?

options.A: Acquire and dispose of project information items
options.B: Manage knowledge, skills, and knowledge assets
options.C: Negotiate intellectual property terms with suppliers
options.D: Track project schedule variance against the SEMP baseline

correct_answer_text: Manage knowledge, skills, and knowledge assets

question: When planning Knowledge Management, the handbook advises that if no identified project will benefit from a candidate knowledge asset, then it:

options.A: Should still be captured because all knowledge is inherently valuable
options.B: Should be archived permanently with no review criteria
options.C: Probably should not be considered for capture
options.D: Must be escalated to executive leadership for a capture decision

correct_answer_text: Probably should not be considered for capture

question: Under "Share knowledge assets throughout the organization," the SE Handbook recommends establishing which of the following to enable reapplication of knowledge?

options.A: A taxonomy for the reapplication of knowledge
options.B: A configuration management board for project baselines
options.C: A formal risk register for each knowledge item
options.D: An earned-value reporting cadence for KM assets

correct_answer_text: A taxonomy for the reapplication of knowledge

question: Regarding end-of-project lessons learned, what does the handbook say is characteristic of an effective Knowledge Management process?

options.A: It waits until all project personnel have departed before initiating capture so that bias is minimized
options.B: It captures relevant information only at major decision gates, not during routine work
options.C: It relies solely on exit interviews because tacit knowledge cannot be captured during execution
options.D: It has knowledge capture mechanisms in place to capture relevant information throughout the life of the project rather than only at the end

correct_answer_text: It has knowledge capture mechanisms in place to capture relevant information throughout the life of the project rather than only at the end

question: Within the KM "Manage knowledge, skills, and knowledge assets" activity, which task is explicitly called out by the handbook?

options.A: Assess and track where the knowledge assets are being used to understand their utility and applicability
options.B: Negotiate licensing fees for every reused asset on a per-use basis
options.C: Encrypt all knowledge assets to prevent reuse outside the originating project
options.D: Eliminate any asset that has not been used within the current fiscal year

correct_answer_text: Assess and track where the knowledge assets are being used to understand their utility and applicability

question: The handbook lists several ways the KM system supports a project. Which of the following is NOT one of the explicitly listed forms of support?

options.A: Knowledge captured from technical experts
options.B: Lessons learned captured from previous similar projects
options.C: Architecture or design patterns that are commonly encountered
options.D: Real-time earned-value performance data from active projects

correct_answer_text: Real-time earned-value performance data from active projects

question: Among the "common approaches and tips" for KM planning, which item is specifically identified by the handbook?

options.A: Criteria for accepting, qualifying, and retiring knowledge assets
options.B: Mandatory annual rewrites of all captured assets regardless of relevance
options.C: A requirement that all assets be open-sourced to external partners
options.D: A prohibition on capturing tacit knowledge in any form

correct_answer_text: Criteria for accepting, qualifying, and retiring knowledge assets

question: In the "KM for Product Lines and Reuse" elaboration, what does the handbook identify as a key responsibility of the KM system for the first instance of a product-line system?

options.A: To capture the domain engineering artifacts in a way that facilitates their use in the future
options.B: To certify the first instance as production-ready without further review
options.C: To delete predecessor artifacts to avoid version conflicts
options.D: To outsource domain engineering to a third-party reuse library

correct_answer_text: To capture the domain engineering artifacts in a way that facilitates their use in the future

question: The handbook warns about "Potential Reuse Issues," particularly for COTS and NDI elements. Which trap does it explicitly illustrate using a NASA Mars probe example?

options.A: Assuming the new system will operate in the same environment as the prior one when in fact it will not
options.B: Assuming the new performance budget is identical to the prior one without re-analysis
options.C: Assuming the reuse asset's licensing terms permit modification without review
options.D: Assuming requirements traceability has already been completed by the original supplier

correct_answer_text: Assuming the new system will operate in the same environment as the prior one when in fact it will not

question: A senior systems engineer with 30 years of domain experience is retiring next quarter. Per the SE Handbook's KM description, which characterization best fits the type of knowledge most at risk of being lost and the effort required to retain it?

options.A: Explicit knowledge embodied in policies and procedures, retainable simply by archiving existing documents
options.B: Tacit knowledge internalized in the individual without conscious realization, requiring specialized techniques to identify and capture
options.C: Project information items that automatically transfer when the engineer's email account is preserved
options.D: Domain engineering artifacts that the Portfolio Management process is required to harvest at project closure

correct_answer_text: Tacit knowledge internalized in the individual without conscious realization, requiring specialized techniques to identify and capture

question: Per the SE Handbook, KM efforts typically focus on organizational objectives. Which of the following is explicitly listed as one of those KM objectives?

options.A: Reducing project-level earned-value reporting overhead
options.B: Eliminating the need for end-of-project lessons learned reviews
options.C: Avoidance of relearning practices and the sharing of good practices or lessons learned
options.D: Centralizing all configuration management decisions under a single organizational board

correct_answer_text: Avoidance of relearning practices and the sharing of good practices or lessons learned

question: According to the SE Handbook, knowledge can come from which two sources?

options.A: Only from individuals via their personal experience
options.B: Only from organizations via processes, practices, and lessons learned
options.C: From individuals (through experience) and from organizations (through processes, practices, and lessons learned)
options.D: Only from external suppliers and standards bodies

correct_answer_text: From individuals (through experience) and from organizations (through processes, practices, and lessons learned)

question: Within the KM activity "Share knowledge assets throughout the organization," what is the explicit purpose of establishing a representation for domain models and domain architectures?

options.A: To enforce a single mandatory modeling notation across all projects
options.B: To replace the Systems Engineering Management Plan with a model-based equivalent
options.C: To help ensure an understanding of the domain and identify and manage opportunities for common system elements and their representations (e.g., patterns, reference architectures, common requirements)
options.D: To serve as the authoritative source for project budget allocation

correct_answer_text: To help ensure an understanding of the domain and identify and manage opportunities for common system elements and their representations (e.g., patterns, reference architectures, common requirements)

question: Under the KM activity "Manage knowledge, skills, and knowledge assets," the handbook explicitly directs which action as the domain, family of systems, or product line evolves?

options.A: Retire all prior assets and recreate them from scratch on each project
options.B: Freeze the associated knowledge assets so they remain identical to the original baseline
options.C: Ensure the associated knowledge assets are revised or replaced to reflect the latest information, and revise associated domain models and architectures as needed
options.D: Transfer ownership of the assets to an external standards body for maintenance

correct_answer_text: Ensure the associated knowledge assets are revised or replaced to reflect the latest information, and revise associated domain models and architectures as needed

question: Per Figure 2.22, which item is a typical OUTPUT of the Knowledge Management process (rather than an input)?

options.A: Organization strategic plan
options.B: Project lessons learned
options.C: Records/artifacts
options.D: Reused system or system element

correct_answer_text: Reused system or system element

question: Per Figure 2.22, "Records/artifacts" appears on which side of the KM IPO diagram?

options.A: As a typical input to the KM process
options.B: As a typical output of the KM process
options.C: As a control (constraint) rather than an input or output
options.D: As an enabler external to the process boundary

correct_answer_text: As a typical input to the KM process

question: Among the "common approaches and tips" for KM planning, which pair of items does the handbook explicitly include alongside accept/qualify/retire criteria?

options.A: Procedures for controlling changes to knowledge assets, and a mechanism for knowledge asset storage and retrieval
options.B: A mandate to certify every reused asset through an external auditor, and quarterly licensing reviews
options.C: A requirement to delete unused assets after one fiscal year, and a ban on tacit knowledge capture
options.D: A real-time earned-value dashboard for KM assets, and project-level baselines for each asset

correct_answer_text: Procedures for controlling changes to knowledge assets, and a mechanism for knowledge asset storage and retrieval

question: A reuse team proposes deploying a COTS sensor controller, originally qualified for benchtop laboratory use, into a vibration-heavy automotive application. Per the handbook's "Potential Reuse Issues," which trap does this scenario most directly illustrate?

options.A: Assuming licensing terms allow modification without review
options.B: Assuming the prior asset "worked perfectly" so it must work in the new application, even when the new application is outside the qualified range
options.C: Assuming requirements traceability has been completed by the original supplier
options.D: Assuming a SEMP exists for the reuse activity

correct_answer_text: Assuming the prior asset "worked perfectly" so it must work in the new application, even when the new application is outside the qualified range

question: Per the "Potential Reuse Issues" discussion, what trap does the handbook flag specifically with respect to emergent requirements or behaviors when reusing COTS?

options.A: COTS suppliers always provide a documented emergence analysis, so no trap exists
options.B: A solution that worked in the past is used without consideration for solution evolution, and if COTS is used there may be no way to adapt or modify it for emergent requirements
options.C: Emergent behaviors are always discovered during requirements analysis, never during integration
options.D: The KM system is required to predict every emergent behavior before reuse is permitted

correct_answer_text: A solution that worked in the past is used without consideration for solution evolution, and if COTS is used there may be no way to adapt or modify it for emergent requirements

question: For subsequent instances of a product line or system family, what role does the handbook assign to the KM system?

options.A: To freeze the original domain engineering artifacts and prohibit any updates
options.B: To provide the domain engineering information and capture any variations, updates of technology, and lessons learned
options.C: To replace the Technical Processes for those subsequent instances
options.D: To transfer responsibility for reuse decisions to the project management practitioner only

correct_answer_text: To provide the domain engineering information and capture any variations, updates of technology, and lessons learned

question: In developing an understanding of the domain, the handbook says the domain representations should include all of the following EXCEPT:

options.A: Definition of the boundaries
options.B: Relationships of the domains to other domains
options.C: Domain models that incorporate the commonalities and differences allowing for sensitivity analysis
options.D: A real-time earned-value baseline for each domain element

correct_answer_text: A real-time earned-value baseline for each domain element

question: Categorize each item in the Knowledge Management Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.22).

items.text: Share knowledge and skills throughout the organization
Share knowledge assets throughout the organization
Manage knowledge, skills, and knowledge assets

question: According to the SE Handbook, what is the primary concern of the Technical Management Processes as defined in ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288?

options.A: Defining stakeholder needs and translating them into system requirements
options.B: Managing the resources and assets allocated by organization management and applying them to fulfill agreements entered into by the organization(s)
options.C: Acquiring components and services from external suppliers under contractual agreements
options.D: Establishing the organization-wide policies and procedures that govern all projects

correct_answer_text: Managing the resources and assets allocated by organization management and applying them to fulfill agreements entered into by the organization(s)

question: How many processes make up the Technical Management Processes group, and which set are they?

options.A: Seven: Project Planning, Project Assessment and Control, Decision Management, Risk Management, Configuration Management, Information Management, and Measurement
options.B: Eight: Project Planning, Project Assessment and Control, Decision Management, Risk Management, Configuration Management, Information Management, Measurement, and Quality Assurance
options.C: Six: Project Planning, Project Assessment and Control, Risk Management, Configuration Management, Measurement, and Quality Assurance
options.D: Nine: Project Planning, Project Assessment and Control, Decision Management, Risk Management, Configuration Management, Information Management, Knowledge Management, Measurement, and Quality Assurance

correct_answer_text: Eight: Project Planning, Project Assessment and Control, Decision Management, Risk Management, Configuration Management, Information Management, Measurement, and Quality Assurance

question: The handbook defines technical management as the application of technical and administrative resources to do which of the following?

options.A: Acquire, integrate, and verify system elements
options.B: Plan, organize, and control engineering functions
options.C: Operate, maintain, and dispose of fielded systems
options.D: Design, analyze, and validate system architectures

correct_answer_text: Plan, organize, and control engineering functions

question: The handbook notes that a life cycle from the project management practitioner's point of view differs from the SE practitioner's view. Which pair correctly identifies these viewpoints?

options.A: PM: project start to project end; SE: system concept to system retirement
options.B: PM: contract award to contract closeout; SE: requirements to verification
options.C: PM: system concept to system retirement; SE: project start to project end
options.D: PM: acquisition to disposal; SE: design to operation

correct_answer_text: PM: project start to project end; SE: system concept to system retirement

question: As quoted from ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288, what is the purpose of the Project Planning process?

options.A: To assess if the plans are aligned and feasible and to direct execution to meet objectives
options.B: To produce and coordinate effective and workable plans
options.C: To identify, analyze, treat, and monitor risks throughout the project
options.D: To establish the technical baselines and control changes to project artifacts

correct_answer_text: To produce and coordinate effective and workable plans

question: According to the Description of the Project Planning process, planning begins and ends at which points?

options.A: It starts at contract award and ends at the System Verification Review
options.B: It starts with the identification of a new potential project and continues after authorization and activation of the project until its termination
options.C: It starts at the Preliminary Design Review and ends at the Critical Design Review
options.D: It starts at project authorization and ends when the SEMP is approved

correct_answer_text: It starts with the identification of a new potential project and continues after authorization and activation of the project until its termination

question: The Project Planning process is performed in the organization's context and in compliance with which other technical management or organizational-level process?

options.A: Portfolio Management process
options.B: Quality Management process
options.C: Life Cycle Model Management process
options.D: Knowledge Management process

correct_answer_text: Life Cycle Model Management process

question: Per Figure 2.23 (IPO diagram for Project Planning), which of the following is listed as a typical INPUT to the Project Planning process?

options.A: Systems engineering management plan (SEMP)
options.B: Breakdown structures
options.C: Organization strategic plan
options.D: Project schedule

correct_answer_text: Organization strategic plan

question: Per Figure 2.23 (IPO diagram for Project Planning), which of the following is listed as a typical OUTPUT of the Project Planning process?

options.A: Project portfolio
options.B: Supply response
options.C: Life cycle models
options.D: Systems engineering management plan (SEMP)

correct_answer_text: Systems engineering management plan (SEMP)

question: Per Figure 2.23, the three top-level activities of the Project Planning process are which of the following?

options.A: Define the project; Plan project and technical management; Activate the project
options.B: Plan the project; Execute the plan; Close out the project
options.C: Identify stakeholders; Develop the SEMP; Baseline the WBS
options.D: Initiate; Plan; Control

correct_answer_text: Define the project; Plan project and technical management; Activate the project

question: Under "Define the project," which activity is performed to analyze the project supply response and related agreements?

options.A: Establish the achievement criteria for major milestones and decision gates
options.B: Define the project objectives, assumptions, constraints, and scope
options.C: Develop a Systems Engineering Master Schedule (SEMS)
options.D: Obtain project authorization and resources

correct_answer_text: Define the project objectives, assumptions, constraints, and scope

question: Under "Plan project and technical management," the handbook lists which artifact as the key plan to be generated and communicated for project and technical management/execution, including technical reviews and audits?

options.A: Project Management Plan (PMP)
options.B: Software Development Plan (SDP)
options.C: Systems Engineering Management Plan (SEMP), also called a Systems Engineering Plan (SEP)
options.D: Risk Management Plan (RMP)

correct_answer_text: Systems Engineering Management Plan (SEMP), also called a Systems Engineering Plan (SEP)

question: Per the "Activate the project" activity, which process provides the project authorization that must be obtained to commence execution?

options.A: Acquisition process
options.B: Portfolio Management process
options.C: Quality Management process
options.D: Decision Management process

correct_answer_text: Portfolio Management process

question: The handbook cites which ISO/IEC/IEEE standard as a reference to aid in writing a SEMP?

options.A: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288
options.B: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-1
options.C: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-4 on Systems Engineering Planning
options.D: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-5

correct_answer_text: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-4 on Systems Engineering Planning

question: Under "Common approaches and tips," the handbook says project value should be expressed using which type of measure to guide project decision making?

options.A: Earned value (EV) metrics
options.B: Technical performance measures (TPMs)
options.C: Key performance indicators (KPIs)
options.D: Quality characteristic (QC) measures

correct_answer_text: Technical performance measures (TPMs)

question: How does the handbook characterize the role of the SEMP in integrating the SE effort?

options.A: It is a low-level checklist for system verification activities
options.B: It is the key technical management plan that defines how the total set of engineering processes will be organized, structured, conducted, and controlled to deliver a product that satisfies stakeholder requirements
options.C: It is primarily a financial document used to allocate budget across work packages
options.D: It is the contractual statement of work between acquirer and supplier

correct_answer_text: It is the key technical management plan that defines how the total set of engineering processes will be organized, structured, conducted, and controlled to deliver a product that satisfies stakeholder requirements

question: According to the SEMP guidance, when should a SEMP be prepared and submitted, and for which stages is it used in technical management?

options.A: Prepared at production start; used during production and deployment stages
options.B: Prepared after operational acceptance; used during operation and maintenance stages
options.C: Prepared early in the project, submitted to the customer (or to management for in-house projects), and used in technical management for the concept and development stages
options.D: Prepared at retirement; used to govern disposal of the SoI

correct_answer_text: Prepared early in the project, submitted to the customer (or to management for in-house projects), and used in technical management for the concept and development stages

question: The handbook calls the SEMS an essential part of the SEMP. What is its principal value as a tool for project control?

options.A: It captures the cost baseline used for earned-value management
options.B: It identifies the critical path of technical activities in the project, helping prioritize effort and justify resource requests
options.C: It documents stakeholder requirements and acceptance criteria
options.D: It baselines the system architecture for configuration control

correct_answer_text: It identifies the critical path of technical activities in the project, helping prioritize effort and justify resource requests

question: Per the "Breakdown Structures" elaboration, what is their fundamental purpose in projects?

options.A: To allocate functions to physical components for design optimization
options.B: To hierarchically decompose constructs into manageable and understandable elements
options.C: To define interfaces between system elements and their environments
options.D: To assign quality characteristics to deliverables

correct_answer_text: To hierarchically decompose constructs into manageable and understandable elements

question: Per the handbook, what does the Function Tree (Functional Breakdown Structure, FBS) represent?

options.A: A breakdown of the SoI into successively lower-level details of its physical architecture
options.B: A breakdown of the functions of the required SoI into successively lower levels of its functional architecture, including technical characteristics of each function
options.C: A hierarchical tree of cost elements used to estimate project budgets
options.D: A hierarchical tree of the organizational elements that perform project work

correct_answer_text: A breakdown of the functions of the required SoI into successively lower levels of its functional architecture, including technical characteristics of each function

question: How does the SE Handbook define the Product Tree, or Product Breakdown Structure (PBS)?

options.A: A breakdown of all work activities required to develop the product
options.B: A breakdown of cost elements arranged hierarchically for budget control
options.C: A breakdown of the SoI into successively lower-level details of its physical architecture
options.D: A hierarchical tree of the organizational elements responsible for delivering the product

correct_answer_text: A breakdown of the SoI into successively lower-level details of its physical architecture

question: Per the handbook, the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) organizes the work to reach the project's objectives. On which other breakdown structure is the WBS based during the initial stages of system maturity (e.g., feasibility, conceptual design)?

options.A: The Product Breakdown Structure (PBS)
options.B: The Functional Breakdown Structure (FBS)
options.C: The Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS)
options.D: The Organizational Breakdown Structure (OBS)

correct_answer_text: The Functional Breakdown Structure (FBS)

question: Why does the handbook state that breakdown structures provide a basis for configuration control?

options.A: Because they automatically define interfaces among system elements
options.B: Because once a particular project breakdown structure is baselined, it provides a basis for effective management of changes
options.C: Because they identify the qualified personnel needed for the project
options.D: Because they replace the need for technical reviews and audits

correct_answer_text: Because once a particular project breakdown structure is baselined, it provides a basis for effective management of changes

question: Per Section 2.3.4, why are the Technical Management Processes important to systems engineering of new or existing systems?

options.A: They define the technical solution that satisfies stakeholder requirements
options.B: They ensure the successful management of the SE effort within a project, because the engineering of systems is managed by the conduct of projects
options.C: They establish the organization-wide infrastructure that supports all projects
options.D: They acquire products and services from external suppliers

correct_answer_text: They ensure the successful management of the SE effort within a project, because the engineering of systems is managed by the conduct of projects

question: Per Section 2.3.4, although the PM and SE life-cycle viewpoints differ, the handbook says the two practitioners must collaborate in a "shared space." What primarily distinguishes these viewpoints?

options.A: The PM viewpoint is programmatic (project start to project end), whereas the SE viewpoint is technical and broader (system concept to system retirement)
options.B: The PM viewpoint covers only cost and schedule; the SE viewpoint covers only requirements
options.C: The PM viewpoint is internal to the organization; the SE viewpoint is contractual
options.D: The PM viewpoint is qualitative; the SE viewpoint is quantitative

correct_answer_text: The PM viewpoint is programmatic (project start to project end), whereas the SE viewpoint is technical and broader (system concept to system retirement)

question: Per the "Define the project" activity, which sub-activity captures tailoring of the life cycle model used by the project, including major milestones, decision gates, and project reviews?

options.A: Establish the achievement criteria for major milestones and decision gates
options.B: Define and maintain a life cycle model that could be tailored from the defined life cycle models of the organization
options.C: Obtain authorization for the necessary project resources
options.D: Generate and communicate a SEMP for project and technical management

correct_answer_text: Define and maintain a life cycle model that could be tailored from the defined life cycle models of the organization

question: Per the "Plan project and technical management" activity, on what basis should top-level work packages be defined?

options.A: By acquirer-supplied templates only
options.B: By organization-wide cost accounts inherited from the strategic plan
options.C: By each activity identified, with each work package tied to required resources including procurement strategies
options.D: By the system architect's product breakdown structure exclusively

correct_answer_text: By each activity identified, with each work package tied to required resources including procurement strategies

question: Per the SEMP elaboration, which of the following is identified as a typical content area of a SEMP?

options.A: Detailed unit-level test procedures for each subsystem
options.B: Definition of the SE processes, including interaction with other engineering and project processes
options.C: The acquirer's contractual statement of work line items
options.D: Verification matrices linking each requirement to a test case

correct_answer_text: Definition of the SE processes, including interaction with other engineering and project processes

question: Per the SEMP elaboration, which of the following is NOT listed by the handbook as typical SEMP content?

options.A: Responsibilities and authority of the key engineering roles
options.B: Approach and methods for planning and executing applicable quality characteristic (QC) approaches
options.C: Infrastructure support and resource management (facilities, tools, IT, personnel)
options.D: Detailed financial accounting ledgers and invoicing rules

correct_answer_text: Detailed financial accounting ledgers and invoicing rules

question: Per the handbook, what is the relationship between the WBS, work packages, and the Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS)?

options.A: The CBS is a parallel structure unrelated to the WBS
options.B: Each branch of the WBS defines a work package, and the WBS serves as a reference for the identification of cost elements arranged into the CBS
options.C: The CBS is derived directly from the FBS without using the WBS
options.D: Work packages are derived from the OBS and rolled up into the CBS

correct_answer_text: Each branch of the WBS defines a work package, and the WBS serves as a reference for the identification of cost elements arranged into the CBS

question: Per the breakdown structures elaboration, what does the Organizational Breakdown Structure (OBS) represent?

options.A: A hierarchical tree of cost elements traceable to work packages
options.B: A hierarchical tree of the SoI's physical elements
options.C: A hierarchical tree of the organizational elements (used with the WBS to determine who does what)
options.D: A hierarchical tree of the functions allocated to the SoI

correct_answer_text: A hierarchical tree of the organizational elements (used with the WBS to determine who does what)

question: Per the handbook, in the later stages of system maturity (after feasibility and conceptual design), on which breakdown structure is the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) primarily based?

options.A: The Functional Breakdown Structure (FBS)
options.B: The Product Breakdown Structure (PBS)
options.C: The Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS)
options.D: The Organizational Breakdown Structure (OBS)

correct_answer_text: The Product Breakdown Structure (PBS)

question: Per the handbook, which statement best distinguishes the FBS from the PBS?

options.A: The FBS decomposes functions of the SoI; the PBS decomposes the SoI into successively lower-level details of its physical architecture
options.B: The FBS decomposes the SoI's physical parts; the PBS decomposes functions allocated to those parts
options.C: The FBS organizes work packages; the PBS organizes cost elements
options.D: Both structures decompose the same content but at different scales

correct_answer_text: The FBS decomposes functions of the SoI; the PBS decomposes the SoI into successively lower-level details of its physical architecture

question: Per the "Activate the project" activity, which event marks the practical start of project execution after authorization?

options.A: Approval of the SEMP by the systems engineer
options.B: Baselining of the system architecture
options.C: Obtaining project authorization and resources, then commencing execution of the project plans
options.D: Completion of the System Requirements Review

correct_answer_text: Obtaining project authorization and resources, then commencing execution of the project plans

question: Per the common approaches and tips, which standard does the handbook reference for the format of plans used to develop software (often a Software Development Plan)?

options.A: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288
options.B: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-4
options.C: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-5
options.D: ISO/IEC 12207

correct_answer_text: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-5

question: Per the Project Planning elaboration, what does "compliance with the Life Cycle Model Management process" mean in practice for a project being planned?

options.A: The project must use the exact life cycle model defined at the organization level without any tailoring
options.B: The project must follow the relevant policies and procedures applicable to all projects owned by the organization, as identified and established by Life Cycle Model Management
options.C: The project must report financial performance to the organization's portfolio managers
options.D: The project must adopt the customer's life cycle model in place of the organization's

correct_answer_text: The project must follow the relevant policies and procedures applicable to all projects owned by the organization, as identified and established by Life Cycle Model Management

question: Per Figure 2.23, which of the following is a typical INPUT to the Project Planning process that captures organization-defined direction and project-portfolio context?

options.A: Project schedule
options.B: Project authorization request
options.C: Project portfolio
options.D: Breakdown structures

correct_answer_text: Project portfolio

question: Categorize each item in the Project Planning Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.23).
From Figure 2.24 (Breakdown Structures), drag each description or characteristic to the breakdown structure type it describes.

items.text: Hierarchical tree of all activities/work needed to develop the product, with each branch defining a Work Package (WP)
Serves as the reference/basis for identifying cost elements and for determining who does what (schedule and responsibility allocation)
Based on the FBS in early stages (feasibility, conceptual design) and on the PBS in later stages of system maturity
Also called the Product Tree; a breakdown of the SoI into successively lower-level details of its physical architecture
Decomposition of physical product elements that provides the framework for configuration management (CM) baselines
Hierarchical tree of the organizational elements showing the responsible person/unit for performing each activity
Represents, along with the overall management organization, who does what in conjunction with the WBS
Hierarchical arrangement of cost elements derived from the WBS, used as a framework for financial control
Provides the cost categories structure used for budgeting, cost accounting, and reporting on the project
Also called the Function Tree; a breakdown of the required SoI functions into successively lower levels of its functional architecture
Includes the technical characteristics of each function and provides the basis from which the PBS (Product Tree) is derived

question: According to ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288, what is the purpose of the Project Assessment and Control process?

options.A: To define the work breakdown structure and assign work packages to organizational elements
options.B: To assess if the plans are aligned and feasible, determine the status of the project, technical and process performance, and direct execution to help ensure performance is according to plans and schedules, within projected budgets, to satisfy technical objectives
options.C: To identify, analyze, treat, and monitor risks throughout the project life cycle
options.D: To establish a measurement program that supplies objective information for management decisions

correct_answer_text: To assess if the plans are aligned and feasible, determine the status of the project, technical and process performance, and direct execution to help ensure performance is according to plans and schedules, within projected budgets, to satisfy technical objectives

question: When are assessments scheduled under the Project Assessment and Control process?

options.A: Only at the end of each life cycle stage
options.B: Only when a stakeholder formally requests a status review
options.C: Periodically and for all milestones and decision gates
options.D: Only when a deviation from plan has already been detected

correct_answer_text: Periodically and for all milestones and decision gates

question: The Project Assessment and Control process collects data to evaluate which of the following?

options.A: Only the cost and schedule variances against the project budget
options.B: The adequacy of the project infrastructure, the availability of necessary resources, and compliance with project performance measures
options.C: Only the technical maturity of the system of interest at decision gates
options.D: The acquirer-supplier agreements and contractual terms

correct_answer_text: The adequacy of the project infrastructure, the availability of necessary resources, and compliance with project performance measures

question: According to the handbook, the rigor of the Project Assessment and Control process is directly dependent on which factor?

options.A: The size of the acquirer organization and the contract type
options.B: The number of suppliers and external interfaces involved
options.C: The intrinsic characteristics of the project and SoI, such as complexity, urgency, and consequence of failure to deliver or failure of the SoI
options.D: The selected life cycle model (sequential, incremental, or evolutionary)

correct_answer_text: The intrinsic characteristics of the project and SoI, such as complexity, urgency, and consequence of failure to deliver or failure of the SoI

question: Which statement BEST characterizes project control under this process?

options.A: Project control consists exclusively of corrective actions taken after a nonconformance has occurred
options.B: Project control involves both preventive and corrective actions taken to ensure that the project is performing according to plans and schedules and within projected budgets
options.C: Project control is limited to authorizing the project to proceed through decision gates
options.D: Project control is the act of updating the Systems Engineering Management Plan after each milestone

correct_answer_text: Project control involves both preventive and corrective actions taken to ensure that the project is performing according to plans and schedules and within projected budgets

question: Per Figure 2.25, which item is a typical INPUT to the Project Assessment and Control process?

options.A: Project status report/dashboard
options.B: Project control request
options.C: Systems engineering management plan (SEMP)
options.D: Project lessons learned

correct_answer_text: Systems engineering management plan (SEMP)

question: Per Figure 2.25, which item is a typical OUTPUT of the Project Assessment and Control process?

options.A: Project budget
options.B: Project measurement data
options.C: Configuration baseline
options.D: Project control request

correct_answer_text: Project control request

question: Which activity is listed under the "Assess the project" activity group rather than under "Control the project"?

options.A: Initiate preventive actions when assessments indicate a trend toward deviation
options.B: Initiate corrective actions when assessments indicate deviation from approved plans
options.C: Determine cost, schedule, and performance variances through assessment of status versus plans
options.D: Authorize the project to proceed when assessments support a decision gate or milestone event

correct_answer_text: Determine cost, schedule, and performance variances through assessment of status versus plans

question: Within the "Control the project" activities, what should be initiated when assessments indicate a trend toward deviation?

options.A: Corrective actions
options.B: Preventive actions
options.C: Problem resolution
options.D: Change actions

correct_answer_text: Preventive actions

question: Per the "Control the project" activities, what action should be initiated when assessments indicate nonconformance with performance success criteria?

options.A: Preventive actions
options.B: Corrective actions
options.C: Problem resolution
options.D: Replanning of the entire project schedule

correct_answer_text: Problem resolution

question: According to the handbook, change actions to reflect contractual changes to cost, time, or quality are usually triggered by what?

options.A: The discovery of new technical risks during assessment
options.B: The impact of acquirer or supplier requests
options.C: Internal quality assurance findings
options.D: Failure of a system element during verification

correct_answer_text: The impact of acquirer or supplier requests

question: How does the handbook describe the relationship between the Project Assessment and Control process and the Measurement process?

options.A: Measurement is a sub-activity of Project Assessment and Control and produces no independent outputs
options.B: The Measurement process provides useful insights into risks, technical reviews and audits, and quality assurance; the results feed an integrated view supporting assessment and control decisions
options.C: Measurement is performed only at decision gates and is independent of project assessment
options.D: Project Assessment and Control replaces the Measurement process during the production stage

correct_answer_text: The Measurement process provides useful insights into risks, technical reviews and audits, and quality assurance; the results feed an integrated view supporting assessment and control decisions

question: Regarding life cycle model selection, what does the handbook state about assessment cadence?

options.A: Sequential models require more frequent assessments than incremental or evolutionary models
options.B: Incremental and evolutionary models typically schedule frequent assessments and make project control adjustments on tighter feedback cycles than sequential development models
options.C: Assessment cadence is identical across all life cycle models
options.D: Evolutionary models eliminate the need for periodic assessments because feedback is continuous

correct_answer_text: Incremental and evolutionary models typically schedule frequent assessments and make project control adjustments on tighter feedback cycles than sequential development models

question: How does the handbook describe the division of accountability between PM and SE practitioners in this process?

options.A: PM is accountable for technical activities while SE is accountable for the overall project results
options.B: PM and SE share equal accountability for both technical and project-level outcomes
options.C: PM is accountable for the overall results of the project, with SE accountable for the achievement of the technical activities
options.D: Accountability is delegated entirely to the acquirer for assessment and to the supplier for control

correct_answer_text: PM is accountable for the overall results of the project, with SE accountable for the achievement of the technical activities

question: Per the elaboration on Technical Reviews and Audits, from what viewpoint should each technical review or audit be conducted?

options.A: From a purely technical viewpoint focused only on requirements compliance
options.B: From an integrated project viewpoint including technical status and progress, cost and schedule status, and impacts and risk assessment
options.C: From the acquirer's contractual viewpoint to confirm deliverable acceptance
options.D: From the supplier's quality assurance viewpoint to confirm process compliance

correct_answer_text: From an integrated project viewpoint including technical status and progress, cost and schedule status, and impacts and risk assessment

question: What does the handbook recommend regarding the collection of project measures?

options.A: Collect as many measures as possible to maximize visibility into project status
options.B: Collect only measures explicitly required by the acquirer's contract
options.C: Avoid the collection of measures that are not used in decision making
options.D: Limit measurement to schedule and cost variance to reduce overhead

correct_answer_text: Avoid the collection of measures that are not used in decision making

question: Where does the handbook recommend formally describing the methods and techniques for Project Assessment and Control?

options.A: In the project's risk register
options.B: In the Systems Engineering Management Plan (SEMP) or PMP, agreed with the project team
options.C: In the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) dictionary
options.D: In the Configuration Management Plan

correct_answer_text: In the Systems Engineering Management Plan (SEMP) or PMP, agreed with the project team

question: Under the "Assess the project" sub-activity, which task is explicitly intended to check that the people side of the project is sound?

options.A: Initiate corrective actions when deviation is observed
options.B: Determine whether project roles, responsibilities, accountabilities, and authorities are adequate
options.C: Authorize the project to proceed at a decision gate
options.D: Update project planning to reflect change actions

correct_answer_text: Determine whether project roles, responsibilities, accountabilities, and authorities are adequate

question: Which activity belongs to the "Control the project" sub-activity rather than to "Assess the project"?

options.A: Monitor areas of high risk such as critical tasks and new technologies/applications
options.B: Using project measures and milestone status, assess the progress of the project
options.C: Authorize the project to proceed when assessments support a decision gate or milestone event
options.D: Conduct required reviews, audits, and inspections to determine readiness to proceed

correct_answer_text: Authorize the project to proceed when assessments support a decision gate or milestone event

question: A weekly assessment reveals that earned-value indices are steadily drifting toward the schedule reserve threshold but no plan deviation has yet occurred. Per the "Control the project" activities, which response is most appropriate?

options.A: Initiate corrective actions because a deviation from approved plans has occurred
options.B: Initiate preventive actions because the assessment indicates a trend toward deviation
options.C: Initiate problem resolution because nonconformance with performance success criteria exists
options.D: Implement change actions to reflect contractual changes to cost, time, or quality

correct_answer_text: Initiate preventive actions because the assessment indicates a trend toward deviation

question: Match the trigger to the correct "Control the project" response per the handbook.

options.A: Trend toward deviation -> corrective action; deviation from plan -> preventive action
options.B: Nonconformance with success criteria -> change action; acquirer request -> problem resolution
options.C: Trend toward deviation -> preventive; nonconformance with success criteria -> problem resolution; deviation from plan -> corrective; acquirer/supplier request -> change action
options.D: All four triggers map to corrective action, distinguished only by severity

correct_answer_text: Trend toward deviation -> preventive; nonconformance with success criteria -> problem resolution; deviation from plan -> corrective; acquirer/supplier request -> change action

question: Per Figure 2.25, which is listed as a typical OUTPUT (not an input) of the Project Assessment and Control process?

options.A: Project budget
options.B: Project schedule
options.C: Project status report/dashboard
options.D: Breakdown structures

correct_answer_text: Project status report/dashboard
