question: Per Figure 2.25, which item appears among the typical INPUTS to the Project Assessment and Control process?

options.A: Change request
options.B: Project lessons learned
options.C: Project control request
options.D: Project assessment and control records/artifacts

correct_answer_text: Change request

question: The handbook notes that "what gets measured gets done." What caveat does it attach to this maxim?

options.A: Teams should measure every available indicator to ensure none is missed
options.B: Projects should avoid the collection of measures that are not used in decision making
options.C: Only contractually mandated measures should be collected
options.D: Measures should be limited to schedule and cost variance

correct_answer_text: Projects should avoid the collection of measures that are not used in decision making

question: Per the "Integration of Technical Management Artifacts" elaboration, why does the handbook stress an integrated view of results from the Technical Management Processes?

options.A: Because each process is independent and produces results that cannot be cross-referenced
options.B: Because relationships exist between these processes and their artifacts (e.g., Measurement provides insights into risks, technical reviews and audits, and quality assurance), and decision makers need to see trends and trigger points quickly
options.C: Because acquirer contracts require a single consolidated report
options.D: Because only the integrated view is admissible at a decision gate

correct_answer_text: Because relationships exist between these processes and their artifacts (e.g., Measurement provides insights into risks, technical reviews and audits, and quality assurance), and decision makers need to see trends and trigger points quickly

question: Which pair of mechanisms does the handbook explicitly identify as providing an integrated, summarized view of project information for the Project Assessment and Control process?

options.A: The risk register and the change log
options.B: The project status report and the project dashboard
options.C: The WBS dictionary and the OBS chart
options.D: The SEMP and the PMP

correct_answer_text: The project status report and the project dashboard

question: Per the "Assess the project" activities, what specific role does monitoring of critical tasks and new technologies/applications play?

options.A: It substitutes for the Risk Management process during the production stage
options.B: It is the assessment activity dedicated to monitoring areas of high risk, with reference to the Risk Management process (Section 2.3.4.4)
options.C: It is part of the change action workflow triggered by acquirer requests
options.D: It is restricted to evolutionary life cycle models only

correct_answer_text: It is the assessment activity dedicated to monitoring areas of high risk, with reference to the Risk Management process (Section 2.3.4.4)

question: According to the handbook, what triggers the "Update project planning" step inside the "Control the project" activities?

options.A: Annual budget cycles imposed by the acquirer
options.B: Any change in the configuration baseline regardless of plan impact
options.C: The need to reflect project control actions or corrective actions that have been initiated
options.D: The completion of every technical review or audit

correct_answer_text: The need to reflect project control actions or corrective actions that have been initiated

question: The handbook's "Common approaches and tips" identifies which industry framework as a source of guidelines (including Earned Value Management techniques) for project assessment?

options.A: ISO 9001 quality management
options.B: PMI - Project Management Institute
options.C: ITIL service management
options.D: CMMI process improvement

correct_answer_text: PMI - Project Management Institute

question: Per the activity list, what does the project status report gather as its content?

options.A: Only schedule and cost variance against the WBS
options.B: Technical progress aspects (e.g., performance, requirements compliance, V&V progress) and technical management aspects (e.g., schedule, cost, risk, configuration status)
options.C: Only decision gate results and milestone events
options.D: Only acquirer-facing communications required by contract

correct_answer_text: Technical progress aspects (e.g., performance, requirements compliance, V&V progress) and technical management aspects (e.g., schedule, cost, risk, configuration status)

question: The handbook lists critical areas the project team must identify and control through which set of mechanisms?

options.A: Measurement, risk management, analysis, configuration management, and information management
options.B: Acquirer reviews, supplier audits, and contractual change requests
options.C: Technical reviews, decision gates, and milestone events only
options.D: Earned value, schedule reserve, and cost reserve only

correct_answer_text: Measurement, risk management, analysis, configuration management, and information management

question: Categorize each item in the Project Assessment and Control Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.25).

items.text: Plan for project assessment and control

question: Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288, what is the purpose of the Decision Management process?

options.A: To identify, analyze, treat, and monitor risks continually throughout the system life cycle
options.B: To provide a structured, analytical framework for objectively identifying, characterizing, and evaluating a set of alternatives for a decision at any point in the life cycle and select the most beneficial course of action
options.C: To control the configuration of work products and changes to them so that the integrity of the system baseline is preserved
options.D: To plan, monitor, and control the technical effort to ensure the system is delivered within cost and schedule constraints

correct_answer_text: To provide a structured, analytical framework for objectively identifying, characterizing, and evaluating a set of alternatives for a decision at any point in the life cycle and select the most beneficial course of action

question: Which of the following BEST distinguishes the Decision Management process from the Risk Management process?

options.A: Decision Management treats uncertainties continually across the life cycle, while Risk Management evaluates discrete alternatives at decision points
options.B: Decision Management transforms a broadly stated decision situation into a recommended course of action by evaluating alternatives, while Risk Management identifies, analyzes, treats, and monitors uncertainties (threats/opportunities) continually
options.C: Decision Management is concerned only with disposal approaches, while Risk Management is concerned with the entire life cycle
options.D: Decision Management produces a risk register, while Risk Management produces a decision register

correct_answer_text: Decision Management transforms a broadly stated decision situation into a recommended course of action by evaluating alternatives, while Risk Management identifies, analyzes, treats, and monitors uncertainties (threats/opportunities) continually

question: According to the Description of the Decision Management process, which combination of participants is required to execute the process?

options.A: Only a decision maker and a project manager
options.B: Only subject matter experts and end users
options.C: A decision maker with full responsibility, authority, and accountability; a decision analyst with a suite of decision tools; subject matter experts with performance models; and a representative set of end users and other stakeholders
options.D: A configuration manager, a risk manager, and a verification lead acting jointly

correct_answer_text: A decision maker with full responsibility, authority, and accountability; a decision analyst with a suite of decision tools; subject matter experts with performance models; and a representative set of end users and other stakeholders

question: Per Table 2.4 (Partial list of decision situations), which decision situation is identified with the Concept stage?

options.A: Select maintenance approach
options.B: Perform make-or-buy decision
options.C: Select production process and location
options.D: Conduct analysis of alternatives

correct_answer_text: Conduct analysis of alternatives

question: Per Table 2.4, which decision situation is associated with the Development life cycle stage?

options.A: Select disposal approach
options.B: Select verification and validation methods
options.C: Craft a technology development strategy
options.D: Select production process and location

correct_answer_text: Select verification and validation methods

question: Per Table 2.4, which decision situation is identified with the Retirement life cycle stage?

options.A: Select system architecture
options.B: Select maintenance approach
options.C: Select disposal approach
options.D: Select lower-level elements

correct_answer_text: Select disposal approach

question: According to Figure 2.26 (IPO diagram), which of the following is listed as a typical INPUT to the Decision Management process?

options.A: Decision register
options.B: Decision management report
options.C: System analysis report
options.D: Decision management strategy/approach

correct_answer_text: System analysis report

question: According to Figure 2.26, which of the following is a typical OUTPUT of the Decision Management process?

options.A: Constraints on solution
options.B: Decision situations
options.C: System analysis report
options.D: Decision register

correct_answer_text: Decision register

question: The Decision Management process activities are organized into which three top-level groupings?

options.A: Identify risks; Analyze risks; Treat risks
options.B: Prepare for decisions; Analyze the decision information; Make and manage decisions
options.C: Plan decisions; Execute decisions; Verify decisions
options.D: Frame decisions; Document decisions; Audit decisions

correct_answer_text: Prepare for decisions; Analyze the decision information; Make and manage decisions

question: Which activity is listed under "Prepare for decisions" rather than under "Analyze the decision information"?

options.A: Generate creative alternatives
options.B: Assess alternatives via deterministic analysis
options.C: Develop the decision management strategy/approach for system or project decisions
options.D: Identify uncertainties and conduct probabilistic analysis

correct_answer_text: Develop the decision management strategy/approach for system or project decisions

question: Under "Make and manage decisions," what must be recorded along with the decision itself?

options.A: Only the final selected alternative
options.B: Relevant data, models, and supporting documentation (i.e., the decision authority, source, and rationale)
options.C: Only the names of the stakeholders consulted
options.D: Only the cost and schedule impact of the decision

correct_answer_text: Relevant data, models, and supporting documentation (i.e., the decision authority, source, and rationale)

question: According to the Elaboration, which decision management method is MOST employed by SE practitioners?

options.A: Single-attribute utility (SAU) analysis
options.B: Multiple objective decision approach (MODA), in which an objective function synthesizes an alternative's response across multiple, often competing, objectives
options.C: Pure cost-benefit ratio ranking
options.D: Decision trees applied with a single number per end node

correct_answer_text: Multiple objective decision approach (MODA), in which an objective function synthesizes an alternative's response across multiple, often competing, objectives

question: When is a decision tree described as a well-suited analytical approach for a decision problem?

options.A: When there are many continuous variables and no discrete events between alternatives and consequences
options.B: When clear, important, and discrete events stand between the implementation of alternatives and the eventual consequences, and the structure has only a few decision and chance nodes
options.C: Whenever the decision involves more than ten objectives
options.D: Only when end-node consequences cannot be expressed as a single number

correct_answer_text: When clear, important, and discrete events stand between the implementation of alternatives and the eventual consequences, and the structure has only a few decision and chance nodes

question: Per the Elaboration on developing objectives and measures, MODA models should use which type of objectives?

options.A: Means objectives (how) rather than fundamental objectives
options.B: Fundamental objectives (why, what, where, and when), not means objectives (how)
options.C: Only stakeholder preference objectives, excluding technical performance measures
options.D: Only cost objectives, since they are most easily measured

correct_answer_text: Fundamental objectives (why, what, where, and when), not means objectives (how)

question: Per the Elaboration, measures used in MODA models (also known as MOEs, KPPs, MOPs, or TPMs) must possess which set of characteristics?

options.A: Quantitative, financial, and digital
options.B: Ambiguous, broad, indirect, theoretical, and aspirational
options.C: Unambiguous, comprehensive, direct, operational, and understandable
options.D: Optional, conflicting, redundant, and means-oriented

correct_answer_text: Unambiguous, comprehensive, direct, operational, and understandable

question: Per the Common Approaches and Tips, why is it important to verify and validate the data and assumptions used in decision analyses?

options.A: Because configuration management requires baselining of input data
options.B: Because the validity of the analysis results depends on the use of valid data and assumptions, and the application of appropriate analytic methods
options.C: Because ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 prohibits the use of subject matter expert estimates
options.D: Because only verified data can be added to the risk register

correct_answer_text: Because the validity of the analysis results depends on the use of valid data and assumptions, and the application of appropriate analytic methods

question: Per the Common Approaches and Tips, how is the Decision Management process typically applied across the life cycle?

options.A: It is applied only once, at the program initiation decision
options.B: It is applied in conjunction with most of the Technical Management and Technical Processes, since many decisions occur across project management, system definition, and life cycle activities
options.C: It is applied only when a formal trade study is contractually required
options.D: It is applied as a standalone process, independent of other technical and management processes

correct_answer_text: It is applied in conjunction with most of the Technical Management and Technical Processes, since many decisions occur across project management, system definition, and life cycle activities

question: Per Table 2.4, which of the following decision situations is associated with the Production life cycle stage?

options.A: Select system architecture
options.B: Select production process and location
options.C: Perform make-or-buy decision
options.D: Select maintenance approach

correct_answer_text: Select production process and location

question: Per Table 2.4, which decision situation is identified with the Utilization, support life cycle stage?

options.A: Select disposal approach
options.B: Select lower-level elements
options.C: Select maintenance approach
options.D: Conduct analysis of alternatives

correct_answer_text: Select maintenance approach

question: A program is choosing among three candidate system architectures versus a fourth, more novel candidate during the Concept stage. Which Table 2.4 decision situation BEST characterizes this activity?

options.A: Perform make-or-buy decision
options.B: Select verification and validation methods
options.C: Conduct analysis of alternatives (and select system architecture)
options.D: Select disposal approach

correct_answer_text: Conduct analysis of alternatives (and select system architecture)

question: Within whose policy and guidelines is the Decision Management process executed?

options.A: The decision analyst
options.B: The risk manager
options.C: The system sponsor
options.D: The configuration control board

correct_answer_text: The system sponsor

question: Under the "Prepare for decisions" activity grouping, the handbook notes that analyses to provide input for decisions are often performed by which other SE process?

options.A: The Risk Management process
options.B: The Configuration Management process
options.C: The System Analysis process (Section 2.3.5.6)
options.D: The Quality Assurance process

correct_answer_text: The System Analysis process (Section 2.3.5.6)

question: Per the Elaboration on framing, tailoring, and structuring decisions, which set of context elements helps ensure understanding of the decision context?

options.A: Only the decision authority and the budget code
options.B: System baseline, ConOps with system boundaries/interfaces, time frame, explicit stakeholder list, available resources, and expected type of action
options.C: Only the risk register and the configuration baseline
options.D: Only the contractor proposal and the program schedule

correct_answer_text: System baseline, ConOps with system boundaries/interfaces, time frame, explicit stakeholder list, available resources, and expected type of action

question: Per the Elaboration, what alternative to enumerating a finite number of alternatives is cited as effectively and efficiently exploring the trade space?

options.A: Single-point design
options.B: Set-Based Design (SBD)
options.C: Waterfall design
options.D: Monte Carlo simulation

correct_answer_text: Set-Based Design (SBD)

question: Per the Elaboration on assessing alternatives via deterministic analysis, how should the decision team engage subject matter experts?

options.A: By asking each SME to pick the winning alternative based on intuition
options.B: By creating models using operational and test data, the defined objectives, measures, and alternatives, and using structured scoring sheets
options.C: By limiting SME involvement to validating cost numbers only
options.D: By replacing SMEs with stakeholder voting

correct_answer_text: By creating models using operational and test data, the defined objectives, measures, and alternatives, and using structured scoring sheets

question: Per the Elaboration on identifying uncertainty, how can subject matter experts characterize uncertainty in scores for system element performance?

options.A: By providing a single deterministic point estimate
options.B: By making three assessments — assuming low, moderate, and high performance — yielding upper, nominal, and lower bound scores
options.C: By delegating scoring to a configuration control board
options.D: By assuming uncertainty is negligible at the system element level

correct_answer_text: By making three assessments — assuming low, moderate, and high performance — yielding upper, nominal, and lower bound scores

question: Per the Elaboration on assessing impact of uncertainty, which set of techniques does decision analysis use as forms of sensitivity and uncertainty analysis?

options.A: Failure modes and effects analysis only
options.B: Line diagrams, tornado diagrams, waterfall diagrams, Monte Carlo simulation, decision trees, and influence diagrams
options.C: Gantt charts, PERT charts, and burn-down charts
options.D: Pugh matrices and QFD houses of quality

correct_answer_text: Line diagrams, tornado diagrams, waterfall diagrams, Monte Carlo simulation, decision trees, and influence diagrams

question: Per the Elaboration on improving alternatives, why is it NOT considered good practice to end the analysis by simply highlighting the alternative with the highest total value?

options.A: Because the highest-value alternative is usually infeasible
options.B: Because such a premature ending fails to mine the data for opportunities to modify element design choices to reveal untapped value and reduce risk
options.C: Because contracts require selecting the lowest-cost alternative
options.D: Because ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 prohibits ranking by total value

correct_answer_text: Because such a premature ending fails to mine the data for opportunities to modify element design choices to reveal untapped value and reduce risk

question: Per the Elaboration on presenting recommendations, how should the recommendation be framed to increase the likelihood of tangible action?

options.A: As a narrative briefing without specific tasks
options.B: As an actionable task list, with decisions documented using digital engineering artifacts and reports including analysis, decisions, and rationale
options.C: As a single PowerPoint slide containing the winning alternative only
options.D: As a confidential memo to the decision authority only

correct_answer_text: As an actionable task list, with decisions documented using digital engineering artifacts and reports including analysis, decisions, and rationale

question: Per the Elaboration on identifying uncertainty, when significant uncertainty is present during a decision analysis, what action should be taken?

options.A: Ignore the uncertainty since the analysis is deterministic
options.B: Reassign the decision to the Configuration Management process
options.C: Identify risks (linking to the Risk Management process)
options.D: Document the uncertainty only in the final report

correct_answer_text: Identify risks (linking to the Risk Management process)

question: The measures used in MODA models (MOEs, KPPs, MOPs, TPMs, etc.) are discussed further in which other handbook section, indicating cross-process linkage?

options.A: Section 2.3.4.4 Risk Management
options.B: Section 2.3.4.7 Measurement
options.C: Section 2.3.5.6 System Analysis
options.D: Section 2.1.4 Technical Reviews and Audits

correct_answer_text: Section 2.3.4.7 Measurement

question: Categorize each item in the Decision Management Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.26).
Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 as cited in the SE Handbook, what is the purpose of the Risk Management process?

options.A: To eliminate all risks from the system life cycle before development begins
options.B: To identify, analyze, treat, and monitor the risks continually
options.C: To transfer all project risks to the supplier through contractual mechanisms
options.D: To document risks after they have materialized as issues for lessons learned

correct_answer_text: To identify, analyze, treat, and monitor the risks continually

question: The SE Handbook describes Risk Management as a disciplined approach to dealing with what fundamental phenomenon?

options.A: The uncertainty that is present throughout the entire system life cycle
options.B: The technical complexity introduced by systems-of-systems integration
options.C: The cost overruns that historically affect large defense programs
options.D: The schedule pressure imposed by external regulatory deadlines

correct_answer_text: The uncertainty that is present throughout the entire system life cycle

question: According to the Risk Management process description, what is a primary objective with respect to opportunity management?

options.A: To eliminate any opportunity that would distract from the baseline plan
options.B: To identify and manage uncertainties that threaten the value provided by the organization
options.C: To identify and manage uncertainties that enhance or increase the value provided by the organization
options.D: To transfer all opportunities to supplier organizations for execution

correct_answer_text: To identify and manage uncertainties that enhance or increase the value provided by the organization

question: Because risk cannot be reduced to zero, what additional objective does the SE Handbook identify for risk and opportunity management?

options.A: To document residual risk for legal indemnification purposes only
options.B: To achieve a proper balance between risk and opportunity
options.C: To maximize opportunity regardless of associated risk exposure
options.D: To assign all residual risk to the customer at contract award

correct_answer_text: To achieve a proper balance between risk and opportunity

question: Per the IPO diagram (Figure 2.27) for the Risk Management process, which item is identified as a typical INPUT?

options.A: Risk management strategy/approach
options.B: Risk register
options.C: Candidate risks and opportunities
options.D: Risk management report

correct_answer_text: Candidate risks and opportunities

question: Per the IPO diagram (Figure 2.27) for the Risk Management process, which is a typical OUTPUT of the process?

options.A: Candidate risks and opportunities
options.B: Risk register
options.C: Stakeholder needs
options.D: Configuration baseline

correct_answer_text: Risk register

question: Which of the following is listed in Figure 2.27 as one of the typical activities of the Risk Management process?

options.A: Define system requirements
options.B: Maintain the risk profile
options.C: Establish configuration baselines
options.D: Execute project closeout

correct_answer_text: Maintain the risk profile

question: Under the activity "Plan risk management," the SE Handbook directs the project to develop the risk management strategy/approach and to do what else?

options.A: Capture the Risk Management process context, including risk categories
options.B: Quantitatively assess every identified risk using Monte Carlo simulation
options.C: Issue contractual flow-down of risk to all subcontractors
options.D: Close all risks below the threshold prior to project initiation

correct_answer_text: Capture the Risk Management process context, including risk categories

question: Per the SE Handbook, the risk profile is established and maintained to include the context of the risk and which set of characteristics?

options.A: Vendor cost data, contract type, and acquisition strategy
options.B: Likelihood of occurrence, severity of consequences, risk thresholds, and priority
options.C: System architecture, interface definitions, and verification status
options.D: Staff assignment, training records, and employee morale measures

correct_answer_text: Likelihood of occurrence, severity of consequences, risk thresholds, and priority

question: Under the "Analyze risks" activity, after a risk is estimated for likelihood and consequence, what is the next defined step per the SE Handbook?

options.A: Immediately execute the highest-cost treatment available
options.B: Close the risk in the register once it has been documented
options.C: Use the risk thresholds to evaluate the risk for potential treatment
options.D: Notify external regulatory authorities of the risk

correct_answer_text: Use the risk thresholds to evaluate the risk for potential treatment

question: Under "Treat risks that exceed their risk threshold," which of the following is an explicit step listed by the SE Handbook?

options.A: Re-baseline the system requirements specification
options.B: Establish measures to provide insight into the risk treatment effectiveness
options.C: Re-plan the entire project schedule
options.D: Transfer the risk to the customer via change request

correct_answer_text: Establish measures to provide insight into the risk treatment effectiveness

question: Per the "Monitor risks" activity, what does the SE Handbook direct teams to do throughout the life cycle?

options.A: Monitor only the risks remaining from project initiation
options.B: Monitor for new risks and sources of risk
options.C: Limit monitoring to the risks owned by the prime contractor
options.D: Suspend monitoring once treatment plans are funded

correct_answer_text: Monitor for new risks and sources of risk

question: Per the SE Handbook "common approaches and tips," where is the risk management plan (RMP) tailored?

options.A: In the Quality Assurance process
options.B: In the Configuration Management process
options.C: In the Project Planning process
options.D: In the Acquisition process

correct_answer_text: In the Project Planning process

question: According to the SE Handbook, what factor has been found to have a significant effect on the performance of the Risk Management process?

options.A: The size of the engineering staff and its average years of experience
options.B: The geographical distribution of project teams
options.C: The organization's structure and culture
options.D: The choice of programming language used in implementation

correct_answer_text: The organization's structure and culture

question: Per the SE Handbook, what pattern is offered as a good practice for writing identifiable and clarifiable risk statements?

options.A: "Given <input>, when <action>, then <result>"
options.B: "If <situation>, then <consequence>, for <stakeholder>"
options.C: "Shall <function>, with <performance>, under <condition>"
options.D: "Risk = Likelihood x Consequence"

correct_answer_text: "If <situation>, then <consequence>, for <stakeholder>"

question: What does the SE Handbook say about terms such as "positive risk" and concept models that define opportunity as a subset of risks?

options.A: They are the preferred ISO-mandated terminology
options.B: They are required for compliance with ISO 31000
options.C: Experience has shown they serve only to confuse
options.D: They should be used exclusively in safety-critical industries

correct_answer_text: Experience has shown they serve only to confuse

question: Per the SE Handbook "common approaches and tips," risk management is most successful when which condition is achieved?

options.A: When risks are owned exclusively by a dedicated risk manager
options.B: When risks are reviewed only at decision gates
options.C: When risk-based thinking is embraced and integrated into the culture
options.D: When risks are quantified solely by financial impact

correct_answer_text: When risk-based thinking is embraced and integrated into the culture

question: What does the SE Handbook warn could result from negative feedback toward personnel who identify a potential problem?

options.A: Improved schedule performance through reduced reporting overhead
options.B: Increased external auditor confidence in the program
options.C: Failure to identify and address serious risk-laden situations
options.D: More accurate quantification of consequence ratings

correct_answer_text: Failure to identify and address serious risk-laden situations

question: According to the SE Handbook, why may reliance on quantitative measures of risk be insufficient?

options.A: Because ISO 31000 prohibits quantitative measures of risk
options.B: Because variation in stakeholder perspectives, perceptions, and tolerance levels, plus high uncertainty in available data, can make such reliance insufficient
options.C: Because qualitative measures are always more accurate than quantitative measures
options.D: Because risk thresholds are always defined qualitatively in the RMP

correct_answer_text: Because variation in stakeholder perspectives, perceptions, and tolerance levels, plus high uncertainty in available data, can make such reliance insufficient

question: How does the SE Handbook characterize external risks in project management?

options.A: They are typically over-represented and over-resourced
options.B: They are often neglected in project management
options.C: They are always under the direct control of project participants
options.D: They are excluded from the Risk Management process by ISO/IEC/IEEE 16085

correct_answer_text: They are often neglected in project management

question: The SE Handbook identifies two prominent published definitions of "risk." Which of the following is one of them?

options.A: The variance of project cost performance index over the life cycle
options.B: The probability of system failure during operational deployment
options.C: The effect of uncertainty on objectives
options.D: The expected monetary value of all unfavorable outcomes

correct_answer_text: The effect of uncertainty on objectives

question: The SE Handbook notes the second prominent risk definition — "the combination of the probability of occurrence of harm and the severity of that harm" — is commonly used in which engineering discipline?

options.A: Systems-of-systems engineering
options.B: Software engineering
options.C: Safety engineering
options.D: Manufacturing engineering

correct_answer_text: Safety engineering

question: Per the SE Handbook, what are the two components of the measurement of risk shown in Figure 2.28?

options.A: Cost impact and schedule impact
options.B: The likelihood that an event will occur and the undesirable consequence of the event if it does occur
options.C: The technical complexity and the programmatic uncertainty
options.D: The risk threshold and the risk priority

correct_answer_text: The likelihood that an event will occur and the undesirable consequence of the event if it does occur

question: Regarding the generic consequence/likelihood matrix in Figure 2.28, what important caution does the SE Handbook provide?

options.A: It can be directly applied to any project without modification
options.B: It must be used unchanged to ensure cross-project comparability
options.C: It is conceptual and cannot be applied without careful customization to address the specific project
options.D: It applies only to safety-critical medical systems

correct_answer_text: It is conceptual and cannot be applied without careful customization to address the specific project

question: According to the SE Handbook, how can the Figure 2.28 consequence/likelihood matrix be adapted to represent opportunities?

options.A: By inverting the likelihood scale only
options.B: By using a positive consequence scale, changing the outcome adjective from undesirable to desirable
options.C: By replacing the likelihood scale with a financial value scale
options.D: The matrix cannot be used for opportunities; a separate framework is required

correct_answer_text: By using a positive consequence scale, changing the outcome adjective from undesirable to desirable

question: Per the SE Handbook discussion supporting Figure 2.29, what is the relationship between opportunity management and risk management?

options.A: Opportunity management replaces risk management once a project is mature
options.B: They are essential to — and performed concurrently with — the planning process, but require separate and unique techniques
options.C: They are mutually exclusive activities that cannot be performed on the same project
options.D: Opportunity management is performed only after all risks have been closed

correct_answer_text: They are essential to — and performed concurrently with — the planning process, but require separate and unique techniques

question: Per Figure 2.30 in the SE Handbook, which four categories represent the typical risk interactions illustrated?

options.A: Internal, external, operational, and strategic
options.B: Likelihood, consequence, priority, and threshold
options.C: Technical, cost, schedule, and programmatic
options.D: Acceptance, avoidance, mitigation, and transference

correct_answer_text: Technical, cost, schedule, and programmatic

question: According to the SE Handbook, what does the Risk Management process allow a project to do with respect to negative outcomes?

options.A: Eliminate all negative outcomes from the system life cycle
options.B: Take a proactive, structured approach to anticipate negative outcomes and respond to them before they occur
options.C: Document negative outcomes only after the system enters operation
options.D: Defer response to negative outcomes until the retirement stage

correct_answer_text: Take a proactive, structured approach to anticipate negative outcomes and respond to them before they occur

question: When pursuing a new opportunity, what specific care does the SE Handbook direct project teams to take?

options.A: Document the opportunity in the risk register without further analysis
options.B: Reassign the opportunity to a different organization to avoid conflict of interest
options.C: Consider new and increased risk created as a result of pursuing the opportunity
options.D: Convert the opportunity to a baseline requirement immediately

correct_answer_text: Consider new and increased risk created as a result of pursuing the opportunity

question: Per the SE Handbook, how should risks and opportunities associated with system development be managed within an organization?

options.A: Independently of any organizational strategies, project by project
options.B: In a manner consistent with the organization's overall risk and opportunity management strategies
options.C: Only at the corporate executive level, isolated from project teams
options.D: Solely through the supplier's risk register

correct_answer_text: In a manner consistent with the organization's overall risk and opportunity management strategies

question: Per the SE Handbook discussion of Risk Management and the system life cycle, what does it observe about risks across life cycle stages?

options.A: Risks are identical at every life cycle stage and need not be re-evaluated
options.B: Risks in the concept stage are quite different than the risks in the retirement stage
options.C: Risks become relevant only during the operation stage
options.D: Risks should be considered only within the current stage and never in other stages

correct_answer_text: Risks in the concept stage are quite different than the risks in the retirement stage

question: Which standard does the SE Handbook identify as providing detailed descriptions and application guidance for over 30 risk assessment techniques ranging from brainstorming and checklists to FMEA, FTA, Monte Carlo simulation, and Bayesian methods?

options.A: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288
options.B: ISO/IEC/IEEE 16085
options.C: ISO/IEC 31010
options.D: ISO Guide 73

correct_answer_text: ISO/IEC 31010

question: Per the SE Handbook, which risk assessment technique mentioned in ISO/IEC 31010 explicitly addresses opportunity in its name?

options.A: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA)
options.B: Fault Tree Analysis (FTA)
options.C: Strength, Weakness, Opportunity, and Threat (SWOT) Analysis
options.D: Monte Carlo simulation

correct_answer_text: Strength, Weakness, Opportunity, and Threat (SWOT) Analysis

question: Per the SE Handbook, which variant of SWOT analysis emphasizes the external environment over the internal environment?

options.A: TOWS (Threats, Opportunities, Weaknesses, and Strengths)
options.B: STEEP (Social, Technological, Economic, Environmental, Political)
options.C: PESTLE (Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, Environmental)
options.D: OCTAVE (Operationally Critical Threat, Asset, and Vulnerability Evaluation)

correct_answer_text: TOWS (Threats, Opportunities, Weaknesses, and Strengths)

question: Per the SE Handbook's "Risk Treatment Approaches," which set of four basic approaches is identified for treating risks?

options.A: Identify, analyze, treat, and monitor
options.B: Acceptance, avoidance, control (or mitigation), and transference
options.C: Plan, do, check, and act
options.D: Enhance, exploit, share, and accept

correct_answer_text: Acceptance, avoidance, control (or mitigation), and transference

question: Per the SE Handbook, how is the risk treatment approach "Avoidance" defined?

options.A: Accept the risk and do no more
options.B: Avoid the risk through change of requirements or redesign
options.C: Take actions to reduce the risk by expending budget and/or other resources
options.D: Transfer the risk by agreement with another party

correct_answer_text: Avoid the risk through change of requirements or redesign

question: Per the SE Handbook, how is the risk treatment approach "Control (or Mitigation)" defined?

options.A: Accept the risk and do no more
options.B: Avoid the risk through change of requirements or redesign
options.C: Taking actions to reduce the risk by expending budget and/or other resources to reduce likelihood and/or consequence over time
options.D: Transfer the risk by agreement with another party that it is in their scope to treat

correct_answer_text: Taking actions to reduce the risk by expending budget and/or other resources to reduce likelihood and/or consequence over time

question: Per the SE Handbook, what guidance is given for selecting a party to whom risk is transferred?

options.A: Select the lowest-bidding partner irrespective of capability
options.B: Select a partner with no prior exposure to the risk area
options.C: Look for a partner that has experience in the dedicated risk area
options.D: Select an internal organizational unit rather than an external partner

correct_answer_text: Look for a partner that has experience in the dedicated risk area

question: Per the SE Handbook's description of the Risk Management process, what is the proper way to characterize a "risk" as distinct from a problem that has already materialized?

options.A: A risk is a realized negative outcome that has already affected the project
options.B: A risk is a potential problem characterized by likelihood and consequence that has not yet occurred and may be anticipated and responded to before it occurs
options.C: A risk is any defect found during verification testing
options.D: A risk is a contractual claim filed by a stakeholder against the supplier

correct_answer_text: A risk is a potential problem characterized by likelihood and consequence that has not yet occurred and may be anticipated and responded to before it occurs

question: A project team writes the following statement: "If the supplier's new fabrication process is not qualified by PDR, then unit production cost will exceed the not-to-exceed target, for the program office." Per the SE Handbook, which good practice for risk statements does this exemplify?

options.A: The shall/with/under pattern used for writing requirements
options.B: The if-situation/then-consequence/for-stakeholder pattern
options.C: The given/when/then behavior-driven pattern
options.D: The Risk = Likelihood x Consequence formulation

correct_answer_text: The if-situation/then-consequence/for-stakeholder pattern

question: Per the SE Handbook "Maintain the risk profile" activity, the risk profile is established and maintained to include the context of the risk along with which additional content?

options.A: Only the likelihood and consequence ratings
options.B: Likelihood of occurrence, severity of consequences, risk thresholds, priority, and the risk action requests along with the status of their treatment
options.C: Only the residual risk after treatment is applied
options.D: Only the assignment of each risk to an organizational owner

correct_answer_text: Likelihood of occurrence, severity of consequences, risk thresholds, priority, and the risk action requests along with the status of their treatment

question: A subsystem lead encounters an emerging concern but decides not to log it because previous risk reporters were criticized in management reviews. Per the SE Handbook, what is the likely organizational consequence of this culture?

options.A: Improved schedule performance through reduced reporting overhead
options.B: Higher quantitative accuracy of consequence ratings
options.C: Failure to identify and address serious risk-laden situations because engaged stakeholders will not fully cooperate
options.D: Better alignment with ISO/IEC/IEEE 16085 requirements

correct_answer_text: Failure to identify and address serious risk-laden situations because engaged stakeholders will not fully cooperate

question: Per the SE Handbook, who is responsible for identifying risks early and continuously throughout the project life cycle?

options.A: Only the project risk manager
options.B: Only the program-level risk review board
options.C: All personnel
options.D: Only the customer's representative

correct_answer_text: All personnel

question: A project decides to relocate a risky in-house silicon fabrication step to a specialized foundry that already routinely performs that step. Per the SE Handbook's four basic approaches to risk treatment, which approach has been chosen?

options.A: Acceptance
options.B: Avoidance
options.C: Control (or Mitigation)
options.D: Transference

correct_answer_text: Transference

question: A project decides to delete an exotic performance requirement and redesign around a proven, lower-performance component, eliminating the technical risk associated with the exotic requirement. Per the SE Handbook, which risk treatment approach is this?

options.A: Acceptance
options.B: Avoidance
options.C: Control (or Mitigation)
options.D: Transference

correct_answer_text: Avoidance

question: A risk falls below the project's treatment threshold, and the team logs it but takes no further action. Per the SE Handbook's four basic risk treatment approaches, this corresponds to which approach?

options.A: Acceptance — accept the risk and do no more
options.B: Avoidance — change requirements or redesign
options.C: Control (or Mitigation) — expend resources to reduce likelihood and/or consequence
options.D: Transference — agreement with another party

correct_answer_text: Acceptance — accept the risk and do no more

question: A project allocates budget to initiate a parallel development of an alternate algorithm so that, if the primary algorithm fails qualification, an alternate is available — thereby reducing the likelihood of an unrecoverable schedule slip. Per the SE Handbook, which risk treatment approach is this?

options.A: Acceptance
options.B: Avoidance
options.C: Control (or Mitigation)
options.D: Transference

correct_answer_text: Control (or Mitigation)

question: Per the SE Handbook discussion of opportunity assessment techniques in ISO/IEC 31010, what does the handbook explicitly note about a comparable published set of techniques for opportunity management?

options.A: ISO/IEC 31010 provides 30+ techniques dedicated specifically to opportunity management
options.B: A comparable set of (published) techniques for opportunity management is not available, although many ISO/IEC 31010 techniques can also be used to assess positive outcomes
options.C: ISO Guide 73 supersedes ISO/IEC 31010 for opportunity assessment
options.D: Opportunity assessment is excluded from ISO/IEC 31010 and ISO/IEC/IEEE 16085

correct_answer_text: A comparable set of (published) techniques for opportunity management is not available, although many ISO/IEC 31010 techniques can also be used to assess positive outcomes

question: Per the SE Handbook, Fault Tree Analysis (FTA) from ISO/IEC 31010 can be repurposed for opportunity analysis as what variant?

options.A: Bayesian Tree Analysis
options.B: Success Tree Analysis
options.C: Opportunity Mode and Effects Analysis (OMEA)
options.D: Strength Tree Analysis

correct_answer_text: Success Tree Analysis

question: Per the SE Handbook, what is the stated objective with respect to the allocation of resources in risk management?

options.A: Maximize the total resources spent on risk treatment regardless of benefit
options.B: Balance the allocation of resources such that a minimum amount of resources achieves the greatest risk mitigation (or opportunity realization) benefits
options.C: Apply equal resources to every identified risk irrespective of priority
options.D: Transfer the entire risk management budget to the supplier

correct_answer_text: Balance the allocation of resources such that a minimum amount of resources achieves the greatest risk mitigation (or opportunity realization) benefits

question: Per the SE Handbook, low-likelihood/high-severity risks (the type highlighted by Taleb and Siegel) may require what departure from typical practice?

options.A: They should always be accepted because their likelihood is low
options.B: They may require treatment and monitoring regardless of the estimated likelihood of occurrence
options.C: They should be transferred to suppliers exclusively because of severity
options.D: They should be excluded from the risk register because they are statistically improbable

correct_answer_text: They may require treatment and monitoring regardless of the estimated likelihood of occurrence

question: Per the SE Handbook, for a credible risk after analysis, what document identifies the treatment strategy, the trigger points for action, and information ensuring effective execution — and may itself be part of the risk record on the risk profile?

options.A: Risk Management Plan (RMP)
options.B: Risk Treatment Plan
options.C: Risk Register
options.D: Configuration Management Plan

correct_answer_text: Risk Treatment Plan

question: Per the SE Handbook, for risks that have significant consequences, what additional plan should be created and what should it specify?

options.A: A configuration management plan specifying baseline change procedures
options.B: A contingency plan that should include the triggers for enacting it in case the risk treatment is not successful
options.C: A verification plan specifying acceptance test procedures
options.D: A stakeholder engagement plan specifying communication cadence

correct_answer_text: A contingency plan that should include the triggers for enacting it in case the risk treatment is not successful

question: Per the SE Handbook "Risk Monitoring" discussion, what do measures used in the Risk Management process help to do?

options.A: Eliminate the need for a risk profile
options.B: Identify new risks as well as provide insight into the effectiveness of the risk treatments
options.C: Replace the risk thresholds defined in the RMP
options.D: Authorize closure of risks before treatment is executed

correct_answer_text: Identify new risks as well as provide insight into the effectiveness of the risk treatments

question: Per the SE Handbook, what is the typical format for tracking technical performance as an indicator of technical risk?

options.A: A heat map of cost variance vs. schedule variance
options.B: A graph of a planned value of a key parameter plotted against calendar time, with a second contour showing the actual value achieved
options.C: A Monte Carlo histogram of likelihood ratings
options.D: A fault tree with cut-set probabilities

correct_answer_text: A graph of a planned value of a key parameter plotted against calendar time, with a second contour showing the actual value achieved

question: Per the SE Handbook discussion of Risk Management and the system life cycle, what is often necessary regarding risks in stages other than the current one?

options.A: Risks in other stages should be ignored until that stage is reached
options.B: It is often necessary to consider risks in other stages while performing activities in the current stage
options.C: Risks may only be reviewed at the system retirement stage
options.D: Risks in other stages are owned solely by external regulators

correct_answer_text: It is often necessary to consider risks in other stages while performing activities in the current stage

question: Per the SE Handbook "common approaches and tips," how should the project handle the risk terminology used by the team?

options.A: Adopt whichever terminology each contributor prefers to encourage diversity
options.B: Define and communicate the risk terminology to be used by the project and include it in the project's risk management plan (RMP)
options.C: Defer terminology definition to the supplier risk register
options.D: Use only the terminology in ISO Guide 73, never the ISO/IEC/IEEE 16085 terms

correct_answer_text: Define and communicate the risk terminology to be used by the project and include it in the project's risk management plan (RMP)

question: Per the SE Handbook, an organization should manage risks and opportunities associated with system development in what relation to organization-level strategies?

options.A: Independently of any organizational strategy, project by project
options.B: In a manner consistent with the organization's overall risk and opportunity management strategies, integrated with all organizational processes
options.C: Only after the organization's strategic plan has been retired
options.D: Solely through the supplier's risk register, isolated from the parent organization

correct_answer_text: In a manner consistent with the organization's overall risk and opportunity management strategies, integrated with all organizational processes

question: Categorize each item in the Risk Management Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.27).

items.text: Treat risks that exceed their risk threshold

question: From Figure 2.30, drag each risk scenario to its risk category.

items.text: Heat dissipation requirement may not be achievable with the current cooling design
Subsystem mass exceeds the allocated mass budget, threatening performance margins
New composite material has unproven reliability at required operating temperatures
Software algorithm may fail to meet real-time latency requirements under peak load
Raw material commodity price volatility may push procurement above budget
Estimated labor hours for integration and test underestimated by vendor proposal
Currency exchange rate fluctuation increases cost of foreign-sourced components
Rework from failed qualification testing drives unplanned cost growth
Long-lead supplier component delivery may slip beyond planned need date
Limited test facility availability could delay environmental qualification testing
Concurrent design iterations may extend integration phase beyond baseline
Critical-path subcontractor staffing shortages threaten milestone completion
Regulatory or certification approval timeline may extend beyond contract award date
Customer mission changes introduce new requirements after baseline approval
Loss of key program funding due to shifting stakeholder priorities
Export control or ITAR restrictions limit access to required foreign partners

question: According to ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 as cited in the Handbook, what is the purpose of the Configuration Management (CM) process?

options.A: To generate, obtain, confirm, transform, retain, retrieve, and dispose of information to designated stakeholders
options.B: To manage system and system element configurations over their life cycle
options.C: To establish a framework for assessing risks and opportunities throughout the life cycle
options.D: To plan, assess, and control the project's technical and management activities

correct_answer_text: To manage system and system element configurations over their life cycle

question: The Handbook describes Configuration Management as which type of process?

options.A: A Technical Process
options.B: An Agreement Process
options.C: A Technical Management Process
options.D: An Organizational Project-Enabling Process

correct_answer_text: A Technical Management Process

question: Per the Handbook, what does Configuration Management establish and maintain regarding a product's configuration?

options.A: Consistency, integrity, traceability, and control
options.B: Cost, schedule, scope, and quality
options.C: Verification, validation, audit, and review
options.D: Availability, reliability, maintainability, and safety

correct_answer_text: Consistency, integrity, traceability, and control

question: According to the Handbook, CM provides which combination of enduring qualities across the full life cycle of the product?

options.A: Speed, agility, and innovation
options.B: Truth, trust, and traceability
options.C: Performance, cost, and schedule
options.D: Quality, reliability, and maintainability

correct_answer_text: Truth, trust, and traceability

question: Per the Handbook's CM description (referencing SAE-EIA 649C), CM applies processes, resources, and controls to establish and maintain consistency between which two items?

options.A: The product baseline and the configuration audit report
options.B: The functional baseline and the allocated baseline
options.C: The product configuration information and the product
options.D: The Configuration Items and the Change Control Board

correct_answer_text: The product configuration information and the product

question: According to Figure 2.31 (IPO diagram for the CM process), which of the following is identified as a typical INPUT to the CM process?

options.A: Configuration management strategy/approach
options.B: Configuration baseline
options.C: Candidate items for configuration management
options.D: Configuration verification and audit report

correct_answer_text: Candidate items for configuration management

question: Per Figure 2.31, which of the following is listed as a typical OUTPUT of the Configuration Management process?

options.A: Candidate items for configuration management
options.B: Variance/deviation/waiver request
options.C: Risk profile
options.D: Project management plan

correct_answer_text: Variance/deviation/waiver request

question: Per Figure 2.31, a 'change request' appears in which portion(s) of the CM IPO diagram?

options.A: Inputs only
options.B: Outputs only
options.C: Both inputs and outputs
options.D: Activities only

correct_answer_text: Both inputs and outputs

question: Which of the following is NOT one of the five CM process activities listed in the Handbook (Figure 2.31)?

options.A: Prepare for configuration management
options.B: Perform configuration identification
options.C: Perform configuration change management
options.D: Perform configuration retirement

correct_answer_text: Perform configuration retirement

question: According to the Handbook, the result of CM planning could be any of the following EXCEPT:

options.A: A standalone configuration management strategy
options.B: A section incorporated within the SEMP
options.C: Part of the digital implementation throughout the development platform
options.D: A standalone Risk Treatment Plan owned by the CCB

correct_answer_text: A standalone Risk Treatment Plan owned by the CCB

question: Per the Handbook, which activity establishes unique identifiers for items and data under configuration management and structures those items?

options.A: Perform configuration identification
options.B: Perform configuration change management
options.C: Perform configuration status accounting
options.D: Perform configuration verification and audit

correct_answer_text: Perform configuration identification

question: According to the Handbook, baselines are established as part of which CM activity, and at what events?

options.A: Configuration change management; only at Critical Design Review
options.B: Configuration identification; at appropriate points throughout the life cycle, often coinciding with a project milestone or decision gate
options.C: Configuration verification and audit; only at FCA and PCA
options.D: Prepare for CM; only during planning before any technical work begins

correct_answer_text: Configuration identification; at appropriate points throughout the life cycle, often coinciding with a project milestone or decision gate

question: Per the Handbook, Configuration Items (CIs) are designated by which CM activity?

options.A: Configuration change management, after a CCB approves a change
options.B: Configuration status accounting, when As-Built data is reconciled
options.C: Configuration identification, where distinct configuration control (governance) is required
options.D: Configuration verification and audit, after PCA completion

correct_answer_text: Configuration identification, where distinct configuration control (governance) is required

question: According to the Handbook, the disposition of changes and variances is often performed by what type of body?

options.A: Functional Configuration Audit (FCA) teams
options.B: Change boards (e.g., Configuration Control Board [CCB], Configuration Review Board [CRB])
options.C: Project Assessment and Control panels
options.D: Independent Verification & Validation (IV&V) agents

correct_answer_text: Change boards (e.g., Configuration Control Board [CCB], Configuration Review Board [CRB])

question: Per the Handbook, configuration change management includes which of the following activities for requests for change/variances?

options.A: Only identification and approval/disapproval
options.B: Only impact analysis and implementation
options.C: Identification, recording, review (incl. impact analysis), approve/disapprove, tracking, processing, implementing, and closing
options.D: Establishing unique identifiers and validating release of items

correct_answer_text: Identification, recording, review (incl. impact analysis), approve/disapprove, tracking, processing, implementing, and closing

question: Which CM activity is responsible for communicating and maintaining the status of controlled events, items, and data across the life cycle to stakeholders?

options.A: Configuration identification
options.B: Configuration change management
options.C: Configuration status accounting
options.D: Configuration verification and audit

correct_answer_text: Configuration status accounting

question: Per the Handbook, an example given of configuration status accounting supporting Project Assessment and Control metrics is:

options.A: Convening the CCB to disposition ECPs
options.B: Performing reconciliation of the As-Designed data with the As-Built data
options.C: Conducting the functional configuration audit (FCA)
options.D: Tagging specific commits to establish baselines

correct_answer_text: Performing reconciliation of the As-Designed data with the As-Built data

question: According to the Handbook, configuration verification is performed in conjunction with which other process(es)?

options.A: Risk Management and Decision Management
options.B: Project Planning and Project Assessment and Control
options.C: Verification and Quality Assurance
options.D: Information Management and Measurement

correct_answer_text: Verification and Quality Assurance

question: Per the Handbook, auditing at events like a functional configuration audit (FCA) or a physical configuration audit (PCA) is often accomplished:

options.A: At the beginning of the concept stage
options.B: At the end of the development effort and/or testing
options.C: Only during the retirement stage
options.D: Continuously and only by the acquirer's supply chain

correct_answer_text: At the end of the development effort and/or testing

question: Per the Handbook, what role does auditing play in support of configuration verification?

options.A: Auditing replaces verification once the product baseline is approved
options.B: Auditing validates traceability and status between the product to its design, product design to its requirements, and the implementation of changes
options.C: Auditing establishes the unique identifiers used by configuration identification
options.D: Auditing dispositions engineering change proposals on behalf of the CCB

correct_answer_text: Auditing validates traceability and status between the product to its design, product design to its requirements, and the implementation of changes

question: According to the Handbook's 'Common approaches and tips,' when should the Configuration Management process begin and end?

options.A: Begin at System Requirements Review and end at PDR
options.B: Begin at CDR and end at acceptance testing
options.C: Begin at the beginning of the system life cycle and continue through until retirement of the system
options.D: Begin at first production delivery and continue through utilization only

correct_answer_text: Begin at the beginning of the system life cycle and continue through until retirement of the system

question: Per the Handbook elaboration, additional CM guidance can be found in which set of current standards?

options.A: ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and ISO 27001
options.B: SAE-EIA 649C, ISO 10007, and IEEE 828
options.D: MIL-STD-882, NIST 800-30, and ANSI/EIA-632

correct_answer_text: SAE-EIA 649C, ISO 10007, and IEEE 828

question: Per the Handbook elaboration, which agile SWE practices are highlighted as well-established CM techniques now used more broadly (including MBSE)?

options.A: Establishing baselines by tagging specific commits, managing revisions as a stream of commits, and using branching/merging for concurrent changes
options.B: Using only manual review boards to disposition all changes
options.C: Replacing FCA and PCA with informal peer reviews
options.D: Eliminating CIs and baselines in favor of continuous, untracked iteration

correct_answer_text: Establishing baselines by tagging specific commits, managing revisions as a stream of commits, and using branching/merging for concurrent changes

question: According to the Handbook, what is the role of the 'digital thread' relative to CM?

options.A: It replaces configuration baselines with continuous integration pipelines
options.B: It establishes communication paths between individually configured domains and ties together the appropriate configurations to form a consistent configuration for a specific system/product
options.C: It is solely a cybersecurity construct for monitoring software vulnerabilities
options.D: It is the formal mechanism by which CCBs approve engineering change proposals

correct_answer_text: It establishes communication paths between individually configured domains and ties together the appropriate configurations to form a consistent configuration for a specific system/product

question: Per the Handbook, configuration management helps ensure all of the following EXCEPT:

options.A: Product functional, performance, and physical characteristics are properly identified, documented, controlled, validated, and verified to establish product integrity
options.B: Changes to product characteristics are properly identified, reviewed, approved, documented, and implemented
options.C: Products produced against a given set of data are known, verified, and validated
options.D: Project cost and schedule variances are minimized through earned value management

correct_answer_text: Project cost and schedule variances are minimized through earned value management

question: Per the Handbook, which CM activity manages changes and variances (non-conformances) throughout the system life cycle to ensure product/system integrity?

options.A: Perform configuration identification
options.B: Perform configuration change management
options.C: Perform configuration status accounting
options.D: Prepare for configuration management

correct_answer_text: Perform configuration change management

question: Per the Handbook's 'Prepare for configuration management' activity, when in the product life cycle should configuration management be planned?

options.A: Only after the functional baseline has been approved
options.B: As early as possible in the product life cycle
options.C: At Critical Design Review, when CIs are first identified
options.D: Only after the first engineering change proposal is submitted

correct_answer_text: As early as possible in the product life cycle

question: Per the Handbook, which of the following is explicitly listed as a sub-element of planning and managing configuration management under 'Prepare for configuration management'?

options.A: Convening the Change Control Board (CCB) to disposition ECPs
options.B: Conducting the Functional Configuration Audit (FCA) and Physical Configuration Audit (PCA)
options.C: Supplier configuration management
options.D: Reconciling the As-Designed data with the As-Built data

correct_answer_text: Supplier configuration management

question: Per the Handbook, which of the following is NOT one of the listed sub-elements of 'Prepare for configuration management'?

options.A: Applying adequate configuration management resources and assigning responsibility
options.B: Establishing performance and status measurements
options.C: Configuration management training
options.D: Designating Configuration Items (CIs) where distinct configuration control is required

correct_answer_text: Designating Configuration Items (CIs) where distinct configuration control is required

question: According to the Handbook, the 'product configuration information processes' established during CM planning include collection and processing, controlling status, providing inter-operability and exchange, and which other element?

options.A: Long-term preservation
options.B: Earned value management
options.C: Risk burndown reporting
options.D: Knowledge management taxonomy

correct_answer_text: Long-term preservation

question: Per the Handbook, an important practice for the change/variance process within configuration change management is to:

options.A: Approve all changes automatically once impact analysis is complete
options.B: Track and manage implementation activities and close the loop ensuring that both the product and its associated information have been evolved to the current approved configuration
options.C: Limit change boards to software-only configuration items
options.D: Avoid recording disapproved changes to keep the configuration record clean

correct_answer_text: Track and manage implementation activities and close the loop ensuring that both the product and its associated information have been evolved to the current approved configuration

question: Per the Handbook, configuration verification includes all of the following EXCEPT:

options.A: Review of CM processes
options.B: Verifying that the system has achieved specified requirements and that the design is accurately and completely documented in configuration information
options.C: Verifying that physical, functional, and interface requirements defined in the approved product definition information are achieved by the product
options.D: Approving engineering change proposals on behalf of the Configuration Control Board

correct_answer_text: Approving engineering change proposals on behalf of the Configuration Control Board

question: According to the Handbook, the acquirer may have the requirement or wish to perform which CM-related activity in the supply chain?

options.A: Surveillance and, where necessary, audits to ensure the correct application of CM processes in their supply chain
options.B: Direct ownership of the supplier's Configuration Control Board
options.C: Re-baselining supplier CIs at every project milestone
options.D: Replacement of the supplier's CM strategy with the acquirer's SEMP

correct_answer_text: Surveillance and, where necessary, audits to ensure the correct application of CM processes in their supply chain

question: Per the Handbook's 'Common approaches and tips,' what is described as 'key for the successful application' of CM across various domains?

options.A: Centralizing all CM authority within a single enterprise-level CCB
options.B: Tailoring of the configuration management approach, including appropriate understanding of the information and processes needed to fulfill CM requirements
options.C: Mandating use of a single commercial CM tool across the supply chain
options.D: Deferring CM activities until the product baseline is established

correct_answer_text: Tailoring of the configuration management approach, including appropriate understanding of the information and processes needed to fulfill CM requirements

question: According to the Handbook's elaboration, CM must account for horizontal and vertical integration in addition to other factors that can affect the system definition over time. This is because:

options.A: Change is a fundamental characteristic of every large-scale system during its life cycle, making it imperative to understand the impact of change across all interacting elements
options.B: Horizontal and vertical integration eliminate the need for change boards
options.C: CM is only applicable once the system has been horizontally integrated
options.D: Integration mandates that all CIs use the same software platform

correct_answer_text: Change is a fundamental characteristic of every large-scale system during its life cycle, making it imperative to understand the impact of change across all interacting elements

question: Per the Handbook, in Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE), what is the most challenging aspect that CM must address?

options.A: Eliminating change boards entirely in favor of automated merges
options.B: Maintaining the relationships between the appropriate configurations of each domain while ensuring accountability and consistency
options.C: Replacing baselines with continuous-delivery release tags
options.D: Limiting CM to the software domain only

correct_answer_text: Maintaining the relationships between the appropriate configurations of each domain while ensuring accountability and consistency

question: Per the Handbook elaboration, which agile SWE CM practice specifically addresses concurrent changes?

options.A: Managing revisions as a stream of commits
options.B: Tagging specific commits to establish baselines
options.C: Branching and merging
options.D: Automating testing through CI/CD

correct_answer_text: Branching and merging

question: Per the Handbook elaboration, automated testing, evaluation, and/or deployment in agile SWE CM practices is accomplished through:

options.A: Manual CCB review of each commit
options.B: A Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) process
options.C: The Functional Configuration Audit (FCA) at end of development
options.D: The Physical Configuration Audit (PCA) before deployment

correct_answer_text: A Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) process

question: Per the Handbook elaboration, security in the agile SWE CM practices referenced is ensured by:

options.A: Manual penetration testing only at PCA
options.B: The DevSecOps life cycle by integrating security tools into DevOps
options.C: Encrypting only the As-Built configuration data
options.D: Delegating security entirely to the acquirer's supply chain audits

correct_answer_text: The DevSecOps life cycle by integrating security tools into DevOps

question: Per the Handbook, although cyber security is traditionally thought of as a software engineering problem, proper configuration management needs to also include:

options.A: Replacement of CCBs with security incident response teams
options.B: Continuous auditing of potential cyber vulnerabilities
options.C: Quarantining all hardware CIs from configuration control
options.D: Excluding system interfaces from baseline definitions

correct_answer_text: Continuous auditing of potential cyber vulnerabilities

question: Categorize each item in the Configuration Management Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.31).
Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 [6.3.6.1], what is the purpose of the Information Management process?

options.A: To collect, analyze, and report objective data and information to support effective management and address information needs about products, services, and processes
options.B: To generate, obtain, confirm, transform, retain, retrieve, disseminate, and dispose of information to designated stakeholders
options.C: To establish baselines, control changes, and maintain configuration item integrity throughout the system life cycle
options.D: To capture, retain, and share organizational expertise so that lessons learned are preserved and reused across projects

correct_answer_text: To generate, obtain, confirm, transform, retain, retrieve, disseminate, and dispose of information to designated stakeholders

question: The Handbook lists six qualities the Information Management process plans, executes, and controls to provide information that is unambiguous, complete, verifiable, consistent, traceable, and which other quality?

options.A: Profitable
options.B: Auditable
options.C: Presentable
options.D: Reusable

correct_answer_text: Presentable

question: A program manager must distinguish between Information Management and Knowledge Management. Which statement BEST captures the distinction described in the Handbook?

options.A: Information Management retains organizational expertise across projects, while Knowledge Management handles only project-level documents and records
options.B: Information Management manages defined project information (technical, project, contractual, user, etc.), while Knowledge Management is motivated by information sharing across the organization and the sharing of knowledge and skills throughout the organization
options.C: Information Management controls technical baselines, while Knowledge Management controls all project artifacts regardless of baseline
options.D: Information Management and Knowledge Management are synonymous and use a single integrated plan

correct_answer_text: Information Management manages defined project information (technical, project, contractual, user, etc.), while Knowledge Management is motivated by information sharing across the organization and the sharing of knowledge and skills throughout the organization

question: The Handbook states that Information Management must be associated very closely with which other process to ensure the integrity, initial release, and change control of information and data?

options.A: Knowledge Management
options.B: Quality Assurance
options.C: Risk Management
options.D: Configuration Management

correct_answer_text: Configuration Management

question: Per Figure 2.32 (IPO diagram), which pair represents typical INPUTS to the Information Management process?

options.A: Candidate items for information management; change request
options.B: Information register; information management records/artifacts
options.C: Information management strategy/approach; information management report
options.D: Project plan; configuration management plan

correct_answer_text: Candidate items for information management; change request

question: Per Figure 2.32, which of the following is NOT identified as a typical OUTPUT of the Information Management process?

options.A: Information management strategy/approach
options.B: Information register
options.C: Candidate items for information management
options.D: Information management report

correct_answer_text: Candidate items for information management

question: Per Figure 2.32, the Information Management process activities are grouped into which two top-level activities?

options.A: Plan for Information Management; Audit Information Management
options.B: Prepare for Information Management; Perform Information Management
options.C: Define Information Architecture; Distribute Information
options.D: Capture Information; Dispose of Information

correct_answer_text: Prepare for Information Management; Perform Information Management

question: Under the 'Prepare for Information Management' activity, identifying valid sources of information and designating authorities and responsibilities for origination, generation, capture, release, archival, and disposal must be done in accordance with which process?

options.A: Risk Management Process
options.B: Quality Management Process
options.C: Configuration Management Process
options.D: Knowledge Management Process

correct_answer_text: Configuration Management Process

question: Under 'Perform Information Management,' the Handbook states that designated information shall be archived for compliance with which set of requirements?

options.A: Marketing, financial, and operational requirements
options.B: Legal, audit, knowledge retention, and project closure requirements
options.C: Security, safety, and reliability requirements only
