options.D: Stakeholder, customer, and end-user requirements

correct_answer_text: Legal, audit, knowledge retention, and project closure requirements

question: The Handbook defines 'information assets' in the Information Management context as which of the following?

options.A: Only the digital files stored in a configuration-controlled repository
options.B: Intangible information and any tangible form of its representation, including drawings, models, specifications, memos, email, computer files, and databases
options.C: Only intellectual property protected by copyright, trademark, or patent
options.D: The organizational knowledge retained by employees as tacit expertise

correct_answer_text: Intangible information and any tangible form of its representation, including drawings, models, specifications, memos, email, computer files, and databases

question: According to the Handbook, what does 'information security' generally refer to?

options.A: The encryption of all project data at rest and in transit
options.B: The set of access controls and user authentication mechanisms within an organization
options.C: The protection, confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the information assets
options.D: The classification of information as proprietary, export-restricted, or unclassified

correct_answer_text: The protection, confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the information assets

question: The Handbook notes that the historical knowledge mechanisms for the decision-making, risk, and configuration management processes are under whose responsibility?

options.A: Configuration management working in concert with information management
options.B: Knowledge management working independently of configuration management
options.C: Project planning, exclusively
options.D: Quality assurance, working with the project management office

correct_answer_text: Configuration management working in concert with information management

question: A systems engineer must distinguish Information Management, Knowledge Management, and Configuration Management. Which statement BEST captures the three-way distinction as presented in the Handbook?

options.A: Information Management manages defined project information; Knowledge Management is motivated by information sharing across the organization and sharing of knowledge and skills; Configuration Management ensures integrity, initial release, and change control of the information and data
options.B: Information Management controls technical baselines; Knowledge Management manages defined project information; Configuration Management archives organizational expertise
options.C: All three are synonymous and produce a single integrated plan covering project artifacts, baselines, and tacit knowledge
options.D: Information Management is a subset of Knowledge Management, which is in turn a subset of Configuration Management

correct_answer_text: Information Management manages defined project information; Knowledge Management is motivated by information sharing across the organization and sharing of knowledge and skills; Configuration Management ensures integrity, initial release, and change control of the information and data

question: Per the Handbook's definition list, which statement BEST describes 'Information' (as distinct from 'information assets')?

options.A: Only digital files held in a configuration-controlled repository
options.B: What an organization has compiled or its employees know, which can be stored and communicated, and may include classified or unclassified, export-restrictive, proprietary, and protected or unprotected intellectual property
options.C: The tangible representations of organizational data, such as drawings, models, memos, and databases
options.D: The set of policies, practices, procedures, and software used to protect organizational data

correct_answer_text: What an organization has compiled or its employees know, which can be stored and communicated, and may include classified or unclassified, export-restrictive, proprietary, and protected or unprotected intellectual property

question: Which Handbook term refers specifically to the life-cycle approach to implementing, maintaining, and improving the interrelated set of policies, controls, and procedures that ensure the security of an organization's information assets?

options.A: Information security
options.B: Information security management
options.C: Information Security Management System
options.D: Configuration management

correct_answer_text: Information Security Management System

question: Per the Handbook, 'information security management' is BEST described as which of the following?

options.A: The protection, confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the information assets themselves
options.B: The controls used to achieve information security, accomplished by implementing a suitable set of formalized controls such as policies, practices, procedures, organizational structures, and software
options.C: The life-cycle approach used to implement, maintain, and improve interrelated policies, controls, and procedures
options.D: The intangible information and any tangible form of its representation

correct_answer_text: The controls used to achieve information security, accomplished by implementing a suitable set of formalized controls such as policies, practices, procedures, organizational structures, and software

question: Under 'Prepare for Information Management,' which activity supports establishing and maintaining a single, project-wide reference for system-relevant data terminology and definitions?

options.A: Establishing and maintaining a system data dictionary
options.B: Defining the base measures, derived measures, and indicators
options.C: Maintaining the information register and information management report
options.D: Archiving designated information for compliance with legal and audit requirements

correct_answer_text: Establishing and maintaining a system data dictionary

question: Under 'Prepare for Information Management,' which of the following sub-activities is explicitly listed in the Handbook?

options.A: Periodically obtain or transform artifacts of information at each increment or sprint
options.B: Retrieve and distribute information to designated parties according to agreed schedules
options.C: Define formats and media for capture, retention, transmission, and retrieval of information
options.D: Dispose of unwanted, invalid, or unverifiable information according to organizational policy

correct_answer_text: Define formats and media for capture, retention, transmission, and retrieval of information

question: Under 'Perform Information Management,' the Handbook states that information shall be maintained according to which set of requirements?

options.A: Legal, audit, knowledge retention, and project closure requirements
options.B: Integrity, security, and privacy requirements
options.C: Marketing, financial, and operational requirements
options.D: Origination, generation, capture, release, archival, and disposal requirements

correct_answer_text: Integrity, security, and privacy requirements

question: Per the Handbook, disposing of unwanted, invalid, or unverifiable information must be done according to which combination of requirements?

options.A: Organizational policy, security, privacy, and legal requirements applicable to the data
options.B: Stakeholder, customer, and end-user requirements only
options.C: Knowledge management and lessons-learned requirements only
options.D: Reliability, maintainability, and availability requirements

correct_answer_text: Organizational policy, security, privacy, and legal requirements applicable to the data

question: Per Figure 2.32, which item appears as BOTH a typical input and a typical output of the Information Management process?

options.A: Information register
options.B: Information management strategy/approach
options.C: Change request
options.D: Information management records/artifacts

correct_answer_text: Change request

question: A project is closing out and the SE lead must hand off information assets so that lessons learned and expertise can be reused on future projects. According to the Handbook, this organizational sharing of information and the sharing of knowledge and skills throughout the organization is primarily the concern of which process?

options.A: Configuration Management
options.B: Quality Management
options.C: Knowledge Management
options.D: Risk Management

correct_answer_text: Knowledge Management

question: Per the Handbook's 'common approaches and tips,' a design engineer's notebook (in any media or format) is an example of which recommended practice?

options.A: An information register output of Configuration Management
options.B: An information-rich artifact that should be identified and stored for later use even if the information is informal
options.C: A measurement record/artifact captured under the Measurement process
options.D: An information asset that must be disposed of at project closure

correct_answer_text: An information-rich artifact that should be identified and stored for later use even if the information is informal

question: Per the Handbook, in which process is the information management plan tailored to the project, and with which other plan should it align?

options.A: Tailored in the Risk Management process and aligned with the Quality Management Plan
options.B: Tailored in the Measurement process and aligned with the Project Assessment and Control Plan
options.C: Tailored in the Project Planning process and aligned with the Configuration Management Plan
options.D: Tailored in the Knowledge Management process and aligned with the Decision Management Plan

correct_answer_text: Tailored in the Project Planning process and aligned with the Configuration Management Plan

question: Per the Handbook, the information management plan identifies the system-relevant information to be collected, retained, controlled, secured, and disseminated, together with which additional element?

options.A: A schedule for disposal
options.B: A list of measures of effectiveness (MOEs)
options.C: A risk register and risk-treatment options
options.D: A configuration item baseline and change-request log

correct_answer_text: A schedule for disposal

question: Per the Handbook, identifying the information set at the start of a project is specifically recommended when following which engineering approach?

options.A: A vee-model approach
options.B: A digital engineering approach
options.C: An agile-only approach
options.D: A waterfall approach

correct_answer_text: A digital engineering approach

question: Categorize each item in the Information Management Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.32). Note: 'Change request' appears as both a Typical Input and Typical Output in Figure 2.32; per de-duplication rule it is placed only in Output here.
Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 (and ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939), what is the purpose of the Measurement process?

options.A: To enforce contractual quality penalties when measured values fall outside performance thresholds
options.B: To collect, analyze, and report objective data and information to support effective management and address information needs about the products, services, and processes
options.C: To audit organizational processes against ISO 9001 quality standards and report non-conformances
options.D: To establish base measures and derived measures used exclusively for software engineering progress tracking

correct_answer_text: To collect, analyze, and report objective data and information to support effective management and address information needs about the products, services, and processes

question: According to the handbook, what is the key SE measurement objective?

options.A: To produce monthly earned value management reports for the project sponsor
options.B: To collect every available data point so that historical analysis can be performed later if needed
options.C: To assess the SE processes and work products with respect to project and organization needs, including timeliness, meeting performance requirements and quality characteristics, product conformance to standards, effective use of resources, and continuous process improvement in reducing cost and cycle time
options.D: To ensure that the Quality Assurance process has sufficient evidence to certify final product release

correct_answer_text: To assess the SE processes and work products with respect to project and organization needs, including timeliness, meeting performance requirements and quality characteristics, product conformance to standards, effective use of resources, and continuous process improvement in reducing cost and cycle time

question: Per the handbook, what drives the data that should be collected, analyzed, and reported in the Measurement process?

options.A: The data drives the information needs, which in turn drive the decisions to be made
options.B: The decisions to be made drive the information needs, and the information needs drive the data to be collected, analyzed, and reported
options.C: The measurement repository capacity drives which data items are practical to collect
options.D: The configuration management plan dictates which data items are baselined and therefore must be measured

correct_answer_text: The decisions to be made drive the information needs, and the information needs drive the data to be collected, analyzed, and reported

question: According to Figure 2.33 (IPO diagram for the Measurement process), which pair are the typical INPUTS to the Measurement process?

options.A: Measurement strategy/approach and measurement report
options.B: Measurement register and measurement records/artifacts
options.C: Measurement needs and measurement data
options.D: Risk register and decision report

correct_answer_text: Measurement needs and measurement data

question: According to Figure 2.33, which of the following is NOT listed as a typical OUTPUT of the Measurement process?

options.A: Measurement strategy/approach
options.B: Measurement register
options.C: Measurement report
options.D: Measurement data

correct_answer_text: Measurement data

question: The Measurement process activities are grouped into two top-level activities. Which pair correctly identifies them?

options.A: Plan measurement and Audit measurement
options.B: Prepare for measurement and Perform measurement
options.C: Define measures and Report measures
options.D: Collect data and Improve processes

correct_answer_text: Prepare for measurement and Perform measurement

question: Under "Prepare for measurement," the handbook says the practitioner should define the base measures, derived measures, indicators, data collection, measurement frequency, measurement repository, reporting method and frequency, and which other two items?

options.A: Trigger points or thresholds, and review authority
options.B: Earned value baseline, and cost-account manager
options.C: Tolerance band shape, and TRL ladder
options.D: Configuration item identification, and change board chair

correct_answer_text: Trigger points or thresholds, and review authority

question: Under "Perform measurement," what does the handbook say should be done with the measurement data?

options.A: Archive the raw data and release it to stakeholders only after final acceptance review
options.B: Gather, process, store, verify, and analyze the data to obtain measurement results (information products)
options.C: Convert the raw data into earned value indices for the project control account manager
options.D: Aggregate the data into a single composite quality score that is reported to executive leadership

correct_answer_text: Gather, process, store, verify, and analyze the data to obtain measurement results (information products)

question: Which of the following is identified in Table 2.5 as a measurement benefit to the Project Manager / Technical Lead?

options.A: Replaces formal risk management with quantitative trend analysis
options.B: Eliminates the need for technical reviews and audits
options.C: Identifies and corrects problems early
options.D: Guarantees on-time delivery once thresholds are met

correct_answer_text: Identifies and corrects problems early

question: Which statement best reflects the handbook's "common approaches and tips" guidance on measurement?

options.A: All data that can be collected automatically should be collected, regardless of intended use, to build a rich historical baseline
options.B: Measurement for measurement's sake is a waste of time and effort; collecting data without an information need and an intended use is not effective use of limited resources
options.C: Each measure should be reviewed only once per life cycle stage, at the exit decision gate
options.D: The frequency of measure review is set by the configuration management plan and is independent of risk

correct_answer_text: Measurement for measurement's sake is a waste of time and effort; collecting data without an information need and an intended use is not effective use of limited resources

question: When an agreement identifies Measures of Effectiveness (MOEs) that must be met, which derived measures does the handbook say are "default measures to be included within the measurement plan"?

options.A: Earned value cost performance index (CPI) and schedule performance index (SPI)
options.B: Leading indicators and lagging indicators for organizational maturity
options.C: Measures of Performance (MOPs) and Technical Performance Measures (TPMs) that provide the necessary insight into meeting the MOEs
options.D: Key Performance Parameters (KPPs) and Technology Readiness Levels (TRLs)

correct_answer_text: Measures of Performance (MOPs) and Technical Performance Measures (TPMs) that provide the necessary insight into meeting the MOEs

question: Per Table 2.6, which document is identified as the "General Reference" for the Measurement process?

options.A: INCOSE Systems Engineering Measurement Primer, Version 2.0 (2010)
options.B: Practical Software and Systems Measurement (PSM) Guide V4.0c (2003)
options.C: Systems and Software Engineering – Measurement Process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939, 2017)
options.D: CMMI for Development V2.0, Measurement and Quantitative Management Process Areas (2018)

correct_answer_text: Systems and Software Engineering – Measurement Process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939, 2017)

question: Per Table 2.6, which reference is identified as the focus for "Technical Measurement / Performance"?

options.A: Technical Measurement Guide (Roedler and Jones, 2005)
options.B: Systems Engineering Leading Indicators Guide, Version 2.0 (Roedler, et al., 2010)
options.C: System Development Performance Measurement Report (NDIA, et al., 2011)
options.D: Project Manager's Guide to Systems Engineering Measurement for Project Success (INCOSE PMGtSEMfPS, 2015)

correct_answer_text: Technical Measurement Guide (Roedler and Jones, 2005)

question: Per the INCOSE Measurement Primer (2010), the Measurement process may be thought of as which kind of system?

options.A: A feedforward open-loop reporting system
options.B: A statistical process control (SPC) system
options.C: A feedback control system in which value is obtained when data analysis provides insight for assessment or action by decision makers
options.D: A batch data warehouse from which dashboards are rendered on demand

correct_answer_text: A feedback control system in which value is obtained when data analysis provides insight for assessment or action by decision makers

question: Per Figure 2.34 (Integration of Measurement, Risk Management, and Decision Management), how does the Risk Management process create new measurement work?

options.A: It assigns risk owners who themselves perform the measurement activities
options.B: It identifies risks that need to be monitored, thus creating information needs that drive new measures
options.C: It delivers a fixed library of risk indicators that the Measurement process must report on quarterly
options.D: It overrides existing measures whenever a risk's likelihood exceeds a defined threshold

correct_answer_text: It identifies risks that need to be monitored, thus creating information needs that drive new measures

question: Per Figure 2.34 and the surrounding text, what is the relationship between the Measurement process and the Decision Management process?

options.A: Decision Management replaces the need for measurement once a baseline has been approved
options.B: The results of both risk management and measurement provide essential insight to decision makers that is essential to the Decision Management process
options.C: Decision Management is a standalone process and does not consume measurement outputs
options.D: Measurement directly executes decision alternatives selected by the Decision Management process

correct_answer_text: The results of both risk management and measurement provide essential insight to decision makers that is essential to the Decision Management process

question: Per Roedler et al. (2010), what best defines a "leading indicator" in SE measurement?

options.A: A status snapshot of current cost and schedule performance against the approved baseline
options.B: A measure that aggregates historical data to compute a moving average of process compliance
options.C: A measure for evaluating the effectiveness of how a specific activity is applied on a project in a manner that provides information about impacts that are likely to affect the system performance or SE effectiveness objectives
options.D: A red/yellow/green traffic-light status indicator displayed on the project dashboard

correct_answer_text: A measure for evaluating the effectiveness of how a specific activity is applied on a project in a manner that provides information about impacts that are likely to affect the system performance or SE effectiveness objectives

question: How do leading indicators differ from typical status measures, per the handbook?

options.A: Leading indicators provide status and historical information, while status measures use trend information for prediction
options.B: Rather than provide status and historical information, leading indicators use trend information to facilitate predictive (forward-looking) analysis
options.C: Leading indicators are restricted to defense and aerospace domains and cannot be generalized
options.D: Leading indicators are computed only at decision gates and are not collected continuously

correct_answer_text: Rather than provide status and historical information, leading indicators use trend information to facilitate predictive (forward-looking) analysis

question: In Figure 2.35 (Relationship of product-oriented measures), which measure is described as being stated from the acquirer (customer/user) viewpoint and is independent of any particular solution?

options.A: Measures of Performance (MOPs)
options.B: Technical Performance Measures (TPMs)
options.C: Measures of Effectiveness (MOEs)
options.D: Key Performance Parameters (KPPs)

correct_answer_text: Measures of Effectiveness (MOEs)

question: How does the handbook define Key Performance Parameters (KPPs)?

options.A: The full set of system requirements that must each have a threshold and an objective value
options.B: The minimum number of performance parameters needed to characterize the major drivers of operational performance, supportability, and interoperability, each with a threshold and objective value, defined by the acquirer when operational concepts and requirements are defined
options.C: The set of derived technical measures used by the developer to track design progress on critical architectural elements
options.D: Acquirer-defined cost and schedule reserves expressed as percentages of the baseline

correct_answer_text: The minimum number of performance parameters needed to characterize the major drivers of operational performance, supportability, and interoperability, each with a threshold and objective value, defined by the acquirer when operational concepts and requirements are defined

question: Per the handbook, what do Measures of Performance (MOPs) measure, and how are they related to MOEs?

options.A: MOPs measure operational suitability from the user perspective and are independent of MOEs
options.B: MOPs measure attributes considered important to ensure the system has the capability to achieve operational objectives; they are used to assess whether the system meets design or performance requirements necessary to satisfy the MOEs and should be derived from or provide insight for MOEs or other user needs
options.C: MOPs measure cost and schedule performance against the baseline and replace MOEs once development begins
options.D: MOPs are acquirer-defined thresholds that, if not met, trigger termination of the contract

correct_answer_text: MOPs measure attributes considered important to ensure the system has the capability to achieve operational objectives; they are used to assess whether the system meets design or performance requirements necessary to satisfy the MOEs and should be derived from or provide insight for MOEs or other user needs

question: Per the handbook, what are Technical Performance Measures (TPMs) used to do?

options.A: Replace MOEs once the architecture has been baselined and the acquirer has signed off
options.B: Catalog every system requirement so that compliance can be reported at each technical review
options.C: Assess design progress, show compliance to performance requirements, and track technical risks, providing visibility into the status of important project technical parameters
options.D: Compute earned value indices for technical work packages within the integrated master schedule

correct_answer_text: Assess design progress, show compliance to performance requirements, and track technical risks, providing visibility into the status of important project technical parameters

question: Per the handbook, how should the SELECTION of TPMs be constrained?

options.A: TPMs should comprise a full listing of the requirements of the system or system element
options.B: TPMs should be limited to critical technical thresholds or parameters that, if not met, put the project at cost, schedule, or performance risk
options.C: TPMs should be selected by the acquirer only and should mirror the KPPs one-for-one
options.D: TPMs should cover every architectural element identified during architecture definition so that no element is unmeasured

correct_answer_text: TPMs should be limited to critical technical thresholds or parameters that, if not met, put the project at cost, schedule, or performance risk

question: Per the handbook, which project artifact "should define the approach to TPMs"?

options.A: The Configuration Management Plan
options.B: The Information Management Plan
options.C: The Quality Management Plan
options.D: The Systems Engineering Management Plan (SEMP)

correct_answer_text: The Systems Engineering Management Plan (SEMP)

question: Per Figure 2.36 (TPM monitoring) and the surrounding text, how does the TPM tolerance band typically behave across the life cycle?

options.A: The tolerance band stays constant from start to finish to give a stable reference
options.B: The tolerance band starts tight to enforce early discipline and is widened later to accommodate integration variation
options.C: The tolerance band is generally wider earlier in the life cycle and gets tighter as the system development progresses; values outside the band alert management that corrective action may be necessary
options.D: The tolerance band is computed only at the exit decision gate of each stage

correct_answer_text: The tolerance band is generally wider earlier in the life cycle and gets tighter as the system development progresses; values outside the band alert management that corrective action may be necessary

question: The handbook notes that the progress of some TPMs relies on maturing a particular technology. What additional input may therefore be needed?

options.A: A safety case and hazard log for the affected subsystem
options.B: A technology plan and technology readiness level (TRL) assessment as part of the input associated with a TPM
options.C: A make-or-buy decision memo for the affected component
options.D: An ISO 9001 process audit of the responsible supplier

correct_answer_text: A technology plan and technology readiness level (TRL) assessment as part of the input associated with a TPM

question: Under the "Prepare for measurement" activity, the handbook lists base measures, derived measures, and indicators among the items to be defined. Which statement best characterizes how these three terms relate in the measurement information model?

options.A: Base measures, derived measures, and indicators are synonyms for the same raw data item, differing only by the audience receiving them
options.B: Base measures are the lowest-level data items collected; derived measures are computed from base measures (and/or other derived measures); indicators interpret derived measures against decision criteria to support an information need
options.C: Indicators are collected first as raw counts; derived measures aggregate indicators; base measures are the executive-level rollups reported at decision gates
options.D: Base measures and derived measures are interchangeable terms; only "indicator" has a distinct technical meaning in ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939

correct_answer_text: Base measures are the lowest-level data items collected; derived measures are computed from base measures (and/or other derived measures); indicators interpret derived measures against decision criteria to support an information need

question: Per the handbook, what should drive the selection of specific measures on a project?

options.A: The organization's standard measurement catalog, applied uniformly to every project
options.B: Whatever data items the measurement tool suite can automatically collect at lowest cost
options.C: Information needs and how that information will be used to make decisions and take action
options.D: The acquirer's preferred dashboard format and color-coding conventions

correct_answer_text: Information needs and how that information will be used to make decisions and take action

question: According to the handbook, what factors determine the frequency at which each measure should be regularly reviewed by the measurement stakeholders?

options.A: Solely the contractual reporting cadence specified in the agreement
options.B: Frequency of data availability/change, level of risk, maturity of the organization, and cycle times
options.C: The configuration management plan's baseline-change frequency
options.D: The earned value reporting period defined by the cost-account manager

correct_answer_text: Frequency of data availability/change, level of risk, maturity of the organization, and cycle times

question: The handbook lists characteristics of "the best measures." Which set best matches its guidance?

options.A: Manually collected, narrative in form, reviewed only at decision gates, and unique to each project
options.B: Repeatable, implementable with automated data collection or minimal effort, straightforward to understand, and presented in a consistent format on a regular basis (with trend data and projections where applicable)
options.C: Custom-coded for each measure owner, presented in narrative form, and updated only when a stakeholder requests a snapshot
options.D: Aggregated into a single composite score, presented quarterly, and gated by configuration control board approval

correct_answer_text: Repeatable, implementable with automated data collection or minimal effort, straightforward to understand, and presented in a consistent format on a regular basis (with trend data and projections where applicable)

question: A TPM (mass margin) has trended outside its established tolerance band for two consecutive reporting periods. Per the handbook's guidance, which response is most aligned with the Measurement process?

options.A: Widen the tolerance band so the measure is back within limits and continue monitoring
options.B: Treat the trend as an alert that corrective action may be necessary, and conduct further investigation into the measure to identify the root cause so corrective action addresses the cause instead of the symptom
options.C: Drop the TPM from the measurement register because it is no longer useful for decision making
options.D: Wait for the next decision gate and let the gate review board determine whether the trend warrants action

correct_answer_text: Treat the trend as an alert that corrective action may be necessary, and conduct further investigation into the measure to identify the root cause so corrective action addresses the cause instead of the symptom

question: Why does the handbook depict the TPM tolerance band as wider early in the life cycle and tighter as development progresses?

options.A: Early-stage analytical estimates carry greater uncertainty, so wider bands accommodate that uncertainty; as design matures and data become more refined, tighter bands are appropriate to detect deviations that threaten meeting the target at delivery
options.B: Wider early bands accommodate frequent requirement changes that are expected before PDR; tighter later bands enforce contractual penalties as delivery approaches
options.C: Acquirers permit wider early bands as a goodwill gesture and tighten them later to extract concessions during negotiations
options.D: Early-stage measurements are unreliable, so the wide band hides noise; later-stage measurements are calibrated, so the narrow band reflects true precision

correct_answer_text: Early-stage analytical estimates carry greater uncertainty, so wider bands accommodate that uncertainty; as design matures and data become more refined, tighter bands are appropriate to detect deviations that threaten meeting the target at delivery

question: Which of the following is the BEST example of a leading indicator, as the term is used in §2.3.4.7?

options.A: Number of defects found in formal qualification testing of the integrated system after first article assembly
options.B: Trend in requirements volatility (rate of requirements changes per period) used to forecast downstream rework risk before design review
options.C: Total cost variance reported at the end of the prior accounting period
options.D: Count of safety incidents recorded after the system entered operations

correct_answer_text: Trend in requirements volatility (rate of requirements changes per period) used to forecast downstream rework risk before design review

question: The handbook states that "measurement by itself does not control or improve process performance, project success, or product quality." What additional condition must be met for benefit to be realized?

options.A: The measure must be automatically collected and stored in a digital repository
options.B: Measurement results must be provided to decision makers in a manner that provides the needed insight for the right decisions to be made, AND action must be taken
options.C: The measure must be reported on a Red/Yellow/Green dashboard at every milestone review
options.D: The measure must be approved by the configuration control board before being acted upon

correct_answer_text: Measurement results must be provided to decision makers in a manner that provides the needed insight for the right decisions to be made, AND action must be taken

question: Per the handbook, what are TWO key sources of the information needs that drive the measures selected for a project?

options.A: The project's accounting calendar and the supplier's invoice schedule
options.B: The objectives of the project (resources, technical performance, product/process quality, etc.) and the key risks of the project identified by the Risk Management process
options.C: The acquirer's marketing requirements document and the prime contractor's pricing strategy
options.D: The configuration management baseline and the integrated master schedule alone

correct_answer_text: The objectives of the project (resources, technical performance, product/process quality, etc.) and the key risks of the project identified by the Risk Management process

question: Per Figure 2.34 and the surrounding text, what relationship does Measurement have with the Risk Management process (beyond receiving information needs from it)?

options.A: Measurement replaces Risk Management once a measure is automated
options.B: The results of the measurement analysis may uncover new risks that need to be considered by the Risk Management process, while Measurement also helps characterize and quantify identified risks
options.C: Measurement only reports historical results to Risk Management at decision gates
options.D: Risk Management directly produces measures that bypass the Measurement process

correct_answer_text: The results of the measurement analysis may uncover new risks that need to be considered by the Risk Management process, while Measurement also helps characterize and quantify identified risks

question: Per Figure 2.35 (the product-oriented measures hierarchy from Roedler and Jones, 2005), as one moves from MOEs down to TPMs, which two attributes increase?

options.A: Acquirer authority and contractual leverage
options.B: Technical resolution & periodic insight, and scope of technical solution
options.C: Cost of measurement and reporting cadence to the executive sponsor
options.D: Independence from the solution and abstraction from physical parameters

correct_answer_text: Technical resolution & periodic insight, and scope of technical solution

question: Per the handbook, what distinguishes a Key Performance Parameter (KPP) value structure from a typical requirement threshold?

options.A: A KPP has only a single threshold value that the system must meet exactly
options.B: Each KPP has both a threshold value and an objective value, defined by the acquirer at the time the operational concepts and requirements are defined
options.C: A KPP has a tolerance band that widens over the life cycle, like a TPM
options.D: A KPP is computed by the developer from MOPs and has no acquirer-defined values

correct_answer_text: Each KPP has both a threshold value and an objective value, defined by the acquirer at the time the operational concepts and requirements are defined

question: Per the handbook, beyond the Project Manager / Technical Lead, who else does measurement support as part of "an integrated set of management processes"?

options.A: Only the program executive and the contracting officer
options.B: SE practitioners, analysts, quality management/assurance, and nearly all other technical and management functions/roles
options.C: Only the configuration management board and the quality assurance auditor
options.D: Only the acquirer's independent verification and validation (IV&V) agent

correct_answer_text: SE practitioners, analysts, quality management/assurance, and nearly all other technical and management functions/roles

question: Per the handbook, what is the role of Digital Engineering (DE) measures with respect to other product-related measures?

options.A: DE measures replace all traditional product measures once an authoritative source of truth is established
options.B: DE measures focus on the implementation of DE transformations and the realization of measurable benefits, and can also serve as useful leading indicators for other product-related measures
options.C: DE measures are restricted to MBSE tool-usage counts and have no relationship to product measures
options.D: DE measures are lagging indicators only, used after deployment to assess DE return on investment

correct_answer_text: DE measures focus on the implementation of DE transformations and the realization of measurable benefits, and can also serve as useful leading indicators for other product-related measures

question: Categorize each item in the Measurement Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.33).
Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 [6.3.8.1], what is the purpose of the Quality Assurance process?

options.A: To verify and validate the system-of-interest against stakeholder requirements at each life cycle stage gate
options.B: To help ensure the effective application of the organization's Quality Management process to the project
options.C: To establish the organization's overall quality policy, mission, strategies, goals, and objectives
options.D: To detect defects in delivered products and services through inspection, monitoring, and rework

correct_answer_text: To help ensure the effective application of the organization's Quality Management process to the project

question: How does the Handbook (citing ASQ, 2007) broadly define Quality Assurance (QA)?

options.A: The set of activities that detect and remove defects from completed products before delivery to stakeholders
options.B: The statistical sampling techniques used to verify conformance of high-volume production output to tolerance bands
options.C: The set of activities throughout the entire project life cycle necessary to provide adequate confidence that a product or service conforms to stakeholder requirements or that a process adheres to established methodology
options.D: The organizational-level governance activities that establish the quality policy and allocate budget to quality programs

correct_answer_text: The set of activities throughout the entire project life cycle necessary to provide adequate confidence that a product or service conforms to stakeholder requirements or that a process adheres to established methodology

question: Which statement best captures the distinction the Handbook draws between Quality Assurance (QA) and Quality Control (QC)?

options.A: QA puts good things into our processes so they perform as designed, while QC takes bad things out of our processes after they occur to prevent defects from reaching stakeholders
options.B: QA inspects finished products for defects, while QC focuses on training personnel and writing procedures
options.C: QA is performed by the project team, while QC is performed only by external regulatory auditors
options.D: QA and QC are interchangeable terms for the same defect-detection methodology

correct_answer_text: QA puts good things into our processes so they perform as designed, while QC takes bad things out of our processes after they occur to prevent defects from reaching stakeholders

question: Quality Assurance is described as one of the two defect-prevention methods under Quality Management. What is the other method?

options.A: Technical Performance Measurement (TPM)
options.B: Statistical Process Control (SPC)
options.C: Verification and Validation (V&V)
options.D: Quality Control (QC)

correct_answer_text: Quality Control (QC)

question: Per the Figure 2.37 IPO diagram, which of the following is listed as a typical INPUT to the Quality Assurance process?

options.A: Quality assurance corrective action
options.B: Quality assurance evaluation report
options.C: Quality management strategy/approach
options.D: Quality assurance records/artifacts

correct_answer_text: Quality management strategy/approach

question: Per the Figure 2.37 IPO diagram, which of the following is listed as a typical OUTPUT of the Quality Assurance process?

options.A: Quality management criteria and methods
options.B: Quality management corrective action
options.C: Quality management report
options.D: Quality assurance evaluation report

correct_answer_text: Quality assurance evaluation report

question: Which of the following is NOT one of the five activity groups of the Quality Assurance process listed in Figure 2.37?

options.A: Prepare for quality assurance
options.B: Perform product or service evaluations
options.C: Plan quality management
options.D: Treat incidents and problems

correct_answer_text: Plan quality management

question: Under the 'Perform process evaluations' activity, what role does QA surveillance play with respect to the developing organization?

options.A: It substitutes for product verification testing by directly examining the design output
options.B: It provides an independent evaluation of whether the developing organization is in compliance with established procedures
options.C: It defines new procedures whenever existing procedures are found inadequate
options.D: It transfers responsibility for quality from the project team to the QA function

correct_answer_text: It provides an independent evaluation of whether the developing organization is in compliance with established procedures

question: Within 'Treat incidents and problems,' how does the Handbook distinguish an 'incident' from a 'problem'?

options.A: An incident is a confirmed defect in delivered product; a problem is a precursor anomaly identified before delivery
options.B: An incident is a process nonconformance; a problem is a product nonconformance
options.C: An incident is a short-term anomaly or observation that requires immediate attention; a problem is a confirmed nonconformity that would cause the project to fail to meet requirements
options.D: An incident is a stakeholder-reported issue; a problem is a QA-detected issue

correct_answer_text: An incident is a short-term anomaly or observation that requires immediate attention; a problem is a confirmed nonconformity that would cause the project to fail to meet requirements

question: Under 'Perform product or service evaluations,' how does the Handbook characterize the use of product verification results within QA?

options.A: Product verification results replace the need for separate QA process evaluations
options.B: Product verification results are evaluated as evidence of QA effectiveness
options.C: Product verification results are owned solely by the verification team and are not visible to QA
options.D: Product verification results are deferred to the validation activity and do not feed QA

correct_answer_text: Product verification results are evaluated as evidence of QA effectiveness

question: Per the QA 'Elaboration' discussion, what feedback relationship does Quality Control (QC) have with Quality Assurance (QA)?

options.A: QC supplies QA with defect rates and identifies their source in processes, enabling QA to define input and process improvements
options.B: QC supersedes QA once a product enters production, replacing QA process surveillance
options.C: QC reports directly to senior management and bypasses QA in the corrective-action loop
options.D: QC and QA operate independently, exchanging no information by design to preserve independence

correct_answer_text: QC supplies QA with defect rates and identifies their source in processes, enabling QA to define input and process improvements

question: Under 'Perform process evaluations,' how does the QA process address the project supply chain?

options.A: The supply chain is exempted from QA surveillance because suppliers operate under their own quality systems
options.B: Applicable procedural and surveillance requirements are flowed throughout the project supply chain, and subcontractor processes are evaluated for conformance to allocated requirements
options.C: Only the prime contractor's processes are evaluated; subcontractor compliance is presumed once a contract is signed
options.D: Supply-chain evaluation is performed by the acquirer organization rather than by the project QA function

correct_answer_text: Applicable procedural and surveillance requirements are flowed throughout the project supply chain, and subcontractor processes are evaluated for conformance to allocated requirements

question: Which set of sub-tasks correctly belongs to the 'Prepare for quality assurance' activity?

options.A: Document anomalies; perform root cause analysis; track incidents to closure
options.B: Establish and maintain the QA strategy (often captured in a QA plan); establish and maintain QA guidelines, policies, standards, and procedures; define responsibilities and authorities
options.C: Perform evaluations at appropriate life cycle times; evaluate product verification results; ensure QA representation during V&V
options.D: Implement surveillance on processes; evaluate enabling tools; flow requirements to subcontractors

correct_answer_text: Establish and maintain the QA strategy (often captured in a QA plan); establish and maintain QA guidelines, policies, standards, and procedures; define responsibilities and authorities

question: Quoting W. Edwards Deming, the Handbook reinforces which principle as foundational to QA?

options.A: 'Quality is free, but only when production volumes exceed statistical thresholds'
options.B: 'Quality comes not from inspection, but from improvement of the production process'
options.C: 'Quality is what the customer says it is, regardless of process'
options.D: 'Quality requires perfection in every output to be meaningful'

correct_answer_text: 'Quality comes not from inspection, but from improvement of the production process'

question: Within the 'Manage QA records and reports' activity, which task is explicitly identified?

options.A: Establish the organization's quality policy, mission, and strategic objectives
options.B: Allocate educational budget across the workforce to sustain a QM culture
options.C: Define stakeholder requirements and flow them to system elements
options.D: Identify incidents and problems associated with product and process evaluations, and create, maintain, and store records and reports in accordance with applicable requirements

correct_answer_text: Identify incidents and problems associated with product and process evaluations, and create, maintain, and store records and reports in accordance with applicable requirements

question: How does the Handbook position QA relative to Quality Management (QM) at the project level?

options.A: QA replaces QM at the project level; QM applies only to enterprise governance
options.B: QA is a key aspect in QM from the project level to the individual processes, involving resourcing and improved performance of processes with built-in quality designed to prevent defects in delivered products and services
options.C: QA is independent of QM and follows a separate standard with no overlap of activities
options.D: QA is a subset of Quality Control that is executed only after product verification completes

correct_answer_text: QA is a key aspect in QM from the project level to the individual processes, involving resourcing and improved performance of processes with built-in quality designed to prevent defects in delivered products and services

question: On a project, a systems engineer notices that the design team has been releasing baselines without performing the prescribed peer-review procedure documented in the organization's SE process. Under the QA process, which activity is specifically intended to catch this kind of non-compliance?

options.A: Perform product or service evaluations
options.B: Perform process evaluations
options.C: Manage QA records and reports
options.D: Treat incidents and problems

correct_answer_text: Perform process evaluations

question: During a milestone review, a tester reports a short-term observation: an oscillation in a sensor reading that warrants immediate attention but has not yet been confirmed as a nonconformity that would cause the project to miss requirements. Under the 'Treat incidents and problems' activity, how should this observation be classified?

options.A: An incident
options.B: A problem
options.C: A defect
options.D: A corrective action

correct_answer_text: An incident

question: Per the QA Elaboration discussion, the Handbook notes that QA originated in the aerospace industry under what earlier name?

options.A: Configuration management
options.B: Reliability engineering
options.C: Statistical process control
options.D: Total quality management

correct_answer_text: Reliability engineering

question: Which set of sub-tasks correctly belongs to the 'Treat incidents and problems' activity?

options.A: Establish and maintain the QA strategy; define responsibilities and authorities; maintain QA guidelines
options.B: Document, classify, report, and analyze all anomalies; perform root cause analysis and note trends; recommend appropriate actions to resolve anomalies and errors; track all incidents and problems to closure
options.C: Evaluate enabling tools and environments; flow surveillance requirements to subcontractors; perform process compliance audits
options.D: Evaluate product verification results; ensure QA representation during V&V; perform evaluations at appropriate life cycle times

correct_answer_text: Document, classify, report, and analyze all anomalies; perform root cause analysis and note trends; recommend appropriate actions to resolve anomalies and errors; track all incidents and problems to closure

question: A project's QA function applies Work Process Analysis (WPA) to defect data supplied by Quality Control. Per the QA Elaboration discussion, the purpose of doing so is best described as:

options.A: To assign blame to specific workers responsible for defect introduction
options.B: To define and initiate input and process improvements that produce lean outcomes
options.C: To replace QC inspection activities once defect data is available
options.D: To certify finished products for delivery to stakeholders

correct_answer_text: To define and initiate input and process improvements that produce lean outcomes

question: The Handbook's invocation of Deming's principle that 'Quality comes not from inspection, but from improvement of the production process' is intended to reinforce which QA-versus-QC orientation?

options.A: That QA primarily relies on end-of-line inspection of finished products to ensure conformance
options.B: That QC, not QA, is responsible for process improvement and culture
options.C: That QA prevents defects by building quality into processes ('putting good things in'), whereas inspection alone (a QC orientation) is insufficient to produce quality
options.D: That inspection and process improvement are alternative strategies and an organization should choose one

correct_answer_text: That QA prevents defects by building quality into processes ('putting good things in'), whereas inspection alone (a QC orientation) is insufficient to produce quality

question: Which statement best distinguishes Quality Management (QM), Quality Assurance (QA), and Quality Control (QC) as presented in §2.3.4.8?

options.A: QM is the umbrella discipline that prevents and detects defects; QA prevents defects by building quality into processes; QC detects defects (and supplies defect-rate feedback to QA)
options.B: QM, QA, and QC are synonyms for the same set of defect-detection activities
options.C: QC is the umbrella; QA performs inspection within QC; QM addresses corrective action only
options.D: QA establishes organizational policy; QM evaluates processes on projects; QC writes the QA plan

correct_answer_text: QM is the umbrella discipline that prevents and detects defects; QA prevents defects by building quality into processes; QC detects defects (and supplies defect-rate feedback to QA)

question: A program manager argues that because product verification has already been performed by the engineering team, no additional QA action is needed regarding those results. Per the 'Perform product or service evaluations' activity, what is the correct QA treatment of verification results?

options.A: Verification results are out of QA scope and should not be examined by QA
options.B: Verification results are evaluated by QA as evidence of QA effectiveness
options.C: QA must re-execute the verification activity independently before drawing conclusions
options.D: Verification results are passed to QC, which then reports to QA quarterly

correct_answer_text: Verification results are evaluated by QA as evidence of QA effectiveness

question: A subcontractor delivers an assembly accompanied by their own internal quality certification. The prime's QA function still wishes to evaluate the subcontractor's processes. Which QA activity, and which underlying principle, supports this evaluation?

options.A: 'Manage QA records and reports' — because subcontractor certificates must be archived as QA artifacts
options.B: 'Perform process evaluations' — because applicable procedural and surveillance requirements are flowed throughout the project supply chain and subcontractor processes are evaluated for conformance to allocated requirements
options.C: 'Treat incidents and problems' — because all subcontractor deliveries are presumed nonconforming until evaluated
options.D: 'Prepare for quality assurance' — because the QA plan only governs in-house work, not subcontractors

correct_answer_text: 'Perform process evaluations' — because applicable procedural and surveillance requirements are flowed throughout the project supply chain and subcontractor processes are evaluated for conformance to allocated requirements

question: Under the 'Perform process evaluations' activity, beyond auditing the developing organization and the supply chain, what additional object of evaluation is explicitly identified?

options.A: Stakeholder requirements baselines
options.B: Project earned-value and cost-performance indices
options.C: Enabling tools and environments, for conformance and effectiveness
options.D: Risk register entries and their mitigation plans

correct_answer_text: Enabling tools and environments, for conformance and effectiveness

question: Per the Common Approaches and Tips, management's commitment to QA is reflected primarily in which behaviors?

options.A: Personally chairing every QA audit and signing all anomaly reports
options.B: Integrating QM principles into strategic planning and budgeting, and allocating educational resources to achieve and sustain a reliable QM culture
options.C: Outsourcing QA execution to an independent third-party certifier
options.D: Restricting QA findings to internal-only distribution to protect program reputation

correct_answer_text: Integrating QM principles into strategic planning and budgeting, and allocating educational resources to achieve and sustain a reliable QM culture

question: A QA engineer identifies an anomaly during a process surveillance audit. Per the 'Treat incidents and problems' activity, which sequence reflects the prescribed handling once the anomaly is documented?

options.A: Close the anomaly immediately if the responsible team disputes the finding
options.B: Classify and report the anomaly; perform root cause analysis and note trends; recommend appropriate corrective actions; track to closure
options.C: Defer all anomalies to the next milestone review for batch disposition
options.D: Convert every anomaly into a contractual change request before any analysis

correct_answer_text: Classify and report the anomaly; perform root cause analysis and note trends; recommend appropriate corrective actions; track to closure

question: Which pairing correctly maps an example finding to the QA activity group that produced it?

options.A: 'The peer-review procedure was bypassed on three releases' → Perform product or service evaluations
options.B: 'A unit failed its acceptance verification test' → Perform process evaluations
options.C: 'A confirmed nonconformity that would cause the project to fail to meet requirements has been recorded and assigned' → Treat incidents and problems
options.D: 'The QA plan and responsibilities have been updated for the new phase' → Manage QA records and reports

correct_answer_text: 'A confirmed nonconformity that would cause the project to fail to meet requirements has been recorded and assigned' → Treat incidents and problems

question: Categorize each item in the Quality Assurance Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.37).

items.text: Manage quality assurance records and reports

question: Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288, how many Technical Processes are invoked concurrently, iteratively, and recursively throughout the system life cycle?
According to ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 [5.7.5], the Technical Processes are used to do which of the following?

options.A: Establish agreements between an acquirer and supplier and manage supplier performance
options.B: Define the requirements for a system, transform the requirements into an effective product, permit consistent reproduction, use the product, sustain services, and dispose of the product
options.C: Plan, assess, and control the project to meet cost, schedule, and quality objectives
options.D: Provide infrastructure, human resources, knowledge, and quality management for the organization

correct_answer_text: Define the requirements for a system, transform the requirements into an effective product, permit consistent reproduction, use the product, sustain services, and dispose of the product

question: Figure 2.38 (Technical Processes in context) emphasizes which key point about how the Technical Processes are practiced?

options.A: They are practiced strictly sequentially as drawn in the figure
options.B: They are practiced concurrently, iteratively, and recursively as the project team moves down the layers of the system architecture
options.C: They are applied only once per life cycle stage and never revisited
options.D: They are exclusively applied to the integrated SoI and never to system elements

correct_answer_text: They are practiced concurrently, iteratively, and recursively as the project team moves down the layers of the system architecture

question: Per Figure 2.38 and its key points, which Technical Process is applied from the beginning of the project to manage the integrated system as the project team traverses the architecture?

options.A: Verification
options.B: Validation
options.C: Integration
options.D: Transition

correct_answer_text: Integration

question: Following each transformation, which process ensures the output artifacts were transformed "right" as defined by their requirements?

options.A: Validation
options.B: Verification
options.C: Quality Assurance
options.D: Measurement

correct_answer_text: Verification

question: Following each transformation, which process confirms that the output artifacts are the "right" artifacts as defined by the stakeholder needs and requirements?

options.A: Verification
options.B: Validation
options.C: Configuration Management
options.D: Quality Management

correct_answer_text: Validation

question: Once the SoI has been deployed and entered operations, what two checks are performed to ensure it still meets expectations and requirements over time?

options.A: Post-deployment verification (still "right"?) and post-deployment validation (still the "right" SoI?)
options.B: Acceptance testing and warranty verification
options.C: Quality audits and surveillance reviews
options.D: Regression testing and stress testing

correct_answer_text: Post-deployment verification (still "right"?) and post-deployment validation (still the "right" SoI?)

question: Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 [6.4.1.1], what is the purpose of the Business or Mission Analysis process?

options.A: To specify the system requirements that the SoI must satisfy in its operational environment
options.B: To define the overall strategic problem or opportunity, characterize the solution space, and determine potential solution class(es) that can address a problem or take advantage of an opportunity
options.C: To elicit, capture, and prioritize the needs of all stakeholders into stakeholder requirements
options.D: To define the system architecture and allocate requirements to system elements

correct_answer_text: To define the overall strategic problem or opportunity, characterize the solution space, and determine potential solution class(es) that can address a problem or take advantage of an opportunity

question: Which Technical Process initiates the life cycle of the SoI by defining the problem or opportunity space?

options.A: Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition
options.B: System Requirements Definition
options.C: Business or Mission Analysis
options.D: System Architecture Definition

correct_answer_text: Business or Mission Analysis

question: An ASEP candidate must distinguish the first three Technical Processes. Which sequence correctly orders them?

options.A: System Requirements Definition -> Business or Mission Analysis -> Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition
options.B: Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition -> Business or Mission Analysis -> System Requirements Definition
options.C: Business or Mission Analysis -> Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition -> System Requirements Definition
options.D: Business or Mission Analysis -> System Requirements Definition -> Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition

correct_answer_text: Business or Mission Analysis -> Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition -> System Requirements Definition

question: Business or Mission Analysis is fundamentally focused on which space?

options.A: The solution space only, by selecting a final system design
options.B: The implementation space, by allocating requirements to hardware and software components
options.C: The problem or opportunity space, including defining the mission/business problem and characterizing the solution space
options.D: The verification space, by defining test cases and acceptance criteria

correct_answer_text: The problem or opportunity space, including defining the mission/business problem and characterizing the solution space

question: Per the Description of Business or Mission Analysis, which set of activities is included in characterizing the solution space?

options.A: Identifying environmental conditions and business constraints that bound the solution domain
options.B: Defining detailed system requirements and verification criteria
options.C: Decomposing the system into subsystems and allocating requirements
options.D: Performing post-deployment validation against stakeholder real-world expectations

correct_answer_text: Identifying environmental conditions and business constraints that bound the solution domain

question: Per Figure 2.39 (IPO diagram for Business or Mission Analysis), which of the following is a typical INPUT to the process?

options.A: Alternative solution classes
options.B: Concept of Operations (ConOps)
options.C: Business or mission analysis strategy/approach
options.D: Problem or opportunity statement

correct_answer_text: Concept of Operations (ConOps)

question: Per Figure 2.39, which of the following is a typical OUTPUT of the Business or Mission Analysis process (not an input)?

options.A: Organization strategic plan
options.B: Source documents
options.C: Constraints on solution
options.D: Alternative solution classes

correct_answer_text: Alternative solution classes

question: Per Figure 2.39, which of the following is listed as one of the five process Activities for Business or Mission Analysis?

options.A: Define the system architecture
options.B: Allocate system requirements
options.C: Characterize the solution space
options.D: Verify the system against stakeholder needs

correct_answer_text: Characterize the solution space

question: Which analytical technique is explicitly mentioned in the Business or Mission Analysis process for understanding the scope, drivers, constraints, risks, needs, and requirements of the problem space?

options.A: FMEA (Failure Modes and Effects Analysis)
options.B: SWOT (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats) analysis
options.C: FTA (Fault Tree Analysis)
options.D: Critical Path Method (CPM)

correct_answer_text: SWOT (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats) analysis

question: In the Business or Mission Analysis process, the acronym PESTEL refers to which set of factors used in problem/opportunity analysis?

options.A: Performance, Engineering, Safety, Technology, Environment, Logistics
options.B: Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Environmental, and Legal
options.C: People, Equipment, Standards, Tools, Education, Leadership
options.D: Process, Effectiveness, Schedule, Test, Evaluation, Lifecycle

correct_answer_text: Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Environmental, and Legal

question: When defining the problem or opportunity in Business or Mission Analysis, the definition must be:

options.A: Specific to a single chosen system design
options.B: Solution-class independent, since the solution could be an operational change, a change to an existing system or service, or a new system
options.C: Specified down to the component level
options.D: Restricted to new system development only

correct_answer_text: Solution-class independent, since the solution could be an operational change, a change to an existing system or service, or a new system

question: In the Business or Mission Analysis process, what do critical business success measures and their validation criteria establish?

options.A: The detailed test procedures used during system verification
options.B: Critical and desired system performance—thresholds and objectives for system performance parameters that are critical for system success
options.C: The schedule and budget baselines for the program
options.D: The supplier evaluation criteria and contract terms

correct_answer_text: Critical and desired system performance—thresholds and objectives for system performance parameters that are critical for system success

question: Per the "Characterize the solution space" activity, the preliminary life cycle concepts are defined from whose perspective?

options.A: The supplier's perspective regarding manufacturing capability
options.B: The verification engineer's perspective regarding test procedures
options.C: The business stakeholder's perspective of intended use in the intended operational environment
options.D: The end user's perspective regarding training requirements

correct_answer_text: The business stakeholder's perspective of intended use in the intended operational environment

question: Per the Elaboration on "Identify Major Stakeholders," who is primarily responsible for nominating key stakeholders during the Business or Mission Analysis process?

options.A: The SE practitioner
options.B: Business managers
options.C: The acquirer
options.D: The supplier

correct_answer_text: Business managers

question: How does the handbook distinguish business needs from business requirements?

options.A: Business needs and business requirements are synonymous terms
options.B: Business needs identify "what is needed" by the business; business requirements communicate what the business requires of the solution to address those needs without stating a specific solution
options.C: Business needs are derived from system requirements; business requirements drive them
options.D: Business needs are supplier-owned; business requirements are user-owned

correct_answer_text: Business needs identify "what is needed" by the business; business requirements communicate what the business requires of the solution to address those needs without stating a specific solution

question: Which of the following best describes the Concept of Operations (ConOps) as defined in the Business or Mission Analysis process?

options.A: Describes the way the system will be used during operations, for what purpose, in its operational environment by its intended users
options.B: Describes the way the organization will operate to achieve its missions, goals, and objectives, including how the system will impact the acquiring and other organizations
options.C: Describes the way the system will be delivered, integrated, and introduced into operations
options.D: Describes the logistics, support infrastructure, and staffing needed to support the system after deployment

correct_answer_text: Describes the way the organization will operate to achieve its missions, goals, and objectives, including how the system will impact the acquiring and other organizations

question: Which of the following best describes the Operational Concept (OpsCon) as defined in Business or Mission Analysis?

options.A: Describes how the organization will operate to achieve its missions and goals
options.B: Describes the way the system will be used during operations, for what purpose, in its operational environment by its intended users, providing a user-oriented perspective of the to-be-delivered system
options.C: Describes the way the system will be acquired from a supplier
options.D: Describes the way the system will be removed from operation and retired

correct_answer_text: Describes the way the system will be used during operations, for what purpose, in its operational environment by its intended users, providing a user-oriented perspective of the to-be-delivered system

question: Which statement correctly distinguishes ConOps from OpsCon?

options.A: ConOps is system-focused; OpsCon is organization-focused
options.B: ConOps describes how the organization operates to achieve its mission with the system; OpsCon describes how the to-be-delivered system will be used by its intended users in its operational environment
options.C: ConOps and OpsCon are interchangeable terms with no meaningful difference
options.D: ConOps is produced by the supplier; OpsCon is produced by the acquirer

correct_answer_text: ConOps describes how the organization operates to achieve its mission with the system; OpsCon describes how the to-be-delivered system will be used by its intended users in its operational environment

question: Per the Life Cycle Concepts elaboration, the enterprise-level ConOps is ideally treated as which of the following with respect to the Business or Mission Analysis process?

options.A: An input to the process; if it does not exist, it may need to be jointly developed and maintained
options.B: An output of the process generated during solution selection
options.C: A control imposed only after the Stakeholder Needs process
options.D: An artifact that is unrelated to Business or Mission Analysis

correct_answer_text: An input to the process; if it does not exist, it may need to be jointly developed and maintained

question: Which set correctly lists the principal preliminary life cycle concepts established or assured through the Business or Mission Analysis process?

options.A: Test concept, integration concept, manufacturing concept, sustainment concept, training concept
options.B: ConOps, OpsCon, acquisition concept, deployment concept, support concept, and retirement concept
options.C: Marketing concept, financial concept, legal concept, regulatory concept, ethical concept
options.D: Requirements concept, architecture concept, design concept, verification concept, validation concept

correct_answer_text: ConOps, OpsCon, acquisition concept, deployment concept, support concept, and retirement concept

question: After Business or Mission Analysis establishes the preliminary life cycle concepts, where are they then elaborated and refined?

options.A: Through the System Architecture Definition process (Section 2.3.5.4)
options.B: Through the Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition process (Section 2.3.5.2)
options.C: Through the Verification process (Section 2.3.5.9)
options.D: Through the Transition process (Section 2.3.5.11)

correct_answer_text: Through the Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition process (Section 2.3.5.2)

question: Which of the following is NOT one of the 14 ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 Technical Processes (Section 2.3.5) cataloged in the handbook?

options.A: Business or Mission Analysis Process
options.B: System Architecture Definition Process
options.C: Risk Management Process
options.D: Disposal Process

correct_answer_text: Risk Management Process

question: Which set lists Technical Processes (rather than Technical Management or Agreement Processes) per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 Section 2.3.5?

options.A: Acquisition, Supply, Project Planning, Project Assessment and Control
options.B: Business or Mission Analysis, Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition, System Requirements Definition, System Architecture Definition
options.C: Risk Management, Configuration Management, Information Management, Quality Assurance
options.D: Human Resource Management, Knowledge Management, Quality Management, Infrastructure Management

correct_answer_text: Business or Mission Analysis, Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition, System Requirements Definition, System Architecture Definition

question: Per the handbook's introduction to Technical Processes (2.3.5), the Technical Processes are invoked in conjunction with what other ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 process categories?

options.A: Agreement processes only
options.B: Organizational project-enabling processes only
options.C: Agreement and Technical Management process activities
options.D: Acquisition and Supply processes only

correct_answer_text: Agreement and Technical Management process activities

question: Per Figure 2.38, the System of Interest (SoI) at the beginning of the system life cycle could be which of the following?

options.A: Only the fully integrated end-item system
options.B: Only an individual system element at the lowest level of decomposition
options.C: The integrated system, a set of system elements, or a system element within the system architecture
options.D: Only the enabling systems used to support development

correct_answer_text: The integrated system, a set of system elements, or a system element within the system architecture

question: A project is just beginning to formulate a new system and must define the strategic problem to be solved, characterize the solution space, and determine candidate solution classes. Which Technical Process is being executed?

options.A: Stakeholder Needs and Requirements Definition Process
options.B: Business or Mission Analysis Process
options.C: System Architecture Definition Process
options.D: Validation Process

correct_answer_text: Business or Mission Analysis Process

question: Per the handbook's elaboration on Life Cycle Concepts, the Acquisition concept describes which of the following?

options.A: How the system will be removed from operation and retired, including disposal of hazardous materials
options.B: How the system will be procured from a supplier (or produced internally), including stakeholder engagement, requirements definition, design, production, verification, validation, and contract deliverables
options.C: How the system will be delivered, integrated into its operational environment, and introduced into operations
options.D: How the organization will operate to achieve its missions, goals, and objectives

correct_answer_text: How the system will be procured from a supplier (or produced internally), including stakeholder engagement, requirements definition, design, production, verification, validation, and contract deliverables

question: Per the Life Cycle Concepts elaboration, the Deployment concept describes which of the following?

options.A: How the system will be delivered, integrated into its operational environment, and introduced into operations (including integration with systems already in operation or replacement of systems in operation)
options.B: How the system will be used during operations by its intended users
options.C: How the logistics, support infrastructure, and staffing will sustain the system after deployment
options.D: How the system will be removed from operation and retired
