correct_answer_text: (1) The model/simulation accurately reflects the intended behavior of the entity it represents in its operational environment when operated by intended users, and (2) the model/simulation meets the intended purpose for which it was developed

question: Per Figure 2.53 (Validation per level), what does validation of a realized SoI (system validation) confirm?

options.A: That all verification reports have been signed by the approval authority
options.B: That every system requirement has been satisfied with objective evidence
options.C: That the realized SoI meets its intended purpose in its operational environment when operated by the intended users and does not enable unintended users to negatively impact intended use, with acceptable confidence per stakeholder needs and requirements
options.D: That the SoI passes all certifications issued by external authorities

correct_answer_text: That the realized SoI meets its intended purpose in its operational environment when operated by the intended users and does not enable unintended users to negatively impact intended use, with acceptable confidence per stakeholder needs and requirements

question: Per Figure 2.53 'Validation per level', against what is the system architecture validated?

options.A: Against the system requirements only
options.B: Against the stakeholder needs and stakeholder requirements, to confirm it is the right architecture that will result in a design meeting them
options.C: Against external interface control documents
options.D: Against the qualification test plan

correct_answer_text: Against the stakeholder needs and stakeholder requirements, to confirm it is the right architecture that will result in a design meeting them

question: Which validation outcome is conducted prior to transition to the acquirer so that the acquirer can decide whether transition is appropriate?

options.A: Certification
options.B: Qualification
options.C: Readiness for Use
options.D: Acceptance

correct_answer_text: Acceptance

question: Which validation outcome is a written assurance that the system has been developed per a defined procedure and can perform its intended functions in accordance with identified legal or industrial standards, typically performed by outside authorities?

options.A: Acceptance
options.B: Certification
options.C: Qualification
options.D: Readiness for Use

correct_answer_text: Certification

question: Which validation outcome requires that ALL verification and validation actions have been successfully performed and documented, covering not only the SoI itself but also all interfaces with its environment, concluded by an acceptance review and/or operational readiness review?

options.A: Acceptance
options.B: Readiness for Use
options.C: Qualification
options.D: Certification

correct_answer_text: Qualification

question: 'Validation per Level' describes that validation planning and execution proceed in what manner across hierarchical layers?

options.A: Top-down only, beginning with the integrated SoI and decomposing to elements
options.B: Bottom-up only, finalizing each element before any higher-level planning
options.C: Recursively, with planning at each level as definition evolves and execution at each layer as elements are integrated
options.D: Sequentially, completing all validation at one level before any work begins at the next

correct_answer_text: Recursively, with planning at each level as definition evolves and execution at each layer as elements are integrated

question: What does the handbook caution about substituting validation of the realized system with model and simulation results?

options.A: It is acceptable whenever the model has been formally certified by an outside authority
options.B: The SE practitioner is cautioned NOT to substitute realized-system validation with model/simulation results unless absolutely necessary, because doing so reduces confidence and adds delivery/regulatory risk
options.C: Model-based results are preferred to physical validation because they reveal more emergent properties
options.D: Substitution is encouraged once the digital twin has achieved a defined fidelity threshold

correct_answer_text: The SE practitioner is cautioned NOT to substitute realized-system validation with model/simulation results unless absolutely necessary, because doing so reduces confidence and adds delivery/regulatory risk

question: A major goal of systems validation that distinguishes it from purely model-based assessment is:

options.A: Confirming compliance of each individual system requirement via inspection
options.B: Assessing the behavior of the integrated physical system and identifying emergent properties not specifically addressed in stakeholder needs/requirements nor identified during modeling and simulations
options.C: Producing a formal qualification certificate signed by the acquirer
options.D: Documenting all open noncompliances for transfer to the Operation process

correct_answer_text: Assessing the behavior of the integrated physical system and identifying emergent properties not specifically addressed in stakeholder needs/requirements nor identified during modeling and simulations

question: When changes to the SoI definition (e.g., stakeholder needs, requirements, architecture, design, interfaces) are needed because of validation findings, those changes are handled through which processes?

options.A: The Validation process directly modifies the affected artifacts
options.B: The Acquisition and Supply processes via contract change notices
options.C: The Project Assessment and Control process and the Configuration Management process (with downstream changes performed in other Technical Processes)
options.D: The Quality Assurance process and the Knowledge Management process

correct_answer_text: The Project Assessment and Control process and the Configuration Management process (with downstream changes performed in other Technical Processes)

question: Per the handbook, which scenario types must validation address in addition to nominal operations to remain credible?

options.A: Only nominal operations, since off-nominal scenarios fall under safety analysis
options.B: Off-nominal scenarios, alternate cases, misuse cases, and loss scenarios
options.C: Solely scenarios drawn from the qualification test matrix
options.D: Only scenarios in which the intended users perform optimally

correct_answer_text: Off-nominal scenarios, alternate cases, misuse cases, and loss scenarios

question: Validation actions during the Business or Mission Analysis process include which kind of assessment?

options.A: Assessment of the OpsCon through operational scenarios that exercise all system operational modes and demonstrate system-level performance
options.B: Assessment of supplier compliance with the SEMP
options.C: Assessment of hardware/software qualification margins against derating standards
options.D: Assessment of test coverage of derived system requirements

correct_answer_text: Assessment of the OpsCon through operational scenarios that exercise all system operational modes and demonstrate system-level performance

question: What is the principal benefit of using models and simulations for early validation in an MBSE-enabled program?

options.A: It eliminates the need to validate the realized system in the operational environment
options.B: It guarantees that no emergent properties will appear during physical integration
options.C: It enables expectation management and early stakeholder feedback on architecture and design before implementation, reducing later rework
options.D: It transfers validation accountability from the supplier to the acquirer

correct_answer_text: It enables expectation management and early stakeholder feedback on architecture and design before implementation, reducing later rework

question: An engineer reports that the SoI satisfies every system requirement and passed every requirement-by-requirement check. The acquirer's operational users, however, report the delivered system does not actually achieve the mission they intended. Per the handbook's V-and-V distinction, what is the most accurate characterization?

options.A: The system passed verification but has not been shown to pass validation, because validation focuses on the right artifact achieving intended use in the intended operational environment by the intended users
options.B: The system passed validation but failed verification, because validation only addresses individual requirement compliance
options.C: Both verification and validation have been satisfied; the issue is a Transition process gap
options.D: Verification and validation are interchangeable, so the reported gap indicates a contractual rather than technical issue

correct_answer_text: The system passed verification but has not been shown to pass validation, because validation focuses on the right artifact achieving intended use in the intended operational environment by the intended users

question: An SE practitioner is selecting validation methods for confirming that an emergency-response vehicle achieves its stakeholder-defined response-time profile in mixed urban traffic. Which choice best reflects the handbook's guidance on available validation methods?

options.A: Choose only test, since validation methods are restricted to physical testing
options.B: Choose from inspection, analysis, demonstration, or test (the same set of methods used for verification), driven by the success criteria and constraints
options.C: Choose either audit or peer review, because validation is a quality-assurance activity
options.D: Choose only modeling and simulation, since validation is forward-looking

correct_answer_text: Choose from inspection, analysis, demonstration, or test (the same set of methods used for verification), driven by the success criteria and constraints

question: A program team validates a wearable medical device successfully in a clinic with trained clinicians. After fielding, end users at home cannot achieve the same outcomes. Per the handbook's discussion of 'test as you fly, fly as you test', what is the underlying lesson?

options.A: Validation only needs to be repeated when verification results expire
options.B: A positive validation result obtained in a given environment by specific users can turn noncompliant if the environment or class of users changes
options.C: Validation outcomes transfer automatically across user populations once Certification is obtained
options.D: Home-use validation must be deferred to the Maintenance process

correct_answer_text: A positive validation result obtained in a given environment by specific users can turn noncompliant if the environment or class of users changes

question: An SE practitioner is building the validation scenario set for a passenger ferry. Beyond nominal operations, which set of scenarios does the handbook explicitly direct the practitioner to include?

options.A: Only nominal scenarios, with off-nominal cases handled by safety analysis instead
options.B: Only the scenarios that map one-to-one to system requirements
options.C: Off-nominal, alternate cases, misuse cases, and loss scenarios in addition to nominal operations
options.D: Only scenarios that have an analytical closed-form solution

correct_answer_text: Off-nominal, alternate cases, misuse cases, and loss scenarios in addition to nominal operations

question: A consumer drone is validated to operate correctly when flown by trained pilots. During validation planning, an SE practitioner argues an additional validation focus is required to address unintended users (e.g., minors finding and operating it). What does the handbook's purpose statement support?

options.A: Unintended-user behavior is out of scope; that is a regulatory matter
options.B: Validation must also confirm the system does not enable unintended users to negatively impact intended use or use the system in an unintended way
options.C: Unintended-user protection is addressed only by the Certification outcome, not by validation
options.D: Unintended users are addressed solely through Operation and Maintenance processes

correct_answer_text: Validation must also confirm the system does not enable unintended users to negatively impact intended use or use the system in an unintended way

question: Validation of an integrated avionics suite at the supplier's hardware-in-the-loop lab completes successfully. The acquirer still insists on additional validation activities in their own facility, in the intended operational environment, with the intended users. Per the handbook, this acquirer position is:

options.A: Inconsistent with the handbook, since supplier validation is definitive once the approval authority signs
options.B: Consistent with the handbook, which notes the acquirer will often want to conduct additional validation activities in their own facility, in the intended operational environment, and with the intended users
options.C: Only appropriate if the supplier's validation report is flagged as deficient
options.D: Allowed only when explicitly required by a regulator

correct_answer_text: Consistent with the handbook, which notes the acquirer will often want to conduct additional validation activities in their own facility, in the intended operational environment, and with the intended users

question: During validation of a robotic surgical assistant, the team identifies a cascading interface failure between vision and motion-control elements that was not predicted by simulation. What aspect of validation does this finding most directly illustrate, per the handbook?

options.A: Validation is improperly scoped if simulation did not predict the failure
options.B: A major goal of systems validation is assessing integrated physical-system behavior and identifying emergent properties not specifically addressed in stakeholder needs/requirements nor identified during modeling and simulations
options.C: Such failures fall outside the Validation process and are owned by Risk Management
options.D: Such findings invalidate the verification results and force a reverification

correct_answer_text: A major goal of systems validation is assessing integrated physical-system behavior and identifying emergent properties not specifically addressed in stakeholder needs/requirements nor identified during modeling and simulations

question: A project team prepares to present the consolidated validation evidence package for an integrated SoI. Per the handbook, what is the decision the validation approval authority is empowered to make?

options.A: Whether the supplier agreement should be amended to add scope
options.B: Whether the certification body should be selected for the next phase
options.C: Whether sufficient evidence has been provided to show the entity has passed validation with an acceptable degree of confidence
options.D: Whether the verification report can be issued

correct_answer_text: Whether sufficient evidence has been provided to show the entity has passed validation with an acceptable degree of confidence

question: After a maintenance action on a fielded radar, the team must confirm the system can be returned to service. Which validation outcome category does this scenario most directly describe, per the handbook?

options.A: Acceptance
options.B: Certification
options.C: Readiness for Use
options.D: Qualification

correct_answer_text: Readiness for Use

question: A space system requires demonstrated successful execution of all V&V actions covering the SoI itself and all its interfaces with its environment (including the space segment / ground segment interface), with margins evidence, concluded by an acceptance review and/or operational readiness review. Which validation outcome is being characterized?

options.A: Acceptance
options.B: Certification
options.C: Readiness for Use
options.D: Qualification

correct_answer_text: Qualification

question: A consumer electronics product receives a UL mark in the United States/Canada and a CE mark in Europe. Per the handbook, this is an example of which validation outcome?

options.A: Certification, which is a written assurance typically performed by outside authorities without direction as to how the needs are to be validated
options.B: Acceptance, conducted prior to transition to the acquirer
options.C: Readiness for Use, made by the project team and validation authority
options.D: Qualification, demonstrated by the supplier organization

correct_answer_text: Certification, which is a written assurance typically performed by outside authorities without direction as to how the needs are to be validated

question: A validation activity uncovers that a stakeholder requirement was misinterpreted, forcing changes to the system architecture and several interfaces. Per the handbook, how are those resulting changes managed?

options.A: Directly by the Validation process, which has change-authority over architecture and interfaces
options.B: By the Project Assessment and Control process and the Configuration Management process, with downstream changes performed within other Technical Processes
options.C: By the Verification process, which owns all rework activities
options.D: By the Acquisition process, which issues a contract amendment

correct_answer_text: By the Project Assessment and Control process and the Configuration Management process, with downstream changes performed within other Technical Processes

question: An MBSE-enabled program proposes substituting validation of the realized system with validation results obtained from high-fidelity models and simulations to stay on schedule. Per the handbook, what is the SE practitioner's correct posture?

options.A: Accept the substitution; high-fidelity simulation is equivalent evidence
options.B: Resist the substitution unless absolutely necessary, because it adds project risk, reduces confidence relative to validation against the actual realized system in the actual operational environment with intended users, and increases the risk of failing acquirer or regulatory validation on delivery
options.C: Accept the substitution only if the simulation has been baselined in CM
options.D: Accept the substitution only when the supplier and acquirer mutually waive validation

correct_answer_text: Resist the substitution unless absolutely necessary, because it adds project risk, reduces confidence relative to validation against the actual realized system in the actual operational environment with intended users, and increases the risk of failing acquirer or regulatory validation on delivery

question: An SE practitioner argues that planning validation enabling systems (simulators, test beds, facilities, qualified operators) should start far earlier than usually scheduled. Which handbook 'common approach/tip' supports this?

options.A: Defer enabler identification until verification reports surface gaps
options.B: Identify the enabling systems and materials needed for validation early in the life cycle to allow for the necessary lead time to obtain or access them
options.C: Outsource all enabler acquisition to a third-party V&V provider
options.D: Postpone enabler planning until after the approval authority is named

correct_answer_text: Identify the enabling systems and materials needed for validation early in the life cycle to allow for the necessary lead time to obtain or access them

question: Categorize each item in the Validation Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.52).
Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 as cited in the SE Handbook, what is the purpose of the Operation process?

options.A: To sustain the capability of the system to provide a service
options.B: To use the system to deliver its services
options.C: To establish a system that satisfies the agreement between acquirer and supplier
options.D: To transition the verified system into the operational environment

correct_answer_text: To use the system to deliver its services

question: How does the SE Handbook describe the relationship between the Operation process and the Maintenance process?

options.A: The Operation process must be completed before Maintenance begins
options.B: The Operation process is a sub-activity of the Maintenance process
options.C: The Operation process is often concurrent with the Maintenance process of sustaining the system's services
options.D: The Operation process replaces the Maintenance process once the system reaches steady-state use

correct_answer_text: The Operation process is often concurrent with the Maintenance process of sustaining the system's services

question: Which of the following best distinguishes the Operation process (one of the 14 technical processes) from the Utilization stage of the generic life cycle?

options.A: They are synonymous terms that the handbook uses interchangeably
options.B: The Operation stage is one of the six generic life cycle stages; the Operation process is a technical process applied to use the system to deliver its services
options.C: The Operation process applies only during development, while the Utilization stage applies only during disposal
options.D: The Utilization stage is performed exclusively by the supplier, while the Operation process is performed exclusively by the acquirer

correct_answer_text: The Operation stage is one of the six generic life cycle stages; the Operation process is a technical process applied to use the system to deliver its services

question: According to the Operation process description, how does the SoI contribute to the Business or Mission Analysis process during operation?

options.A: By autonomously generating new system requirements without operator involvement
options.B: By cooperating with human operators and diverse interfacing systems
options.C: By replacing the operating organization's strategic planning function
options.D: By executing acquisition contracts on behalf of the operating organization

correct_answer_text: By cooperating with human operators and diverse interfacing systems

question: Per Figure 2.54 (IPO diagram for the Operation process), which of the following is listed as a typical INPUT to the Operation process?

options.A: Operation strategy/approach
options.B: Constraints on solution
options.C: Maintenance and logistics report
options.D: Operation records/artifacts

correct_answer_text: Maintenance and logistics report

question: Per Figure 2.54, which item is listed as a typical OUTPUT of the Operation process rather than a control or input?

options.A: Life cycle concepts
options.B: Installed system
options.C: Trained personnel
options.D: Operational system

correct_answer_text: Operational system

question: Which of the following is one of the four high-level activities of the Operation process as enumerated in the SE Handbook?

options.A: Perform logistics support
options.B: Manage results of operation
options.C: Perform disposal
options.D: Conduct system validation

correct_answer_text: Manage results of operation

question: Under "Prepare for operation," what does the SE Handbook direct the practitioner to plan for as part of operational capability build-up?

options.A: Disposal site selection and decommissioning sequencing
options.B: Site deployment schedules, personnel availability, training, and logistic support availability
options.C: Verification test case authoring and unit-level test sequencing
options.D: Supplier selection criteria and contract award procedures

correct_answer_text: Site deployment schedules, personnel availability, training, and logistic support availability

question: Under "Perform operation," what does the SE Handbook state should be done when abnormal operational conditions warrant it?

options.A: Immediately invoke the Disposal process for the affected system elements
options.B: Conduct planned contingency actions and, if necessary, perform system contingency operations
options.C: Suspend the Operation process until a new OpsCon is approved
options.D: Transfer responsibility for the system to the supplier for re-verification

correct_answer_text: Conduct planned contingency actions and, if necessary, perform system contingency operations

question: Under "Manage results of operation," which handbook process is identified as the one to use for managing incidents and problem resolution?

options.A: Risk Management process
options.B: Configuration Management process
options.C: Quality Assurance process
options.D: Measurement process

correct_answer_text: Quality Assurance process

question: What does the SE Handbook say about responsibility for the Operation process versus the supplier's role?

options.A: The supplier owns the Operation process and delegates oversight to the customer
options.B: The customer is responsible for the Operation process, while the supplier should support the customer throughout the system life cycle
options.C: Responsibility is jointly held and undivided between customer and supplier across all activities
options.D: Neither party is responsible until a separate operations agreement is executed

correct_answer_text: The customer is responsible for the Operation process, while the supplier should support the customer throughout the system life cycle

question: Under "Prepare for operation," which of the following life cycle concept artifacts does the SE Handbook list among those the Operation process is intended to influence?

options.A: The ConOps of the receiving organization and the OpsCon of the SoI
options.B: The Work Breakdown Structure and Integrated Master Schedule
options.C: The Systems Engineering Management Plan and Test and Evaluation Master Plan
options.D: The Product Breakdown Structure and Contract Data Requirements List

correct_answer_text: The ConOps of the receiving organization and the OpsCon of the SoI

question: Which statement best reflects the handbook's guidance on whether Operation can drive changes to requirements?

options.A: Operation cannot drive requirement changes; requirements are frozen at delivery
options.B: Operation may identify system requirements and design gaps, and may drive changes augmenting the initial stakeholder and system requirements
options.C: Operation may identify gaps but only the Maintenance process is allowed to change requirements
options.D: Operation drives changes only to the disposal strategy, not to stakeholder or system requirements

correct_answer_text: Operation may identify system requirements and design gaps, and may drive changes augmenting the initial stakeholder and system requirements

question: According to the SE Handbook, what is the ultimate goal of systems engineering with respect to the Operation process?

options.A: Final disposal of the SoI with minimal environmental impact
options.B: Successful operation of the SoI as a part of the operating organization
options.C: Achievement of a verified system baseline at delivery
options.D: Demonstration of the SoI in a representative test environment

correct_answer_text: Successful operation of the SoI as a part of the operating organization

question: Per the SE Handbook, which three handbook technical processes have a clear distinction yet operate together to deliver and sustain services for the operating organization?

options.A: Operation, Maintenance, and Disposal
options.B: Operation (use the system to deliver services), Maintenance (sustain the capability to provide service), and Support (broader stakeholder support across the life cycle)
options.C: Transition, Operation, and Validation
options.D: Operation, Verification, and Quality Assurance

correct_answer_text: Operation (use the system to deliver services), Maintenance (sustain the capability to provide service), and Support (broader stakeholder support across the life cycle)

question: Per Figure 2.54, which of the following is listed as a typical OUTPUT of the Operation process?

options.A: Trained personnel
options.B: Transition report
options.C: Operation report
options.D: Life cycle concepts

correct_answer_text: Operation report

question: Per Figure 2.54, which of the following is listed as a typical CONTROL on the Operation process (as distinct from an input or output)?

options.A: Maintenance and logistics report
options.B: Operation strategy/approach
options.C: Operational system
options.D: Installed system

correct_answer_text: Operation strategy/approach

question: Per Figure 2.54, which of the following is listed as a typical ENABLER for the Operation process?

options.A: Requirements imposed on enabling systems
options.B: Operation procedure
options.C: Operational system
options.D: Installed system

correct_answer_text: Requirements imposed on enabling systems

question: Under 'Prepare for operation,' the handbook directs identification of which categories of external requirements that may constrain operational activities?

options.A: Relevant regulations, legal requirements, environmental and ethical constraints
options.B: Acquisition contract clauses and intellectual-property licensing terms
options.C: International standards for verification and validation methods
options.D: Supplier internal quality management policies

correct_answer_text: Relevant regulations, legal requirements, environmental and ethical constraints

question: Under 'Perform operation,' the SE Handbook directs the operator to track which performance characteristic in particular?

options.A: Mean time to repair only
options.B: Cost performance index against baseline budget
options.C: System performance, including operational availability
options.D: Net promoter score from end users

correct_answer_text: System performance, including operational availability

question: Under 'Manage results of operation,' the SE Handbook directs that bidirectional traceability be maintained for the system's assets, services, and operational capabilities with which other artifacts?

options.A: Contract deliverables, statement of work, and acquisition baseline
options.B: System architecture, design, and system requirements
options.C: Disposal strategy, decommissioning plan, and retirement schedule
options.D: Verification cases, validation cases, and integration plan

correct_answer_text: System architecture, design, and system requirements

question: Under 'Perform operation,' how does the SE Handbook direct the operator to treat the established operating procedures over time?

options.A: Freeze procedures at delivery; any changes require a new acquisition
options.B: Operate per the established procedures and update the procedures as experience accumulates
options.C: Replace procedures with supplier-issued procedures at each release
options.D: Refer all procedure changes to the Maintenance process and do not modify them during operation

correct_answer_text: Operate per the established procedures and update the procedures as experience accumulates

question: Per the 'Common approaches and tips' for the Operation process, when should enabling systems, products, services, and materials needed for operation be identified?

options.A: After the first operational anomaly is reported
options.B: Early in the life cycle, to allow for the necessary lead time to obtain or access them
options.C: Only during the Maintenance process once the system is in steady-state use
options.D: Just-in-time during the operational stage to minimize storage costs

correct_answer_text: Early in the life cycle, to allow for the necessary lead time to obtain or access them

question: Per the Operation Concepts elaboration, the stakeholders' needs and requirements regarding operation constitute a significant source of system requirements and a significant input to which two other processes?

options.A: Acquisition and Supply processes
options.B: Validation and Transition processes
options.C: Configuration Management and Risk Management processes
options.D: Disposal and Maintenance processes

correct_answer_text: Validation and Transition processes

question: Per the Operation Concepts elaboration, what does the SE Handbook say must be done when the operational environment changes and evolves while the system is being developed?

options.A: Defer all updates until after initial operational capability is declared
options.B: Invest considerable effort in recognizing the changes and updating the life cycle concepts (especially ConOps and OpsCon) and all derived requirements
options.C: Reset the development baseline and restart the Concept stage
options.D: Forward the changes to the supplier as part of a contract modification only

correct_answer_text: Invest considerable effort in recognizing the changes and updating the life cycle concepts (especially ConOps and OpsCon) and all derived requirements

question: Per the Operation Concepts elaboration, how should the life cycles of systems that the SoI interfaces with during operation be treated?

options.A: Ignored, since only the SoI's own life cycle is in scope
options.B: Treated as SoIs in their own right whose life cycles must be coordinated with the life cycle of your SoI
options.C: Assumed to follow identical life cycle stages to the SoI
options.D: Considered only when they are part of the same acquisition contract

correct_answer_text: Treated as SoIs in their own right whose life cycles must be coordinated with the life cycle of your SoI

question: Under 'Prepare for operation,' what does the handbook direct the practitioner to verify regarding information products that accompany the SoI?

options.A: That documentation, manuals, and procedures accompany the SoI; identify gaps and initiate changes as necessary
options.B: That all documentation is delivered exclusively in electronic form
options.C: That the supplier owns and maintains all manuals throughout the operational life
options.D: That only the operational concept document is required at delivery

correct_answer_text: That documentation, manuals, and procedures accompany the SoI; identify gaps and initiate changes as necessary

question: Under 'Prepare for operation,' which strategies does the handbook direct the practitioner to review for compatibility with the OpsCon and completeness with respect to expected operational capabilities?

options.A: Acquisition, supply, and contracting strategies
options.B: Transition, validation, and maintenance strategies
options.C: Risk, quality, and configuration management strategies
options.D: Verification, integration, and disposal strategies

correct_answer_text: Transition, validation, and maintenance strategies

question: Categorize each item in the Operation Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.54).
Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 as cited in the handbook, what is the purpose of the Maintenance process?

options.A: To end the existence of a system element for a specified intended use
options.B: To sustain the capability of the system to provide a service
options.C: To use the system to deliver its services to operators and users
options.D: To establish confidence that the system satisfies stakeholder needs in its operational environment

correct_answer_text: To sustain the capability of the system to provide a service

question: According to the Maintenance process description, the process focuses on sustaining the system's ability to provide services and is most often executed in what temporal relationship to the Operation process?

options.A: It precedes Operation as a prerequisite stage
options.B: It is concurrent with the Operation process
options.C: It is performed only after Operation has ended
options.D: It is performed only during the Transition process

correct_answer_text: It is concurrent with the Operation process

question: Per the Description in 2.3.5.13, which set of activities does Maintenance explicitly include to sustain satisfactory quality, performance, and availability of services?

options.A: Stakeholder needs elicitation, requirements definition, and architecture analysis
options.B: Operations support, logistics, and material management
options.C: Verification, validation, and qualification testing
options.D: Configuration audits, baselining, and release engineering

correct_answer_text: Operations support, logistics, and material management

question: In Figure 2.55 (IPO diagram for the Maintenance process), which item is identified as a typical INPUT to the process?

options.A: Maintenance and logistics strategy/approach
options.B: Maintained and sustained system
options.C: Operation report
options.D: Constraints on solution

correct_answer_text: Operation report

question: Per Figure 2.55, which of the following is a typical OUTPUT of the Maintenance process rather than an input or activity?

options.A: Trained personnel
options.B: Operational system
options.C: Perform logistics support
options.D: Maintained and sustained system

correct_answer_text: Maintained and sustained system

question: Which of the following is listed in Figure 2.55 as one of the four Maintenance process Activities?

options.A: Perform operation
options.B: Manage results of maintenance and logistics
options.C: Define stakeholder needs
options.D: Conduct decision analysis

correct_answer_text: Manage results of maintenance and logistics

question: Per the Elaboration in 2.3.5.13, which mode of maintenance is described as restoring system services to normal operations (e.g., remove and replace hardware, reload software, apply a software patch)?

options.A: Preventive maintenance
options.B: Predictive maintenance
options.C: Corrective maintenance
options.D: System modification

correct_answer_text: Corrective maintenance

question: Per the Elaboration, which mode of maintenance prevents failures and malfunctions by scheduling routine maintenance actions to sustain optimal system operational performance?

options.A: Corrective maintenance
options.B: Preventive maintenance
options.C: Predictive maintenance
options.D: System modification

correct_answer_text: Preventive maintenance

question: Per the Elaboration, predictive maintenance is best described as which of the following?

options.A: A reactive form of corrective maintenance triggered only after failure occurs
options.B: A more advanced preventive maintenance that uses data collected during system operations to predict failures and schedule actions in advance
options.C: A form of maintenance that extends the system's useful life by changing the system to sustain capabilities in a changing environment
options.D: A logistics activity focused solely on inventory replenishment of spare parts

correct_answer_text: A more advanced preventive maintenance that uses data collected during system operations to predict failures and schedule actions in advance

question: Per the Elaboration in 2.3.5.13, system modification as a form of maintenance is characterized by which of the following?

options.A: It removes and replaces failed components to restore service
options.B: It schedules routine actions to prevent failures
options.C: It extends the system's useful life by changing the system to sustain existing capabilities in the changing environment, and sometimes includes adding new capabilities (upgrades)
options.D: It ends the existence of a system element after disposal needs are identified

correct_answer_text: It extends the system's useful life by changing the system to sustain existing capabilities in the changing environment, and sometimes includes adding new capabilities (upgrades)

question: Under the 'Perform maintenance' activity, which action is explicitly described as preventing operation disruptions by scheduling repairs and replacements before failures occur, based on operations history or failure prediction?

options.A: Confirming completion of the system transition at the maintenance sites
options.B: Detecting, identifying, and repairing physical and logical damage to the system
options.C: Scheduling repairs and replacements before failures occur based on operations history or failure prediction
options.D: Tracking all maintenance repairs for analysis to identify performance trends

correct_answer_text: Scheduling repairs and replacements before failures occur based on operations history or failure prediction

question: Under 'Manage results of maintenance and logistics,' which handbook process is explicitly referenced for managing incidents and problem resolution captured during maintenance?

options.A: Quality Assurance process
options.B: Risk Management process
options.C: Configuration Management process
options.D: Measurement process

correct_answer_text: Quality Assurance process

question: Per the 'Common approaches and tips' on coordination, why must maintenance of the SoI be coordinated with the maintenance of interoperating and enabling systems?

options.A: Because the SoI's life cycle stages must always be sequential with those of other systems
options.B: Because failure or malfunction of any system can trigger maintenance actions in other systems for technical, organizational, economic, or political reasons
options.C: Because the Disposal process cannot begin until all interoperating systems are retired together
options.D: Because validation evidence from one system is automatically transferable to others

correct_answer_text: Because failure or malfunction of any system can trigger maintenance actions in other systems for technical, organizational, economic, or political reasons

question: Which statement best distinguishes the Maintenance process from the Operation process as presented in the handbook?

options.A: Operation sustains the system's ability to provide services; Maintenance uses the system to deliver services
options.B: Maintenance sustains the system's capability to provide a service (e.g., support, logistics, repairs); Operation uses the system to deliver its services
options.C: Maintenance and Operation are sequential and never executed concurrently
options.D: Maintenance ends the existence of system elements, while Operation transitions them into use

correct_answer_text: Maintenance sustains the system's capability to provide a service (e.g., support, logistics, repairs); Operation uses the system to deliver its services

question: Per the handbook, how should the Maintenance Process (2.3.5.13) be distinguished from the Maintainability quality characteristic addressed elsewhere (e.g., 3.1.8)?

options.A: The Maintenance Process is a design quality characteristic; Maintainability is a life cycle process for repair execution
options.B: The Maintenance Process is a life cycle process that sustains the system's capability to provide a service through support, logistics, and material management activities, whereas Maintainability is a quality characteristic describing the design property that enables maintenance to be performed effectively
options.C: They are synonymous terms used interchangeably in the handbook
options.D: The Maintenance Process applies only to hardware while Maintainability applies only to software

correct_answer_text: The Maintenance Process is a life cycle process that sustains the system's capability to provide a service through support, logistics, and material management activities, whereas Maintainability is a quality characteristic describing the design property that enables maintenance to be performed effectively

question: A fielded radar SoI is supported by an external power-conditioning system (an interoperating/enabling system) that suffers a transformer failure, disrupting radar availability. Per the 'Common approaches and tips' for the Maintenance process, what is the correct interpretation?

options.A: The radar's Maintenance process is independent and need not respond until the radar itself fails
options.B: Maintenance of the SoI must be coordinated with the maintenance of interoperating and enabling systems because failure or malfunction of any system can trigger maintenance actions in others for technical, organizational, economic, or political concerns
options.C: The Disposal process must be initiated because an enabling system has failed
options.D: Maintenance of the radar shifts to the Operation process owner of the power system without coordination

correct_answer_text: Maintenance of the SoI must be coordinated with the maintenance of interoperating and enabling systems because failure or malfunction of any system can trigger maintenance actions in others for technical, organizational, economic, or political concerns

question: A facility manager uses real-time vibration sensor telemetry from pump bearings to forecast bearing wear and schedules a bearing replacement two weeks before the predicted failure date. Per the Elaboration in 2.3.5.13, this is best classified as which mode of maintenance?

options.A: Corrective maintenance
options.B: Preventive maintenance
options.C: Predictive maintenance
options.D: System modification

correct_answer_text: Predictive maintenance

question: An operating organization replaces the legacy GPS receiver in its fielded UAV with a new dual-frequency GNSS receiver to keep the UAV viable in a changed RF/jamming environment. Per the Elaboration's modes of maintenance, this action is best classified as:

options.A: Corrective maintenance (restoring services to normal)
options.B: Preventive maintenance (scheduling routine actions)
options.C: Predictive maintenance (using operations data to forecast failures)
options.D: System modification (changing the system to sustain existing capabilities in a changing environment, possibly with upgrades)

correct_answer_text: System modification (changing the system to sustain existing capabilities in a changing environment, possibly with upgrades)

question: Per the handbook's tripartite distinction between Operation, Maintenance, and the support disciplines, which statement is correct?

options.A: Operation uses the system to deliver services; Maintenance sustains the system's capability to deliver those services (e.g., logistics, material management, repairs); Logistics Engineering supplies the design-time and supply-chain framework that enables maintenance to be feasible and effective
options.B: Operation, Maintenance, and Logistics Engineering are three names for the same life cycle activity
options.C: Maintenance ends the existence of system elements, Operation delivers services, and Logistics replaces both
options.D: Operation is concurrent with Disposal, while Maintenance occurs only after Operation has ended

correct_answer_text: Operation uses the system to deliver services; Maintenance sustains the system's capability to deliver those services (e.g., logistics, material management, repairs); Logistics Engineering supplies the design-time and supply-chain framework that enables maintenance to be feasible and effective

question: Per Figure 2.55, which item is listed as a Control for the Maintenance process (rather than an Input, Enabler, Output, or Activity)?

options.A: Operational system
options.B: Trained personnel
options.C: Maintenance and logistics strategy/approach
options.D: Maintenance and logistics report

correct_answer_text: Maintenance and logistics strategy/approach

question: Per Figure 2.55, which of the following is listed as an Enabler of the Maintenance process?

options.A: Operation report
options.B: Trained personnel
options.C: Maintained and sustained system
options.D: Maintenance and logistics procedure

correct_answer_text: Trained personnel

question: Per Figure 2.55, which of the following is listed as a typical Output of the Maintenance process (other than the maintained and sustained system itself)?

options.A: Life cycle concepts
options.B: Operation report
options.C: Maintenance and logistics records/artifacts
options.D: Trained personnel

correct_answer_text: Maintenance and logistics records/artifacts

question: During on-call duty, a maintenance technician responds to an unplanned alarm and replaces a failed power supply to restore service. Which pair of classifications best describes this action per the handbook's Maintenance process?

options.A: Scheduled maintenance and predictive maintenance
options.B: Unscheduled maintenance and corrective maintenance
options.C: Scheduled maintenance and system modification
options.D: Unscheduled maintenance and preventive maintenance

correct_answer_text: Unscheduled maintenance and corrective maintenance

question: Per the 'Prepare for maintenance and logistics' activity, which of the following is explicitly listed as part of preparing the maintenance and logistics capability?

options.A: Disposing of obsolete system elements in accordance with environmental statutes
options.B: Establishing warranties and licenses (e.g., software, legal) and lines of communication to activate more support when needed
options.C: Conducting validation testing to confirm the system satisfies stakeholder needs
options.D: Delivering services to operators and users at the operational sites

correct_answer_text: Establishing warranties and licenses (e.g., software, legal) and lines of communication to activate more support when needed

question: An anomaly is observed during operations and routed to the maintenance team. Per 'Manage results of maintenance and logistics,' which combination of actions is explicitly required by the handbook?

options.A: Capture and document the incident, investigate, perform corrective actions, and use the Quality Assurance process for managing incidents and problem resolution
options.B: Capture the incident and immediately initiate the Disposal process for the affected element
options.C: Forward the incident to the Operation process owner and take no maintenance action
options.D: Document the incident only; corrective action belongs solely to the Risk Management process

correct_answer_text: Capture and document the incident, investigate, perform corrective actions, and use the Quality Assurance process for managing incidents and problem resolution

question: A program manager is sizing spares inventory and dispatch resources for a fielded system. Which combination of reliability/maintainability metrics (typically covered alongside Maintainability/Logistics Engineering, e.g., 3.1.8) is most relevant to planning Maintenance process resources and assessing availability?

options.A: MOEs (measures of effectiveness) and MOPs (measures of performance) only
options.B: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF), Mean Time To Repair (MTTR), and Mean Down Time (MDT)
options.C: Net present value (NPV), internal rate of return (IRR), and payback period
options.D: Verification yield, validation completeness, and qualification coverage

correct_answer_text: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF), Mean Time To Repair (MTTR), and Mean Down Time (MDT)

question: Per the handbook, maintenance findings may legitimately reach back into engineering activities. Which statement best captures this feedback effect described in 2.3.5.13?

options.A: Maintenance is strictly downstream and cannot influence requirements or design
options.B: Maintenance may identify requirements and design gaps and may drive changes in the SoI, augmenting the initial stakeholder and system requirements
options.C: Maintenance findings are routed only to the Disposal process and never to requirements
options.D: Maintenance findings may change only the operations manual but not the system architecture

correct_answer_text: Maintenance may identify requirements and design gaps and may drive changes in the SoI, augmenting the initial stakeholder and system requirements

question: Per the 'Common approaches and tips' for the Maintenance process, why should the enabling systems, products, services, and materials needed for maintenance and logistics support be identified early in the life cycle?

options.A: To allow the necessary lead time to obtain or access them
options.B: To ensure the Disposal process can begin before Operation
options.C: To replace the need for a maintenance and logistics strategy
options.D: To eliminate the need for trained personnel as an enabler

correct_answer_text: To allow the necessary lead time to obtain or access them

question: Categorize each item in the Maintenance Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.55).

items.text: Manage results of maintenance and logistics

question: Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 as cited in the handbook, what is the purpose of the Disposal process?

options.A: To restore system services to normal operations after a failure or malfunction occurs in the field
options.B: To end the existence of a system element or system for a specified intended use, appropriately handle replaced or retired elements and waste products, and properly attend to identified critical disposal needs
options.C: To transition the verified system to the operational environment and establish the capability to provide the services required of the system
options.D: To sustain the capability of the system to deliver services by performing corrective, preventive, and predictive maintenance actions

correct_answer_text: To end the existence of a system element or system for a specified intended use, appropriately handle replaced or retired elements and waste products, and properly attend to identified critical disposal needs

question: According to the handbook, the Disposal process is conducted in accordance with applicable guidance, policy, regulations, and statutes during which portion of the system life cycle?

options.A: Only during the retirement stage, after operations have permanently ceased
options.B: Only during the production and utilization stages when waste materials are generated
options.C: Throughout the system life cycle
options.D: Only at the exit decision gate of the utilization stage

correct_answer_text: Throughout the system life cycle

question: The handbook states that incremental disposal can be applied at any point in the life cycle. Which of the following is given as an example of such incremental disposal?

options.A: Permanent decommissioning of the entire system of interest at end-of-life
options.B: Prototypes that are not to be reused or evolved, waste materials during manufacturing, and parts that are replaced during maintenance
options.C: Long-term archival of design documentation and configuration baselines
options.D: Transition of operational responsibility from the supplier organization to the acquirer organization

correct_answer_text: Prototypes that are not to be reused or evolved, waste materials during manufacturing, and parts that are replaced during maintenance

question: Beyond ending the existence of a system, the handbook states that the Disposal process may also be used for what additional purpose?

options.A: To establish the initial life cycle concepts for a new system of interest
options.B: To manage the transition of system elements from a current SoI to a different system
options.C: To validate that the operational system meets stakeholder requirements
options.D: To define the acquisition strategy for replacement enabling systems

correct_answer_text: To manage the transition of system elements from a current SoI to a different system

question: Which of the following best reflects the handbook's description of how environmental concerns shape the Disposal process?

options.A: Environmental concerns are addressed only after disposal is complete, by returning the environment to its original state
options.B: Environmental concerns drive the designer to consider reclaiming the materials or recycling them into new systems
options.C: Environmental concerns are handled exclusively by external regulatory agencies, not by the designer
options.D: Environmental concerns apply only to hazardous materials and have no effect on overall system design

correct_answer_text: Environmental concerns drive the designer to consider reclaiming the materials or recycling them into new systems

question: Per the IPO diagram (Figure 2.56) for the Disposal process, which of the following is listed as a Typical Input?

options.A: Disposal strategy/approach
options.B: Disposal procedure
options.C: Maintained and sustained system
options.D: Traceability mapping

correct_answer_text: Maintained and sustained system

question: Per the IPO diagram (Figure 2.56), which set of items is listed as Typical Outputs of the Disposal process?

options.A: Operation report, maintenance and logistics report, and trained personnel
options.B: Life cycle concepts, operational system, and constraints on solution
options.C: Disposal strategy/approach, disposal procedure, disposed system, disposal report, and disposal records/artifacts
options.D: Validated system, qualified system, and verification report

correct_answer_text: Disposal strategy/approach, disposal procedure, disposed system, disposal report, and disposal records/artifacts

question: The handbook groups the Disposal process activities into three high-level activities. Which of the following correctly lists those three activities?

options.A: Plan for retirement, decommission the system, archive documentation
options.B: Prepare for disposal, perform disposal, finalize the disposal
options.C: Identify hazardous materials, dismantle, recycle
options.D: Define disposal requirements, verify disposal, validate disposal

correct_answer_text: Prepare for disposal, perform disposal, finalize the disposal

question: Within the 'Prepare for disposal' activity, which of the following tasks is explicitly identified by the handbook?

options.A: Decommission the system or system elements to be disposed
options.B: Confirm no adverse effects from the disposal activities and return the environment to its original state
options.C: Review the retirement concept (may be called a disposal concept), including any hazardous materials and other environmental impacts to be encountered during disposal
options.D: Extract all elements and waste materials that are no longer needed from storage sites

correct_answer_text: Review the retirement concept (may be called a disposal concept), including any hazardous materials and other environmental impacts to be encountered during disposal

question: During the 'Perform disposal' activity, which of the following is explicitly identified as an action to take when extracting elements and waste materials that are no longer needed?

options.A: Ensure that the waste products or elements not intended for reuse cannot get back into the supply chain
options.B: Specify containment facilities, storage locations, inspection criteria, and storage periods
options.C: Impose associated constraints on the system requirements
options.D: Maintain documentation of all disposal activities and residual hazards

correct_answer_text: Ensure that the waste products or elements not intended for reuse cannot get back into the supply chain

question: Within the 'Finalize the disposal' activity, which two tasks does the handbook identify?

options.A: Decommission the system and disassemble the elements for ease of handling
options.B: Identify reusable elements and specify storage periods for hazardous materials
options.C: Plan for disposal strategy and impose constraints on the system requirements
options.D: Confirm no adverse effects from the disposal activities and return the environment to its original state, and maintain documentation of all disposal activities and residual hazards

correct_answer_text: Confirm no adverse effects from the disposal activities and return the environment to its original state, and maintain documentation of all disposal activities and residual hazards

question: The handbook explicitly notes which regulatory domains the disposal staff must adhere to during the 'Perform disposal' activity?

options.A: Safety, security, privacy and environment regulations or policies
options.B: Financial accounting and procurement regulations only
options.C: Intellectual property and export control regulations only
options.D: Quality management and configuration management policies only

correct_answer_text: Safety, security, privacy and environment regulations or policies

question: Among the 'Common approaches and tips' for Disposal, the handbook recommends designing the SoI to support which concept, with an explicit cross-reference to Section 3.1.10?

options.A: The lean manufacturing model
options.B: The circular economy
options.C: Agile development methods
options.D: Model-based systems engineering

correct_answer_text: The circular economy

question: Which of the following best captures the handbook's distinction between the Disposal process and the Retirement stage?

options.A: Disposal and Retirement are synonyms used interchangeably in ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288
options.B: The Disposal process is a life cycle stage, while Retirement is a technical process applied within that stage
options.C: The Disposal process is a technical process that can be applied incrementally throughout the life cycle (including during retirement), not only during the retirement stage
options.D: Disposal applies only to hardware elements while Retirement applies only to software and personnel

correct_answer_text: The Disposal process is a technical process that can be applied incrementally throughout the life cycle (including during retirement), not only during the retirement stage

question: According to the Elaboration on disposal analyses, which set of factors should be considered in the analyses that support disposal planning?

options.A: Costs (including LCC), disposal sites, environmental impacts, health and safety issues, responsible agencies, handling and shipping, supporting items, and applicable international, national, and local regulations
options.B: Only the direct labor cost of decommissioning and the cost of waste transportation
options.C: Only environmental impacts and emissions, as financial considerations belong to project management
options.D: Only stakeholder agreements and contractual obligations from the Acquisition process

correct_answer_text: Costs (including LCC), disposal sites, environmental impacts, health and safety issues, responsible agencies, handling and shipping, supporting items, and applicable international, national, and local regulations

question: Which international standards series does the handbook explicitly identify at the end of Section 2.3.5.14 as relevant to environmental management systems and life cycle assessment for disposal?

options.A: The ISO 9000 series
options.B: The ISO 14000 (2015) series
options.C: The ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 series
options.D: The ISO 31000 series

correct_answer_text: The ISO 14000 (2015) series

question: Per the handbook's description of the Disposal process, what does the process generate that must be balanced against stakeholders' needs and other design considerations?

options.A: Verification reports and qualification evidence
options.B: Requirements and constraints
options.C: Operational scenarios and use cases
options.D: Maintenance and logistics support plans

correct_answer_text: Requirements and constraints

question: The handbook states that the Disposal process also includes any steps necessary to do which of the following?

options.A: Re-baseline the operational architecture and re-verify all interfaces
options.B: Return the environment to an acceptable condition; handle elements and waste products in an environmentally sound manner; and document and retain records of disposal activities for external oversight or regulatory agencies
options.C: Procure new enabling systems for the replacement SoI and train its operators
options.D: Validate the residual system performance against stakeholder needs

correct_answer_text: Return the environment to an acceptable condition; handle elements and waste products in an environmentally sound manner; and document and retain records of disposal activities for external oversight or regulatory agencies

question: Within the 'Prepare for disposal' activity, the handbook notes that the enabling systems, products, or services required for disposal can be acquired through which set of approaches?

options.A: Rental, procurement, development, reuse, and subcontracting
options.B: Donation, salvage, scrapping, and incineration
options.C: Internal development only, to maintain configuration control
options.D: Lease-to-own and government-furnished equipment only

correct_answer_text: Rental, procurement, development, reuse, and subcontracting

question: Per the handbook's 'Prepare for disposal' activity, what is specified if the system is to be stored?

options.A: Configuration baselines, interface control documents, and acceptance criteria
options.B: Containment facilities, storage locations, inspection criteria, and storage periods
options.C: Verification procedures, validation procedures, and traceability matrices
options.D: Maintenance concepts, logistics support, and spares provisioning

correct_answer_text: Containment facilities, storage locations, inspection criteria, and storage periods

question: During the 'Perform disposal' activity, the handbook explicitly directs that deactivated system elements be disposed of in what manner?

options.A: Donated to museums by default
options.B: Returned to the original supplier for credit
options.C: Disposed of per the disposal procedure
options.D: Stored indefinitely pending future reuse decisions

correct_answer_text: Disposed of per the disposal procedure

question: In addition to adhering to safety, security, privacy and environment regulations, what does the handbook direct the disposal staff to do during the 'Perform disposal' activity?

options.A: Capture their tacit knowledge for future needs
options.B: Re-verify all operational requirements before decommissioning
options.C: Sign non-disclosure agreements regarding disposed materials
options.D: Validate the disposed system against retired stakeholder needs

correct_answer_text: Capture their tacit knowledge for future needs

question: In the 'Common approaches and tips' for Disposal, the handbook notes that items of cultural and historical value have been lost to posterity because what option was not considered during the retirement stage?

options.A: Long-term archival storage of design documentation
options.B: Sale to private collectors at auction
options.C: Donation of an obsolete system to museums and conservatories
options.D: Reprocessing of historical materials into new systems

correct_answer_text: Donation of an obsolete system to museums and conservatories

question: Which concepts does the handbook identify as driving current trends toward corporate social responsibility that influence cleaner production, operational environments, and eventual disposal of depleted materials?

options.A: Lean six sigma and total quality management
options.B: Zero footprint and zero emissions
options.C: Agile and DevOps
options.D: Cost-as-an-independent-variable and design-to-cost

correct_answer_text: Zero footprint and zero emissions

question: Per the Elaboration in Section 2.3.5.14, which set of methods should be addressed when the project team develops solutions for disposition of the system, elements, and waste products?

options.A: Decommissioning, decontamination, and demilitarization
options.B: Refactoring, regression testing, and re-baselining
options.C: Storing, dismantling, reusing, recycling, reprocessing, and destroying systems, elements, materials, consumables, waste, and enabling systems
options.D: Acquiring, integrating, transitioning, and operating replacement systems

correct_answer_text: Storing, dismantling, reusing, recycling, reprocessing, and destroying systems, elements, materials, consumables, waste, and enabling systems

question: According to the Elaboration in Section 2.3.5.14, how should disposal analyses be treated over the life cycle?

options.A: Completed once at the concept stage and frozen thereafter to preserve traceability
options.B: Performed only after the retirement decision gate is reached
options.C: Readdressed to consider design and project impacts from changing laws and regulations throughout the life cycle
options.D: Delegated entirely to external regulatory agencies after operations begin

correct_answer_text: Readdressed to consider design and project impacts from changing laws and regulations throughout the life cycle

question: Per Figure 2.56, which of the following items is listed among the Typical Outputs of the Disposal process (as opposed to a Typical Input)?

options.A: Life cycle concepts
options.B: Operational system
options.C: Constraints on solution and requirements imposed on enabling systems
options.D: Operation report and maintenance and logistics report

correct_answer_text: Constraints on solution and requirements imposed on enabling systems

question: Within the 'Prepare for disposal' activity, the handbook notes that special methods may need to be implemented for which type of materials when identifying elements that can or cannot be reused?

options.A: Software components and firmware
options.B: Hazardous materials
options.C: COTS components and licensed IP
options.D: Personnel and trained operators

correct_answer_text: Hazardous materials

question: Per the Elaboration in Section 2.3.5.14, how does the handbook characterize the maintenance of materials in closed loops, with reference to McDonough (2013)?

options.A: It maximizes material value without damaging ecosystems
options.B: It minimizes life cycle cost by deferring disposal expenses to end-of-life
options.C: It eliminates the need for environmental management standards
options.D: It replaces the need for incremental disposal during manufacturing

correct_answer_text: It maximizes material value without damaging ecosystems

question: Per the 'Perform disposal' activity, which action does the handbook explicitly identify with respect to elements once the system has been decommissioned?

options.A: Re-baseline the configuration and update the verification artifacts
options.B: Disassemble the elements for ease of handling, including identification and processing of reusable elements
options.C: Return all elements to the original supplier of record
options.D: Transition the elements to operational use in a new SoI before disassembly

correct_answer_text: Disassemble the elements for ease of handling, including identification and processing of reusable elements

question: Categorize each item in the Disposal Process IPO diagram (Figure 2.56).
Per ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 (2023) as cited by the handbook, a Quality Characteristic (QC) is best defined as:

options.A: A measurable functional requirement allocated to a system element
options.B: An inherent characteristic of a product, process, or system related to a requirement
options.C: A verification activity performed during the operations stage
options.D: A stakeholder preference captured only in marketing materials

correct_answer_text: An inherent characteristic of a product, process, or system related to a requirement

question: In previous editions of the INCOSE SE Handbook, what were the QC approaches known as?

options.A: Lifecycle Cost Engineering
options.B: Mission Assurance Disciplines
options.C: Specialty Engineering or the Engineering Specialties
options.D: Functional Engineering Disciplines

correct_answer_text: Specialty Engineering or the Engineering Specialties

question: Why are Quality Characteristics informally referred to as the "-ilities"?

options.A: Because they are always quantified using probability theory
options.B: Because many, but not all, end in "ility" in the English language
options.C: Because they are derived from ISO 9001 quality standards
options.D: Because they only apply to integration and test activities

correct_answer_text: Because many, but not all, end in "ility" in the English language

question: What type of requirements do QC approaches most often generate?

options.A: Functional requirements only
options.B: Derived performance requirements only
options.C: Non-functional requirements (though some such as safety, security, and resilience may also generate functional requirements)
options.D: Interface control requirements only

correct_answer_text: Non-functional requirements (though some such as safety, security, and resilience may also generate functional requirements)

question: Other than "Specialty Engineering" and "-ilities," what additional names does the handbook give for QC approaches?

options.A: Quality Assurance and Control (QAC)
options.B: Design for X (DFX) and Through-Life Considerations
options.C: Verification & Validation Engineering
options.D: Total Quality Management (TQM)

correct_answer_text: Design for X (DFX) and Through-Life Considerations

question: According to Section 3.1.1, how are QC approaches characterized with respect to the system life cycle?

options.A: They are applied only during the concept stage to set requirements
options.B: They are applied only during verification and validation
options.C: They are life cycle perspectives applied throughout the system's life cycle, including production, utilization, support, and retirement
options.D: They are confined to the development stage and handed off at delivery

correct_answer_text: They are life cycle perspectives applied throughout the system's life cycle, including production, utilization, support, and retirement

question: Beyond the engineered system itself, what additional scope must be considered when applying QC approaches?

options.A: Only the SoI's internal subsystems
options.B: Only the development organization's processes
options.C: The system, SoS, or enterprise it is part of, plus its interoperating and enabling systems
options.D: Only the customer's contractual requirements

correct_answer_text: The system, SoS, or enterprise it is part of, plus its interoperating and enabling systems

question: Why are the QC approaches in Section 3.1 presented in alphabetical order?

options.A: To match the order of the ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 process groups
options.B: To avoid giving more weight to one QC approach over another
options.C: To reflect the typical sequence of application across the life cycle
options.D: To match the order in which they were historically introduced

correct_answer_text: To avoid giving more weight to one QC approach over another

question: Per Table 3.1, which QC approach is described as one that "enables change in a timely and cost-effective manner" with representative QCs including Adaptability, Evolvability, Flexibility, and Scalability?

options.A: Resilience Engineering
options.B: Agility Engineering
options.C: Interoperability Analysis
options.D: Sustainability Engineering

correct_answer_text: Agility Engineering

question: Per Table 3.1, which QC approach is associated with representative QCs of Resilience, Robustness, and Survivability?

options.A: System Safety Engineering
options.B: System Security Engineering
options.C: Resilience Engineering
options.D: Reliability, Availability, Maintainability Engineering

correct_answer_text: Resilience Engineering

question: Per Table 3.1, which QC approach "supports the circular economy over its life" with representative QCs of Disposability, Environmental Impact, and Sustainability?

options.A: Logistics Engineering
options.B: Manufacturability/Producibility Analysis
options.C: Affordability Analysis
options.D: Sustainability Engineering

correct_answer_text: Sustainability Engineering

question: According to Section 3.1.1, what does the handbook recommend regarding the personnel who conduct QC approaches?

options.A: The chief systems engineer should perform all QC approaches personally
options.B: Subject matter experts should be consulted and assigned as appropriate
options.C: Only ISO-certified quality auditors may conduct QC approaches
options.D: QC approaches should be deferred to the customer after delivery

correct_answer_text: Subject matter experts should be consulted and assigned as appropriate

question: Will every QC approach in Table 3.1 apply to every system?

options.A: Yes — all QC approaches are mandatory for every SoI
options.B: No — not every QC approach will be applicable to every system or every application domain
options.C: Yes, but only for safety-critical systems
options.D: Only the four "-ility" approaches are universally required

correct_answer_text: No — not every QC approach will be applicable to every system or every application domain

question: From Table 3.1, drag each Quality Characteristic approach to its handbook section / category.
Per the INCOSE SE Handbook, which statement BEST captures the formal definition of system affordability?

options.A: Minimizing the acquisition cost of a system while meeting threshold performance requirements
options.B: The balance of system performance, cost, and schedule constraints over the system life while satisfying mission needs in concert with strategic investment and organizational needs
options.C: The ratio of system effectiveness to the sum of initial cost and sustainment cost
options.D: An estimate of total cost of ownership produced early in the life cycle to inform contract award decisions

correct_answer_text: The balance of system performance, cost, and schedule constraints over the system life while satisfying mission needs in concert with strategic investment and organizational needs

question: According to the handbook, Affordability Analysis is an approach that:

options.A: Maximizes value, providing cost-effective capability over the entire life cycle
options.B: Enables change in a timely and cost-effective manner
options.C: Enables production in a responsible and cost-effective manner
options.D: Ensures the system interacts effectively with other systems

correct_answer_text: Maximizes value, providing cost-effective capability over the entire life cycle

question: Blanchard and Fabrycky observe that many systems are planned, designed, produced, and operated with little initial concern for affordability. Which sequencing error do they identify as typical?

options.A: Economic aspects are usually considered first, with the technical aspects deferred until later
options.B: Schedule constraints are addressed first, with cost aspects deferred until later
options.C: The technical aspects are usually considered first, with the economic aspects deferred until later
options.D: Stakeholder needs are addressed first, with both technical and economic aspects deferred until later

correct_answer_text: The technical aspects are usually considered first, with the economic aspects deferred until later

question: Which of the following is identified by the handbook as a KEY affordability concept?

options.A: Affordability is always equivalent to minimizing acquisition cost across a portfolio
options.B: Accountability (system governance) for affordability needs to be assigned across the life cycle, including stakeholders from the various contextual domains
options.C: Affordability decisions are best deferred until detailed design data is available
options.D: Affordability analysis should be limited to the boundaries of the system of interest only

correct_answer_text: Accountability (system governance) for affordability needs to be assigned across the life cycle, including stakeholders from the various contextual domains

question: The handbook warns that the term 'affordability' is contextually sensitive. What practical consequence does it cite?

options.A: It eliminates the need for an affordability process or framework
options.B: It is significant when writing requirements or comparing two solutions in an affordability trade study, and often leads to misunderstanding and incompatible perspectives on what an 'affordable system' is
options.C: It means affordability analysis can be conducted without stakeholder accountability
