options.D: Systems engineering (SE) and reliability engineering

correct_answer_text: Mechanical engineering (ME) for mechanical elements and electrical engineering (EE) for electrical and electronic elements

question: Which example does the SE Handbook give of "key physical measures" that the SE team must help establish and track at every level of the system architecture when working with the hardware team?

options.A: Mean time between failures (MTBF) and availability budgets
options.B: Lines of code (LOC) and cyclomatic complexity budgets
options.C: Size, weight, and power (SWaP) budgets
options.D: Schedule, cost, and scope budgets

correct_answer_text: Size, weight, and power (SWaP) budgets

question: Per the SE Handbook, how does the SE team typically derive critical hardware attributes across all applicable hardware elements and design teams when full element-level values are not yet known?

options.A: By deferring all allocation until detailed design is complete
options.B: By delegating allocation entirely to each hardware design team
options.C: By using a requirements budget to allocate values from system-level requirements
options.D: By selecting commercial off-the-shelf hardware to bound the values

correct_answer_text: By using a requirements budget to allocate values from system-level requirements

question: Which hardware considerations does Section 5.3.2 explicitly call out, in addition to size, weight, and power (SWaP)?

options.A: Latency, bandwidth, and throughput
options.B: Inertia, balance, frequency, and phase
options.C: Cohesion, coupling, and modularity
options.D: Reusability, portability, and scalability

correct_answer_text: Inertia, balance, frequency, and phase

question: According to the SE Handbook, what benefit does utilizing open source or standard interfaces between SE and HWE provide, when feasible?

options.A: It eliminates the need for interface management entirely
options.B: It guarantees a successful make decision over a buy decision
options.C: It removes the need to document interfaces in the architecture
options.D: It can reduce costs and development time

correct_answer_text: It can reduce costs and development time

question: Per Section 5.3.2, if unique or custom interfaces between hardware elements are necessary, what must occur before design, development, and testing can proceed?

options.A: They must be documented in the architecture
options.B: They must be prototyped on the target hardware
options.C: They must be approved by the project manager
options.D: They must be benchmarked against an open standard

correct_answer_text: They must be documented in the architecture

question: The SE Handbook states that the application of SE to hardware differs from software-only applications in two primary ways. Which statement best captures one of those differences?

options.A: Hardware requirements can always be iterated into the design after delivery, while software cannot
options.B: Hardware performance requirements generally must be built into the initial hardware deliverable rather than iterated into the design (with some exceptions)
options.C: Hardware never requires a make-or-buy decision because solutions are always custom built
options.D: Hardware architecture decomposition continues regardless of whether a make or buy decision is reached

correct_answer_text: Hardware performance requirements generally must be built into the initial hardware deliverable rather than iterated into the design (with some exceptions)

question: Per the SE Handbook, on what basis is a make-or-buy decision typically performed at each level of the architecture during HWE?

options.A: On the preferences of the hardware design team lead
options.B: On the availability of in-house manufacturing capacity
options.C: On the results of a market analysis and tradeoff study of existing solutions that will satisfy the requirements
options.D: On a fixed organizational policy that favors buying over making

correct_answer_text: On the results of a market analysis and tradeoff study of existing solutions that will satisfy the requirements

question: According to Section 5.3.2, what is the effect on architecture decomposition when the result of a make-or-buy decision is to buy?

options.A: Architecture decomposition continues at a finer level of detail
options.B: Architecture decomposition for that system element will end
options.C: The Implementation process is initiated for that element
options.D: The element is moved to the software architecture

correct_answer_text: Architecture decomposition for that system element will end

question: Per the SE Handbook, what role does the SE team play with respect to the hardware team's tracking of allocated budgets?

options.A: The SE team only sets initial budgets and is uninvolved thereafter
options.B: The SE team tracks progress to budgets, determines impacts at the system level, and relays change requests as needed
options.C: The hardware design team independently absorbs any budget overruns without notifying SE
options.D: Budget tracking is delegated entirely to project management with no SE involvement

correct_answer_text: The SE team tracks progress to budgets, determines impacts at the system level, and relays change requests as needed

question: According to the Project Management Institute (PMI), Project Management (PM) is defined as:

options.A: The scientific process of transforming data into insights using advanced analytical methods to make better decisions
options.B: The application of knowledge, skills, tools, and techniques to project activities to meet the project requirements
options.C: The act of enabling people to produce results or achieve outcomes they would not have on their own
options.D: The application of a systematic, disciplined, quantifiable approach to the development, operation, and maintenance of projects

correct_answer_text: The application of knowledge, skills, tools, and techniques to project activities to meet the project requirements

question: Which set of activities does PM include, per Section 5.3.3 of the INCOSE SE Handbook?

options.A: Initiating, planning, executing, monitoring, and closing projects
options.B: Conceiving, producing, utilizing, supporting, and retiring projects
options.C: Defining, designing, developing, deploying, and decommissioning projects
options.D: Analyzing, architecting, allocating, assuring, and auditing projects

correct_answer_text: Initiating, planning, executing, monitoring, and closing projects

question: Within the INCOSE SE Handbook, PM activities are primarily distributed across which group of processes for the SE portion of the responsibilities?

options.A: The Technical Processes
options.B: The Agreement Processes
options.C: The Organizational Project Enabling Processes
options.D: The Technical Management Processes

correct_answer_text: The Technical Management Processes

question: According to the PMBoK, a program is best defined as:

options.A: A single, large-scale project that requires coordinated management
options.B: A set of related projects that are viewed as an entity that requires coordinated management
options.C: An ongoing operational activity within an organization's portfolio
options.D: A collection of unrelated projects managed by the same project manager

correct_answer_text: A set of related projects that are viewed as an entity that requires coordinated management

question: In 2011, INCOSE formed a strategic alliance with PMI and which other organization to research and advance the integration of PM and SE disciplines?

options.A: ISO/IEC/IEEE
options.B: The Project Management Institute's research arm
options.C: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
options.D: The Defense Acquisition University (DAU)

correct_answer_text: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)

question: According to Figure 5.4 'The intersection between PM and SE', what reference is used for the SE Responsibilities side?

options.B: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288, 2023
options.C: PMBoK, 2017
options.D: INCOSE SECF, 2018

correct_answer_text: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288, 2023

question: As stated in Rebentisch (2017), what is the primary difference in deliverables between PM and SE?

options.A: PM delivers technical excellence, while SE delivers schedule performance
options.B: PM delivers the sustainable benefits of the overall project, while SE delivers the technical aspect of the project
options.C: PM delivers stakeholder satisfaction, while SE delivers regulatory compliance
options.D: PM delivers budgetary outcomes, while SE delivers manufacturing readiness

correct_answer_text: PM delivers the sustainable benefits of the overall project, while SE delivers the technical aspect of the project

question: Which of the following BEST characterizes a high degree of integration between PM and SE?

options.A: Strict separation of programmatic and technical responsibilities to avoid scope creep
options.B: The project manager owning all decisions while the SE practitioner provides recommendations
options.C: Collaborative decision making, shared responsibility toward a common goal, and cross-involvement in technical and programmatic aspects
options.D: Use of identical methods, tools, and techniques by both disciplines to eliminate cultural differences

correct_answer_text: Collaborative decision making, shared responsibility toward a common goal, and cross-involvement in technical and programmatic aspects

question: In the early life cycle stages, what is the focus of the SE practitioner versus the project manager?

options.A: The SE practitioner focuses on overall project performance; the project manager focuses on technical details
options.B: The SE practitioner focuses on technical details of the SoI, verification, and validation; the project manager focuses on overall project performance and delivery of benefits, including high-level finance and budgetary requirements
options.C: Both focus exclusively on stakeholder requirements with no division of responsibilities
options.D: The SE practitioner focuses on procurement and contracts; the project manager focuses on architecture and design

correct_answer_text: The SE practitioner focuses on technical details of the SoI, verification, and validation; the project manager focuses on overall project performance and delivery of benefits, including high-level finance and budgetary requirements

question: On which artifact should PM and SE cooperate concurrently during development, according to Section 5.3.3?

options.A: The breakdown structures, including their management through the life cycle
options.B: The verification and validation plan only
options.C: The procurement and contracting documents only
options.D: The marketing and stakeholder communications plan

correct_answer_text: The breakdown structures, including their management through the life cycle

question: Regarding stakeholder focus, which statement BEST captures the typical difference between project managers and SE practitioners?

options.A: Project managers and SE practitioners both focus exclusively on system stakeholders
options.B: Project managers tend to focus on system stakeholders, while SE practitioners tend to focus on project stakeholders
options.C: Project managers tend to focus on project stakeholders, while SE practitioners tend to focus on system stakeholders
options.D: Neither role considers stakeholders directly; that responsibility is delegated to marketing

correct_answer_text: Project managers tend to focus on project stakeholders, while SE practitioners tend to focus on system stakeholders

question: Per the Rebentisch (2017) reference, what specific outcomes are achieved by an integrated PM-SE team?

options.A: Reduced documentation overhead, faster procurement cycles, and lower training costs
options.B: Rapid and effective decision making, effective collaborative work, and effective information sharing
options.C: Standardized tool selection, unified terminology, and identical performance reviews
options.D: Elimination of all programmatic risk and complete avoidance of technical debt

correct_answer_text: Rapid and effective decision making, effective collaborative work, and effective information sharing

question: What happens when there is NOT effective integration between PM and SE, according to Section 5.3.3?

options.A: Productive tension emerges, which generally drives better outcomes through healthy competition
options.B: Unproductive tension emerges between the two disciplines, producing conflict and working at cross purposes with project success
options.C: The project manager automatically assumes all SE responsibilities
options.D: The project proceeds normally but with slightly elevated documentation burdens

correct_answer_text: Unproductive tension emerges between the two disciplines, producing conflict and working at cross purposes with project success

question: Why does the INCOSE SE Handbook emphasize the maturity level of each (PM and SE) party?

options.A: Because only mature SE organizations can deliver value; PM maturity is irrelevant
options.B: Because only mature PM organizations can deliver value; SE maturity is irrelevant
options.C: Because an immature or inexperienced PM organization can render ineffective a mature, high-performing SE organization (and vice versa)
options.D: Because maturity assessments are required by ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 for contract award

correct_answer_text: Because an immature or inexperienced PM organization can render ineffective a mature, high-performing SE organization (and vice versa)

question: Per Bidanda (2022), as cited in Section 5.3.4, Industrial Engineering is BEST defined as:

options.A: The scientific process of transforming data into insights using advanced analytical methods to make better decisions
options.B: Optimizing the utilization of human resources, facilities, equipment, tools, technologies, information, and handling of materials to produce quality products and services safely and cost-effectively considering the needs of customers and employers
options.C: The application of knowledge, skills, tools, and techniques to project activities to meet project requirements
options.D: The application of a systematic, disciplined, quantifiable approach to the development, operation, and maintenance of software

correct_answer_text: Optimizing the utilization of human resources, facilities, equipment, tools, technologies, information, and handling of materials to produce quality products and services safely and cost-effectively considering the needs of customers and employers

question: According to Section 5.3.4, the Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers (IISE) Industrial and SE Body of Knowledge (ISEBoK) (2022) is composed of how many knowledge areas?
Per the IISE statement quoted in Section 5.3.4, Industrial and Systems Engineering is concerned with the design, improvement, and installation of integrated systems of:

options.A: Software, hardware, firmware, and middleware components
options.B: Stakeholder needs, requirements, architecture, and verification artifacts
options.C: People, materials, information, equipment, and energy
options.D: Projects, programs, portfolios, and enterprises

correct_answer_text: People, materials, information, equipment, and energy

question: In Figure 5.5 'IE and SE relationships,' which knowledge area is shown as predominantly the responsibility of Industrial Engineering (IE) rather than SE?

options.A: Systems Verification
options.B: Work Design and Measurement
options.C: Stakeholder Needs and Requirements
options.D: System Architecture (Logical and Physical)

correct_answer_text: Work Design and Measurement

question: Section 5.3.4 describes Operations Research (OR) as including a variety of techniques to quantify and improve the efficiency of systems and organizational processes. According to the text, how do SE practitioners typically engage with OR?

options.A: SE practitioners avoid OR because it falls strictly within the IE domain
options.B: SE practitioners only commission OR studies but never perform them
options.C: SE practitioners can perform OR analyses or use OR studies performed by others to make system decisions
options.D: SE practitioners replace OR analyses with model-based systems engineering (MBSE) techniques

correct_answer_text: SE practitioners can perform OR analyses or use OR studies performed by others to make system decisions

question: Per Section 5.3.4, which of the following is identified as a mathematical technique used within Operations Research and Analysis?

options.A: Failure Modes and Effects Analysis (FMEA)
options.B: Functional Flow Block Diagrams
options.C: Linear programming, network flows, and queuing systems
options.D: Earned Value Management

correct_answer_text: Linear programming, network flows, and queuing systems

question: According to Section 5.3.4, Information Engineering (as cited from Gartner, 2022) is best described as:

options.A: A methodology for developing an integrated information system based on the sharing of common data, with emphasis on decision support needs as well as transaction-processing (TP) requirements
options.B: The application of cybersecurity controls to protect information assets across the system life cycle
options.C: The discipline of producing technical documentation and user manuals for engineered systems
options.D: The use of authoritative sources of system data and models as a continuum across disciplines

correct_answer_text: A methodology for developing an integrated information system based on the sharing of common data, with emphasis on decision support needs as well as transaction-processing (TP) requirements

question: Per Section 5.3.4, what reference does the ISEBoK (2022) cite for the System Design and Engineering knowledge area?

options.A: The INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook
options.B: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288
options.C: The PMI PMBoK
options.D: The SEBoK (2022)

correct_answer_text: The SEBoK (2022)

question: Section 5.3.4 lists topics within Product Design and Development from the ISEBoK perspective. Which of the following is included among those topics?

options.A: Configuration management, baselining, and change control
options.B: Design process; preliminary and detailed design; verification and testing; life cycle costing; and risk and opportunity management
options.C: Mission engineering, business analysis, and stakeholder needs elicitation
options.D: Disposal, retirement, and end-of-life material recovery

correct_answer_text: Design process; preliminary and detailed design; verification and testing; life cycle costing; and risk and opportunity management

question: Regarding how IE impacts/is impacted by SE, Section 5.3.4 cautions that:

options.A: IE has fully superseded SE for industrial and manufacturing systems
options.B: Different organizations use different job titles for IE and SE, and the same job title may have different responsibilities in different organizations, so IE and SE practitioners must work closely together
options.C: IE practitioners should report to SE leadership to avoid misalignment
options.D: SE and IE practitioners should maintain strict separation of duties to prevent overlap

correct_answer_text: Different organizations use different job titles for IE and SE, and the same job title may have different responsibilities in different organizations, so IE and SE practitioners must work closely together

question: Per INFORMS (2022), as cited in the SE Handbook, how is Operations Research (OR) defined?

options.A: The application of engineering principles to the optimization of industrial production lines
options.B: The scientific process of transforming data into insights using advanced analytical methods to make better decisions
options.C: The use of statistical sampling to validate manufacturing quality across the system life cycle
options.D: A discipline that designs information systems based on the sharing of common data and decision support needs

correct_answer_text: The scientific process of transforming data into insights using advanced analytical methods to make better decisions

question: According to the SE Handbook, from what historical context did the name 'operations research' originate?

options.A: The post-World War II rise of industrial engineering in American manufacturing
options.B: The successful use of scientific and mathematical modeling techniques for military operations during World War II
options.C: The development of linear programming by economists during the Great Depression
options.D: The establishment of the Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS) in the 1990s

correct_answer_text: The successful use of scientific and mathematical modeling techniques for military operations during World War II

question: The SE Handbook describes OR as a branch of applied mathematics that includes a wide range of approaches. What is common to all OR approaches?

options.A: The use of stochastic discrete event simulation to represent system behavior
options.B: The application of linear programming to determine optimal decision variables
options.C: The establishment of a model to represent the system to support analysis for decision making
options.D: The reliance on historical test data to forecast future system performance

correct_answer_text: The establishment of a model to represent the system to support analysis for decision making

question: In the SE Handbook's discussion of OR optimization, when is goal programming particularly useful?

options.A: When the system model can be solved directly without iteration
options.B: When thresholds are established as achievement targets on multiple objectives, allowing broader trade-off analysis
options.C: When random numbers are drawn from distributions to represent input uncertainties
options.D: When the objective function and constraints are strictly nonlinear

correct_answer_text: When thresholds are established as achievement targets on multiple objectives, allowing broader trade-off analysis

question: Per the SE Handbook, stochastic discrete event simulations used in OR are often called Monte Carlo simulations because:

options.A: They originated at the Monte Carlo research facility during World War II
options.B: They use deterministic algorithms to produce a single best estimate of system performance
options.C: Random numbers are drawn to represent outcomes from distributions for the input uncertainties
options.D: They rely on linear programming techniques to solve the underlying optimization problem

correct_answer_text: Random numbers are drawn to represent outcomes from distributions for the input uncertainties

question: How does the SE Handbook describe decision analysis as an OR approach?

options.A: It uses random sampling to generate aggregate output probabilities from many simulation runs
options.B: It uses a model of the decision makers' preferences and risk attitudes to analyze tradeoffs between alternatives, and is often used for strategic decision making
options.C: It uses linear programming to determine the best combination of decision variables under deterministic constraints
options.D: It uses queueing theory to assess system processing times under stochastic arrival patterns

correct_answer_text: It uses a model of the decision makers' preferences and risk attitudes to analyze tradeoffs between alternatives, and is often used for strategic decision making

question: According to the SE Handbook, which OR technique is specifically described as being used to elicit stakeholder needs and preferences to construct a value model?

options.A: Network flow analysis
options.B: Queueing theory
options.C: Decision analysis
options.D: Forecasting

correct_answer_text: Decision analysis

question: Per the SE Handbook, which OR technique would most appropriately be used to understand system processing times?

options.A: Queueing theory
options.B: Optimization with inventory theory
options.C: Network flow analysis
options.D: Response surface methodology

correct_answer_text: Queueing theory

question: The SE Handbook cites Blanchard and Fabrycky (2011) on the SE morphology for product (system) realization. Which steps following design synthesis often rely heavily on OR?

options.A: Requirements Allocation and Functional Decomposition
options.B: Verification and Validation
options.C: Estimation and Prediction, and Design Evaluation
options.D: Stakeholder Needs Elicitation and Concept Definition

correct_answer_text: Estimation and Prediction, and Design Evaluation

question: According to the SE Handbook, what is the role of an SE practitioner with respect to OR analyses?

options.A: SE practitioners must always delegate OR analyses to dedicated industrial engineers
options.B: SE practitioners can perform OR analyses themselves or use OR studies performed by others to make system decisions
options.C: SE practitioners use OR only for software-intensive systems, not hardware systems
options.D: SE practitioners apply OR exclusively during the production stage, not earlier life cycle stages

correct_answer_text: SE practitioners can perform OR analyses themselves or use OR studies performed by others to make system decisions

question: Per the SE Handbook, which of the following best describes how optimization is used in OR to support SE?

options.A: It is applied only during the design synthesis stage and never reapplied later in the life cycle
options.B: It is restricted to linear objective functions and convex constraint sets
options.C: It is useful in determining the best combination of decision variables, and may be linear, integer, or nonlinear depending on the form of the objective function and constraints
options.D: It is used primarily to elicit stakeholder preferences for strategic decisions

correct_answer_text: It is useful in determining the best combination of decision variables, and may be linear, integer, or nonlinear depending on the form of the objective function and constraints

question: According to the SE Handbook, decision analysis is fully defensible because it is:

options.A: Based on Monte Carlo simulation with statistically aggregated outputs
options.B: Transparent and traceable, allowing representation of differing preferences of various stakeholders
options.C: Constrained to a single objective function with deterministic inputs
options.D: Validated by historical time series data through forecasting methods

correct_answer_text: Transparent and traceable, allowing representation of differing preferences of various stakeholders

question: According to the US Defense Acquisition University (DAU) Glossary cited in the handbook, Digital Engineering is BEST defined as:

options.A: A document-based approach that converts paper artifacts into PDF format for distribution across the life cycle.
options.B: An integrated digital approach that uses authoritative sources of systems' data and models as a continuum across disciplines to support life cycle activities from concept through retirement.
options.C: The exclusive application of SysML modeling to the system architecture and design definition processes.
options.D: A methodology for digitizing only the verification and validation activities at the end of the system life cycle.

correct_answer_text: An integrated digital approach that uses authoritative sources of systems' data and models as a continuum across disciplines to support life cycle activities from concept through retirement.

question: Which statement BEST describes the relationship between Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) and Digital Engineering in the handbook?

options.A: MBSE and Digital Engineering are synonymous terms referring to the same scope of activity.
options.B: Digital Engineering is a narrower subset of MBSE focused only on SysML diagrams.
options.C: MBSE is one of the core elements of Digital Engineering, with Digital Engineering being the broader, crosscutting approach.
options.D: MBSE replaces Digital Engineering once an enterprise completes digital transformation.

correct_answer_text: MBSE is one of the core elements of Digital Engineering, with Digital Engineering being the broader, crosscutting approach.

question: In MBSE and Digital Engineering, what is the Digital System Model?

options.A: A single monolithic SysML file containing the entire system definition.
options.B: A digital representation of a system, composed of a federated set of models that serve as an authoritative source of truth (ASOT) for the system's design.
options.C: A physical prototype that has been scanned into a CAD environment.
options.D: The collection of textual requirements stored in a requirements management database.

correct_answer_text: A digital representation of a system, composed of a federated set of models that serve as an authoritative source of truth (ASOT) for the system's design.

question: How does the handbook define a digital twin?

options.A: A redundant backup copy of the engineering data repository.
options.B: A static snapshot of the system architecture taken at preliminary design review.
options.C: A digital surrogate of the system, incorporating models to emulate the actual system or some of its elements, which evolves through the life cycle with the mission and definition of the system.
options.D: A set of interconnected cross-discipline model data that expedite the controlled interplay of digital artifacts.

correct_answer_text: A digital surrogate of the system, incorporating models to emulate the actual system or some of its elements, which evolves through the life cycle with the mission and definition of the system.

question: How does the handbook define a digital thread?

options.A: A digital surrogate of the system that emulates the actual system or some of its elements.
options.B: A set of interconnected, cross-discipline model data that seamlessly expedite the controlled interplay of digital artifacts to inform decision makers throughout a system's life cycle.
options.C: The single SysML diagram that connects requirements to verification.
options.D: A communication protocol used between MBSE tools and PLM systems.

correct_answer_text: A set of interconnected, cross-discipline model data that seamlessly expedite the controlled interplay of digital artifacts to inform decision makers throughout a system's life cycle.

question: The handbook describes the Digital System Model as serving as an authoritative source of truth (ASOT). What does ASOT mean in this context?

options.A: A single proprietary software tool licensed by the enterprise for all engineering activities.
options.B: An offline archive of legacy paper documents preserved for compliance audits.
options.C: The federated set of authoritative MBSE and other Digital Engineering technical data and associated artifacts that defines all aspects of the system throughout the life cycle.
options.D: The contractually approved baseline schedule maintained by the project manager.

correct_answer_text: The federated set of authoritative MBSE and other Digital Engineering technical data and associated artifacts that defines all aspects of the system throughout the life cycle.

question: According to the handbook, what does Digital Engineering leverage in order to enable digital threads and digital twins?

options.A: Cloud storage and blockchain ledgers.
options.B: MBSE and the Digital System Model.
options.C: Paper-based configuration control boards.
options.D: Agile sprint backlogs and burndown charts.

correct_answer_text: MBSE and the Digital System Model.

question: Which of the following BEST captures the emphasis the handbook places on Digital Engineering?

options.A: Minimizing reliance on models in favor of physical prototyping at every life cycle stage.
options.B: Limiting model use to the systems engineering discipline only, excluding other engineering disciplines.
options.C: Continuity of models and their use across the life cycle, maximizing the use of models and the computer, consistent and rigorous engineering, strong data management, and continuous improvement through technologic innovation.
options.D: Replacing all engineering processes with automated AI-generated artifacts requiring no human review.

correct_answer_text: Continuity of models and their use across the life cycle, maximizing the use of models and the computer, consistent and rigorous engineering, strong data management, and continuous improvement through technologic innovation.

question: Per the handbook, what must accompany the Digital Engineering supporting infrastructure and environment for it to succeed?

options.A: A capable workforce and culture that is committed to working in accordance with process, following methods, and using tools the organization supplies them.
options.B: An outsourced contractor team that owns all of the organization's models.
options.C: A separate Digital Engineering department that operates independently of mainstream engineering.
options.D: An exclusively cloud-based deployment with no on-premises components.

correct_answer_text: A capable workforce and culture that is committed to working in accordance with process, following methods, and using tools the organization supplies them.

question: How does the handbook characterize Digital Engineering with respect to engineering disciplines?

options.A: It is confined to the systems engineering discipline and does not extend to other engineering disciplines.
options.B: It is crosscutting and includes all engineering disciplines using well-formed models to execute their processes and communicate a system's design.
options.C: It applies only to software engineering and electrical engineering.
options.D: It is limited to hardware engineering disciplines that produce physical CAD models.

correct_answer_text: It is crosscutting and includes all engineering disciplines using well-formed models to execute their processes and communicate a system's design.

question: According to the handbook, what example illustrates the value of considering Digital Engineering opportunities across the life cycle?

options.A: Discarding earlier life cycle models once production begins so as not to confuse later teams.
options.B: Maintaining separate, non-reusable models for each life cycle stage to ensure independence.
options.C: Models and simulations developed during earlier life cycle stages may be reused for verification and training in later life cycle stages.
options.D: Limiting model use to the concept stage and reverting to document-based methods after design.

correct_answer_text: Models and simulations developed during earlier life cycle stages may be reused for verification and training in later life cycle stages.

question: Digital Engineering is described in the handbook as both a project approach and what else?

options.A: A purely academic research topic with no immediate enterprise relevance.
options.B: A short-term tactical fix limited to individual proposal teams.
options.C: An important enterprise digital transformation initiative whose project and enterprise level concerns should be considered together to achieve maximum benefit.
options.D: A vendor-specific tool deployment that does not require organizational change.

correct_answer_text: An important enterprise digital transformation initiative whose project and enterprise level concerns should be considered together to achieve maximum benefit.

question: Per Section 5.5, which three transformations are identified as important to consider when discussing SE transformation?

options.A: From hardware-only to software-only SE, from waterfall to V-model, and from local to global teams
options.B: From no SE to full use of SE, from traditional to agile methodologies (as appropriate), and from document-based to model-based disciplines
options.C: From paper-based to electronic records, from individual to team-based work, and from manual to automated testing
options.D: From project-level SE to enterprise SE, from MBSE to digital twins, and from on-premise to cloud computing

correct_answer_text: From no SE to full use of SE, from traditional to agile methodologies (as appropriate), and from document-based to model-based disciplines

question: According to Section 5.5, what is required for an organization to begin implementing SE where none previously existed?

options.A: Purchase of MBSE tooling and contracting with an external SE consultancy
options.B: A cultural shift to understand the basics of the system life cycle and implement the life cycle processes in the organization's set of processes
options.C: Hiring only INCOSE-certified ASEP/CSEP practitioners to staff every project
options.D: Adoption of agile methodologies and a digital engineering ecosystem before any other SE activity

correct_answer_text: A cultural shift to understand the basics of the system life cycle and implement the life cycle processes in the organization's set of processes

question: Per Section 5.5, what are the main reasons cited that drive the transformations from no SE to full SE and from traditional to agile SE methodologies?

options.A: Increasing regulatory burden and the rise of cybersecurity threats
options.B: Customer demand for fixed-price contracts and reduced documentation
options.C: The system life cycle is being compressed during concept and development stages and/or the life cycle is being shifted to the left with early concept tasks being anticipated with early involvement of SE
options.D: Growth in offshore engineering teams and the need for 24-hour development cycles

correct_answer_text: The system life cycle is being compressed during concept and development stages and/or the life cycle is being shifted to the left with early concept tasks being anticipated with early involvement of SE

question: Per Section 5.5, the third transformation, SE's transformation to a model-based discipline, supports transdisciplinary digital engineering through which combination of elements?

options.A: Procurement reform, supplier consolidation, and contract restructuring
options.B: Document-based reviews, traditional sign-offs, and printed deliverables
options.C: Model-based activity, advancement, organizational change, and a broad model community engagement
options.D: Cloud migration, blockchain ledgers, and quantum computing

correct_answer_text: Model-based activity, advancement, organizational change, and a broad model community engagement

question: Per Section 5.5, the third transformation to a model-based discipline addresses several areas. Which of the following is one of those areas explicitly listed?

options.A: Outsourcing of SE work to lower-cost regions
options.B: Knowledge representation and immersive technologies
options.C: Elimination of stakeholder engagement activities
options.D: Mandatory use of waterfall methodology for all programs

correct_answer_text: Knowledge representation and immersive technologies

question: Per Section 5.5, beyond agile SE practices and methodologies themselves, what else may an organization need in order to integrate agile SE successfully?

options.A: A change in legal entity status and revised stock ownership
options.B: Mandatory ISO 9001 recertification before any agile work begins
options.C: Tools and capabilities that may also cause the organization to need additional infrastructure and human talent
options.D: Complete elimination of all traditional SE artifacts and reviews

correct_answer_text: Tools and capabilities that may also cause the organization to need additional infrastructure and human talent

question: Per Section 5.5, how does the handbook characterize the choice an organization has in adopting agile SE practices?

options.A: Agile SE must always be adopted as a wholesale change across all processes simultaneously
options.B: Every organization will need to determine the best way to integrate agile SE practices and methodologies, whether as a wholesale change or a gradual change for their SE processes
options.C: Agile SE adoption is mandated by INCOSE and must follow a single prescribed roadmap
options.D: Only large enterprises are permitted to adopt agile SE; small organizations should retain traditional SE

correct_answer_text: Every organization will need to determine the best way to integrate agile SE practices and methodologies, whether as a wholesale change or a gradual change for their SE processes

question: Per Section 5.5, what cultural preparation does the handbook indicate is required for the first transformation (from no SE to full use of SE)?

options.A: Organizations need only purchase SE software tools; cultural change is unnecessary
options.B: Organizations should adopt MBSE first and let culture evolve naturally afterward
options.C: Organizations will need to prepare for the major cultural shift required for the team members to learn, understand, and perform SE tasks
options.D: Organizations should reorganize into functional silos before attempting SE adoption

correct_answer_text: Organizations will need to prepare for the major cultural shift required for the team members to learn, understand, and perform SE tasks

question: What is the PRIMARY focus of the INCOSE SE Handbook, as stated at the beginning of Section 5.6?

options.A: The state-of-the-art research directions in SE
options.B: The state-of-the-good-practice in SE
options.C: Future emerging technologies for SE
options.D: The INCOSE Vision 2035 roadmap

correct_answer_text: The state-of-the-good-practice in SE

question: INCOSE's Future of Systems Engineering (FuSE) is a systems community initiative intended to realize which of the following?

options.A: The ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 standard
options.B: The INCOSE Systems Engineering Competency Framework
options.C: The INCOSE Vision 2035
options.D: The Digital Engineering Strategy

correct_answer_text: The INCOSE Vision 2035

question: According to Section 5.6, FuSE seeks to promote SE as essential for achieving success and delivering value in the engineering of which type of systems?

options.A: Purely mechanical and electrical systems
options.B: Document-based legacy systems
options.C: Socio-cyber-physical systems and SoSs at scale subject to non-deterministic influences and effects
options.D: Single-domain deterministic systems

correct_answer_text: Socio-cyber-physical systems and SoSs at scale subject to non-deterministic influences and effects

question: When the FuSE initiative discusses applying SE "at scale," what does it mean by scale?

options.A: Only the size of the engineering team performing SE
options.B: Only the budget allocated to a project
options.C: The challenges in applying SE from smaller (e.g., miniaturization of electronics) through larger scales (e.g., cloud-based systems with millions of users)
options.D: The number of requirements managed in a project repository

correct_answer_text: The challenges in applying SE from smaller (e.g., miniaturization of electronics) through larger scales (e.g., cloud-based systems with millions of users)

question: At what INCOSE event were the terms "AI4SE" and "SE4AI" first used to describe the dual transformation envisioned for both the SE and AI disciplines?

options.A: The 2018 INCOSE International Symposium
options.B: An early 2019 FuSE workshop hosted by INCOSE
options.C: The 2022 INCOSE Vision 2035 release
options.D: The 2023 INCOSE Code of Ethics workshop

correct_answer_text: An early 2019 FuSE workshop hosted by INCOSE

question: According to Section 5.6, what is the goal of AI4SE?

options.A: Replacing SE practitioners with autonomous AI agents
options.B: Applying AI and ML techniques to improve human-driven SE practices, achieving "augmented intelligence"
options.C: Developing AI systems that require no SE oversight
options.D: Using SE methods to certify AI safety only

correct_answer_text: Applying AI and ML techniques to improve human-driven SE practices, achieving "augmented intelligence"

question: Which of the following is identified in Section 5.6 as a primary focus area of AI4SE?

options.A: Replacing MBSE tools with rule-based expert systems
options.B: Reducing the use of models in SE processes
options.C: Enhancing and assisting SE processes, methods, and tools with tangible impacts on engineered system quality and life cycle cycle time
options.D: Eliminating the need for human practitioners in verification activities

correct_answer_text: Enhancing and assisting SE processes, methods, and tools with tangible impacts on engineered system quality and life cycle cycle time

question: According to the FuSE roadmap in Section 5.6, the evolution of SE will account for human abilities and needs in what way?

options.A: As an external factor managed by HR specialists
options.B: As an integral system element and human interactions with systems and SoSs
options.C: As an optional consideration when budgets permit
options.D: As a separate discipline outside SE scope

correct_answer_text: As an integral system element and human interactions with systems and SoSs

question: Per Section 5.6, the FuSE roadmap drives SE to evolve to be increasingly which of the following?

options.A: Standardized, rigid, and document-centric
options.B: Adaptable, evolvable, and fit for purpose
options.C: Centralized, deterministic, and hierarchical
options.D: Manual, paper-based, and discipline-specific

correct_answer_text: Adaptable, evolvable, and fit for purpose

question: Per Section 5.6, greater understanding of inter-coupled technical, economic, social, and environmental systems will enable SE practitioners to make important contributions to which area?

options.A: Defining national accounting standards
options.B: Replacing traditional project management with SE
options.C: The design of viable systems and transition pathways supporting global sustainability transformation
options.D: Eliminating the need for environmental specialists in SE teams

correct_answer_text: The design of viable systems and transition pathways supporting global sustainability transformation

question: According to Section 5.6, how will the scope of SE widen in the future?

options.A: To recognize and include policy, legal, economic, and environmental specializations
options.B: To focus only on hardware engineering disciplines
options.C: To exclude non-technical stakeholders from SE processes
options.D: To restrict SE to defense and aerospace domains

correct_answer_text: To recognize and include policy, legal, economic, and environmental specializations

question: Section 5.6 lists the FuSE objectives for SE. Which of the following is one of the three explicit goals of the FuSE initiative?

options.A: Replace SE practitioners with autonomous tools by 2035
options.B: Position the discipline to leverage new technologies in collaboration with allied fields
options.C: Eliminate the use of models in favor of document-based practices
options.D: Limit SE to traditional aerospace and defense applications

correct_answer_text: Position the discipline to leverage new technologies in collaboration with allied fields

question: Per Section 5.6, what advances should SE practitioners consider keeping current on when performing SE?

options.A: Only advances in mechanical engineering and materials science
options.B: Advances in computing, communications, software, human systems integration, and algorithms such as augmented intelligence and Machine Learning (ML)
options.C: Only advances in management theory and finance
options.D: Only advances in regulatory standards and compliance frameworks

correct_answer_text: Advances in computing, communications, software, human systems integration, and algorithms such as augmented intelligence and Machine Learning (ML)

question: According to the handbook's Therac-25 case, what was the single most consequential architectural change AECL made when moving from Therac-6/20 to Therac-25 that placed dramatically more responsibility on software for safety?

options.A: Switching the application code from a high-level language to assembly language
options.B: Deciding not to duplicate the independent hardware interlocks and status circuits that prior models had used, relying on software to perform safety checks
options.C: Replacing the electron beam with a higher-energy photon beam under operator control
options.D: Outsourcing the operating system to a third-party real-time vendor

correct_answer_text: Deciding not to duplicate the independent hardware interlocks and status circuits that prior models had used, relying on software to perform safety checks

question: The handbook describes Therac-25 as depending on a 'tight integration of software and hardware,' in contrast to its predecessors. What is the principal Systems Engineering lesson the handbook draws from this tight coupling?

options.A: Tight coupling always improves system performance and should be the default architecture
options.B: Software is inherently safer than hardware, so coupling is acceptable when software is mature
options.C: Tightly coupling software with hardware—and removing independent hardware safety functions—shifts safety responsibility to software and can eliminate redundant protections against single-point failures
options.D: Tight coupling is acceptable provided the software is written in assembly language

correct_answer_text: Tightly coupling software with hardware—and removing independent hardware safety functions—shifts safety responsibility to software and can eliminate redundant protections against single-point failures

question: The handbook notes that the Therac-25 software was evolved by one programmer from Therac-20 software, which itself had been derived from Therac-6. What SE concept does the handbook explicitly use to characterize this approach?

options.A: Greenfield development
options.B: Model-based systems engineering
options.C: Brownfield, or legacy, development (see Section 4.3.2)
options.D: Agile spiral development

correct_answer_text: Brownfield, or legacy, development (see Section 4.3.2)

question: Per the Therac-25 case, when did the handbook conclude the errors leading to the overdose accidents were actually introduced?

options.A: During verification and validation of the final delivered system
options.B: During production manufacturing of the LINAC units
options.C: During operations, owing to operator interface misuse in the field
options.D: During the concept and early development stages, when the decision was made to reuse and modify the prior software

correct_answer_text: During the concept and early development stages, when the decision was made to reuse and modify the prior software

question: The handbook states that the starting point for the Therac-25 software (Therac-6) was 'a poorly documented product and no one except the original software developer could follow the logic.' Which SE principle is most directly illustrated by this observation?

options.A: Reuse always reduces risk because the inherited software was previously fielded
options.B: Inadequate documentation and reliance on tacit knowledge of legacy software make safety consequences of reuse difficult to assess
options.C: Premarket equivalency approval is sufficient evidence of safety for reused software
options.D: Assembly language coding inherently prevents safe analysis of legacy logic

correct_answer_text: Inadequate documentation and reliance on tacit knowledge of legacy software make safety consequences of reuse difficult to assess

question: According to the handbook, how was Therac-25 approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in 1982?

options.A: Through a full premarket approval (PMA) clinical trial program
options.B: Through an emergency-use authorization for oncology devices
options.C: Under Premarket Equivalency, on the basis that its software was based on the earlier Therac-20 and Therac-6 models
options.D: Through self-certification by the manufacturer without FDA involvement

correct_answer_text: Under Premarket Equivalency, on the basis that its software was based on the earlier Therac-20 and Therac-6 models

question: The handbook reports that the Therac-25 operator interface had been adjusted to 'simplify the operator interface and minimize data entry,' because initial operators complained entries took too long. What SE risk does this change most directly exemplify for a software-intensive safety-critical system?

options.A: Human-factors changes made to improve productivity can introduce new safety hazards if not analyzed against the safety architecture
options.B: Reducing data entry always improves safety by eliminating operator error
options.C: Operator complaints are not valid system requirements and should be ignored
options.D: Simplified interfaces are mandated by Class II medical device classification

correct_answer_text: Human-factors changes made to improve productivity can introduce new safety hazards if not analyzed against the safety architecture

question: In the Therac-25 design, what was the operator's required action after the software detected a 'minor malfunction' during treatment?

options.A: Power-cycle the LINAC and run a full self-test before any further use
options.B: Press a single 'proceed' key to restart the procedure without re-entering treatment parameters
options.C: Notify the manufacturer before any further treatment could be conducted
options.D: Manually engage a hardware interlock to verify beam status

correct_answer_text: Press a single 'proceed' key to restart the procedure without re-entering treatment parameters

question: Which statement best summarizes the SE lesson the Therac-25 case offers about software-controlled hazards in software-intensive systems (per Section 4.3.4 context)?

options.A: Software-controlled hazards are bounded by the assembler used to write the code
options.B: Software can be designated the sole safety control for a hazard provided the operator interface is simple
options.C: When software alone is responsible for controlling a hazard, the safety analysis, documentation, and independent verification of that software must be commensurate with the hazard's severity
options.D: Software-controlled hazards do not require system-level safety analysis because they are confined to code

correct_answer_text: When software alone is responsible for controlling a hazard, the safety analysis, documentation, and independent verification of that software must be commensurate with the hazard's severity

question: The handbook concludes its Therac-25 case by noting that 'the issues from the Therac case are, unfortunately, still relevant, as evidenced by similar deaths for similar reasons in 2007.' What is the principal SE takeaway from this concluding observation?

options.A: Radiation therapy is inherently unsafe and should be discontinued
options.B: Once a safety lesson has been published, the industry reliably internalizes it
options.C: The recurrence shows that SE practitioners must continue to apply system safety engineering rigor to software-intensive safety-critical systems and not assume earlier lessons have been absorbed
options.D: Modern LINACs no longer rely on software for safety control

correct_answer_text: The recurrence shows that SE practitioners must continue to apply system safety engineering rigor to software-intensive safety-critical systems and not assume earlier lessons have been absorbed

question: How does the handbook contrast Therac-6 and Therac-20 with Therac-25 with respect to computer dependence?

options.A: All three machines were fully computerized and indistinguishable in architecture
options.B: Therac-6 and Therac-20 were stand-alone machines that could be operated without a computer, while Therac-25 depended on a tight integration of software and hardware
options.C: Therac-25 was the only one without a computer; the earlier models were fully digital
options.D: Therac-6 and Therac-20 used networked computers; Therac-25 used a stand-alone computer

correct_answer_text: Therac-6 and Therac-20 were stand-alone machines that could be operated without a computer, while Therac-25 depended on a tight integration of software and hardware

question: From an SE system-safety perspective (handbook Section 3.1.11 tie-in), the Therac-25 case most strongly demonstrates the danger of:

options.A: Excessive redundancy in safety functions causing operator confusion
options.B: Removing independent safety mechanisms (hardware interlocks) and creating single points of failure in software that controls a hazard
options.C: Designing for low production cost via fewer mechanical parts
options.D: Approving devices through equivalence rather than novel design

correct_answer_text: Removing independent safety mechanisms (hardware interlocks) and creating single points of failure in software that controls a hazard

question: Why does the handbook present Therac-25 as a Systems Engineering case study rather than purely a software-engineering case?

options.A: Because the failures arose only in mechanical subsystems independent of software
options.B: Because system-level decisions in the concept and early development stages—architecture choices, removal of independent interlocks, reuse of legacy software, operator-interface simplification, and safety responsibility allocation—drove the accidents
options.C: Because the case is exclusively about manufacturing tolerances of the LINAC
options.D: Because the case focuses on procurement and contract management of medical equipment

correct_answer_text: Because system-level decisions in the concept and early development stages—architecture choices, removal of independent interlocks, reuse of legacy software, operator-interface simplification, and safety responsibility allocation—drove the accidents

question: According to the handbook's Conclusions for the Øresund Bridge case, what single SE practice is identified as the clearest contributor to the project's success?

options.A: Aggressive schedule compression during detailed design
options.B: A solid concept stage that resisted the customer-driven temptation to jump prematurely into development
options.C: Use of advanced model-based systems engineering tools
options.D: Outsourcing risk management to an independent third party

correct_answer_text: A solid concept stage that resisted the customer-driven temptation to jump prematurely into development

question: How did the Øresund owners address concerns of environmental stakeholder groups during the concept stage?

options.A: They commissioned a post-construction environmental impact study
options.B: They invited the head of a key environmental group to be part of the board of directors
options.C: They delegated environmental requirements to the contractor's quality team
options.D: They limited environmental considerations to dredging operations only

correct_answer_text: They invited the head of a key environmental group to be part of the board of directors

question: The handbook attributes the absence of disputes and significant claims against the Øresund owners at project close to which factor?

options.A: Strict contractual penalties for late delivery
options.B: The spirit of partnership
options.C: A fixed-price firm contract that prevented changes
options.D: Government arbitration of all design disagreements

correct_answer_text: The spirit of partnership

question: Which interoperability challenge did the Øresund railway link specifically introduce that required developing a system to switch on the fly?

options.A: Different track gauges between Denmark and Sweden
options.B: Different signal-control software versions
options.C: Different railway power supplies between the two countries
options.D: Different freight-loading platforms at each terminal

correct_answer_text: Different railway power supplies between the two countries

question: Danish trains operate right-handed (like roadways) while Swedish trains pass on the left. The Øresund connection therefore had to provide what?

options.A: A separate parallel track dedicated to each country's traffic
options.B: A logical transition between the two systems, including safety aspects
options.C: A single-direction shuttle service to avoid handedness conflicts
options.D: A purely freight rail link with no passenger crossings

correct_answer_text: A logical transition between the two systems, including safety aspects

question: Which of the following best illustrates risk-informed design decisions that emerged from the Øresund risk analyses?

options.A: Reducing the navigation span and removing protection islands to save cost
options.B: Increasing the navigation span from 330 m to 490 m, realigning and deepening the channel, and adding pier protection islands
options.C: Adopting a single hydrographical model to streamline analysis
options.D: Replacing the railway deck with a second motorway deck to reduce hazards

correct_answer_text: Increasing the navigation span from 330 m to 490 m, realigning and deepening the channel, and adding pier protection islands

question: In the Øresund project, the owners chose to define comprehensive requirements and definition drawings as part of the contract documents. What was the contractor responsible for?

options.A: Re-baselining the owners' requirements after award
options.B: Setting national environmental policy for both countries
options.C: The detailed design and delivering a quality-assured product in accordance with the owners' requirements
options.D: Funding the artificial island construction independently

correct_answer_text: The detailed design and delivering a quality-assured product in accordance with the owners' requirements

question: Because the Øresund project crossed national boundaries, technical requirements were primarily based on which framework, with project-specific amendments?

options.A: ISO 9001 quality standards alone
options.B: The Eurocodes, with amendments to suit the national standards of both countries
options.C: American Association of State Highway and Transportation Officials (AASHTO) codes
options.D: Solely the Danish national standards, applied uniformly to both sides

correct_answer_text: The Eurocodes, with amendments to suit the national standards of both countries

question: Which of the following is the SE lesson the handbook draws from how environmental concerns were handled on the Øresund Bridge?

options.A: Environmental considerations should be deferred until verification activities at handover
options.B: Environmental objectives are best handled separately from system requirements
options.C: Environmental constraints belong in the system's requirements and should be treated as system requirements from the concept stage onward
options.D: Environmental monitoring is unnecessary once a zero solution is defined

correct_answer_text: Environmental constraints belong in the system's requirements and should be treated as system requirements from the concept stage onward

question: The Danish and Swedish authorities required that the bridge be designed so the flow of water, salt, and oxygen into the Baltic was not affected. What was this requirement designated?

options.A: The minimum-impact baseline
options.B: The zero solution
options.C: The Baltic conservation criterion
options.D: The Eurocode environmental allowance

correct_answer_text: The zero solution

question: To verify the environmental design met the zero solution, what approach was used?

options.A: Modeling with two different and independent hydrographical models
options.B: A single high-fidelity model maintained by the contractor
options.C: Post-construction water quality sampling only
options.D: Reliance on Eurocode default assumptions

correct_answer_text: Modeling with two different and independent hydrographical models

question: An SE practitioner studying the Øresund case is asked why the concept stage is so heavily emphasized. Which statement best captures the handbook's reasoning?

options.A: Concept-stage work shaped the project with well-defined time, budget, and quality constraints before transition to the development stage
options.B: Concept-stage work primarily set the aesthetic style of the bridge
options.C: Concept-stage work was a formality used to obtain political approval
options.D: Concept-stage work was repeated in the development stage, so its effect on success was minor

correct_answer_text: Concept-stage work shaped the project with well-defined time, budget, and quality constraints before transition to the development stage

question: The Øresund design called out wind speed (10 min), wave height, ice thickness, and temperature ranges in its initial requirements. What does this exemplify as an SE practice?

options.A: Premature design optimization at the concept stage
options.B: Quantitative specification of ambient environment as part of the system's comprehensive requirements at the start of the project
options.C: Substitution of operational requirements with environmental constraints
options.D: A contractor-driven addition unrelated to owner requirements

correct_answer_text: Quantitative specification of ambient environment as part of the system's comprehensive requirements at the start of the project

question: The handbook lists many disciplines involved in the Øresund cable-stayed bridge design—geotechnical engineering, aerodynamics, wind tunnel tests, ship impact analysis, earthquake analysis, fatigue analysis, traffic forecast, environmental studies, and more. What SE lesson does this breadth illustrate?

options.A: Single-discipline expertise is sufficient for major infrastructure systems
options.B: Multidisciplinary integration is fundamental to SE for complex infrastructure systems
options.C: Discipline integration can be deferred until the operations stage
options.D: Discipline analyses should be performed sequentially with no SE coordination

correct_answer_text: Multidisciplinary integration is fundamental to SE for complex infrastructure systems

question: Which of the following best describes the SE interface-management lesson the Øresund case illustrates?

options.A: Interfaces between sub-suppliers can be resolved entirely during integration testing
options.B: Cross-border infrastructure interoperability—different traffic-handedness and different power supplies—must be explicitly designed and managed as system interfaces
options.C: Interface management is primarily a procurement activity rather than an SE activity
options.D: Interface definition is only required for software-intensive systems

correct_answer_text: Cross-border infrastructure interoperability—different traffic-handedness and different power supplies—must be explicitly designed and managed as system interfaces

question: A key stakeholder-management lesson from the Øresund case for an SE practitioner is:

options.A: Environmental stakeholders should be engaged only after the design is frozen, to avoid scope changes
options.B: Environmental and other key stakeholders should be engaged early—even given governance roles—so their concerns shape the approach during the concept stage
options.C: Only contractually obligated stakeholders need to be considered
options.D: Stakeholder engagement is a public-relations function, not an SE function

correct_answer_text: Environmental and other key stakeholders should be engaged early—even given governance roles—so their concerns shape the approach during the concept stage

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: According to the Stuxnet case study, what is the principal SE lesson that the handbook draws from the attack?

options.A: Antivirus software should always be installed on industrial control systems
options.B: SE practitioners must understand threats, expect attacks to occur, and proactively protect their systems through robust, dynamic, lifecycle system security
options.C: Nuclear facilities should never use programmable logic controllers
options.D: Cybersecurity is solely an information technology concern and lies outside the SE scope

correct_answer_text: SE practitioners must understand threats, expect attacks to occur, and proactively protect their systems through robust, dynamic, lifecycle system security

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: The handbook explicitly cross-references which SE process for the discipline that must be fully engaged to deliver robust and dynamic system security as illustrated by Stuxnet?

options.A: Section 3.1.12 System Security Engineering
options.B: Section 4.3.5 Cyber-Physical Systems only
options.C: Section 6.1 Therac-25 case study
options.D: Section 2.3 Standards and references

correct_answer_text: Section 3.1.12 System Security Engineering

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: How does the handbook characterize the cascade halls at the Natanz fuel enrichment plant?

options.A: Purely mechanical facilities with no digital control
options.B: Cloud-connected SCADA networks managed remotely
options.C: Cyber-physical systems comprising an industrial control system (ICS) of PLCs, computers, an isolated internal network, and thousands of centrifuges
options.D: Stand-alone IT data centers without any physical actuators

correct_answer_text: Cyber-physical systems comprising an industrial control system (ICS) of PLCs, computers, an isolated internal network, and thousands of centrifuges

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: What is the SE lesson the handbook draws from the fact that Natanz's internal network was air-gapped yet was still compromised?

options.A: Air gaps provide absolute protection and should be the only defense considered
options.B: Air gaps eliminate insider threats by design
options.C: Air gaps reduce risk but do not eliminate it; threats to the system exist both inside and outside the system boundary
options.D: Air gaps are unnecessary if antivirus is current

correct_answer_text: Air gaps reduce risk but do not eliminate it; threats to the system exist both inside and outside the system boundary

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: How does the handbook define a zero-day attack in the Stuxnet case?

options.A: An attack that is detected and patched on the same day it is launched
options.B: An attack executed on day zero of system deployment by the development team
options.C: An attack that exploits a previously unknown vulnerability in a computer application that developers have had no time to address and patch
options.D: An attack confined to legacy systems older than ten years

correct_answer_text: An attack that exploits a previously unknown vulnerability in a computer application that developers have had no time to address and patch

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: Even with an air gap, the handbook lists several remaining attack vectors against Natanz. Which set is consistent with the case?

options.A: Wireless intrusion, satellite uplink, and remote desktop only
options.B: Malicious insider collusion, non-malicious insertion of memory devices from outside, visiting service technicians, and supply chain intervention
options.C: Radio-frequency interference and electromagnetic pulse attacks only
options.D: Phishing emails sent to centrifuge controllers

correct_answer_text: Malicious insider collusion, non-malicious insertion of memory devices from outside, visiting service technicians, and supply chain intervention

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: Which of the following is one of the four explicit lessons the handbook enumerates for SE practitioners from the Stuxnet case?

options.A: Standardize on a single PLC vendor for all critical assets
options.B: Knowing what to do (intelligence)—adversaries will attempt to gain intelligence on the system; SE must consider methods to prevent this
options.C: Outsource all cybersecurity to certified third parties
options.D: Disable all USB ports in every operational environment

correct_answer_text: Knowing what to do (intelligence)—adversaries will attempt to gain intelligence on the system; SE must consider methods to prevent this

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: What SE implication does the handbook draw from the observation that Stuxnet's attack code could be updated and changed over time after insertion?

options.A: Once a system is fielded, no further security activity is required
options.B: Dynamic updating shows that SE practitioners need to prepare for situations after a successful attack has occurred
options.C: Software patching should be disabled to prevent re-infection
options.D: Attack code never changes once inserted, so static defenses suffice

correct_answer_text: Dynamic updating shows that SE practitioners need to prepare for situations after a successful attack has occurred

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: The handbook notes that Stuxnet infected many sites other than Natanz but would only activate under what condition?

options.A: When the host computer had internet connectivity
options.B: When the site was configured to certain specific system specifications matching the intended target
options.C: When triggered manually by a remote operator
options.D: When the malware detected antivirus software present

correct_answer_text: When the site was configured to certain specific system specifications matching the intended target

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: What does the Stuxnet case study demonstrate about the relationship between cyber attacks and physical systems?

options.A: Cyber attacks can only corrupt data and cannot produce physical effects
options.B: Cyber-physical system attacks can cause real physical damage, ranging from disruption of the production process up to potential permanent damage to the affected centrifuges
options.C: Physical systems are immune to malware once installed
options.D: Cyber attacks always require physical access to cause physical effects

correct_answer_text: Cyber-physical system attacks can cause real physical damage, ranging from disruption of the production process up to potential permanent damage to the affected centrifuges

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: What was the primary mechanism by which Stuxnet was believed to have crossed Natanz's air gap, according to the handbook?

options.A: A satellite-borne wireless transmitter
options.B: A USB removable media device originally infected on a computer outside the FEP and carried inside, with PLC supply chain and maintenance personnel as additional suggested vectors
options.C: A direct internet connection to the cascade hall network
options.D: An undetected radio backdoor in the PLCs

correct_answer_text: A USB removable media device originally infected on a computer outside the FEP and carried inside, with PLC supply chain and maintenance personnel as additional suggested vectors

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: Why does the handbook argue that the economic threat from Stuxnet-like attacks is growing, even though Stuxnet itself was expensive to create?

options.A: Defensive technologies have become more expensive to deploy
options.B: Although the creation and deployment of Stuxnet were expensive, its strategy, tactical methods, and code mechanisms became openly available for others to reuse and build upon at much less expense
options.C: Cyber insurance premiums have decreased the cost of attacks
options.D: Industrial control systems are no longer in widespread use

correct_answer_text: Although the creation and deployment of Stuxnet were expensive, its strategy, tactical methods, and code mechanisms became openly available for others to reuse and build upon at much less expense

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: Which database does the handbook explicitly tell SE practitioners they should be aware of in connection with the cybersecurity lessons of the Stuxnet case?

options.A: A vulnerability database maintained by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
options.B: The International Atomic Energy Agency centrifuge registry
options.C: The INCOSE risk register database
options.D: The ISO/IEC 15288 process catalog

correct_answer_text: A vulnerability database maintained by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: According to the Stuxnet 'crafting the code' lesson, what should SE practitioners be prepared for during system design?

options.A: Only external threats from the public internet
options.B: Only insider threats with privileged access
options.C: Many different attack vectors, including internal threats
options.D: Only threats that exploit publicly known vulnerabilities

correct_answer_text: Many different attack vectors, including internal threats

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: How did Stuxnet achieve proper insertion into Windows operating systems outside the Natanz FEP?

options.A: By guessing administrator passwords through brute force
options.B: By using a variety of zero-day exploits and stolen certificates, then initiating a multistage propagation mechanism beginning with infected USB removable media
options.C: By exploiting a known and unpatched legacy vulnerability disclosed years earlier
options.D: By tricking users into installing a counterfeit antivirus product

correct_answer_text: By using a variety of zero-day exploits and stolen certificates, then initiating a multistage propagation mechanism beginning with infected USB removable media

section_title: Cybersecurity Considerations in Systems Engineering—the Stuxnet Attack
question: What broader change in the SE risk landscape does the handbook conclude the Stuxnet case represents?

options.A: A return to purely analog control systems in critical infrastructure
options.B: The end of cyber-physical system targeting as a viable threat
options.C: An inflection point that ushered in a new era of system attack methodology and cyber-physical system targeting, requiring SE perspective to adjust accordingly
options.D: Confirmation that traditional safety analysis alone is sufficient for digital systems

correct_answer_text: An inflection point that ushered in a new era of system attack methodology and cyber-physical system targeting, requiring SE perspective to adjust accordingly

question: According to the NeoNurture case study, what is the primary Systems Engineering lesson that the handbook draws from the failure of donated modern incubators in developing-world hospitals?

options.A: Medical devices should be standardized internationally to reduce part proliferation
options.B: Sometimes the highest technology solution is not the best, and maintainability and logistics must be considered at the project outset
options.C: Donations of medical equipment should be coordinated through a single international body
options.D: Software-controlled medical devices are inherently less reliable than mechanical devices
