correct_answer_text: Sometimes the highest technology solution is not the best, and maintainability and logistics must be considered at the project outset

question: The NeoNurture case study concludes that maintainability and logistics must be addressed during which life cycle stage(s)?

options.A: Production and utilization stages, when operational data first becomes available
options.B: Concept and development stages, because it is too late to address these in later stages
options.C: Retirement stage, when disposal and reuse decisions are made
options.D: Support stage only, since that is when maintenance actually occurs

correct_answer_text: Concept and development stages, because it is too late to address these in later stages

question: Studies cited in the NeoNurture case suggest what fraction of medical technology donated to developing countries breaks within the first five years of use?

options.A: As much as 25%
options.B: As much as 50%
options.C: As much as 75%
options.D: As much as 95%

correct_answer_text: As much as 95%

question: Why did Design that Matters choose automotive parts (headlights, dashboard fans, door chimes, motorcycle batteries) as the basis for the NeoNurture incubator?

options.A: Automotive parts are cheaper than medical-grade components in every market
options.B: Automotive parts have higher inherent reliability than purpose-built medical components
options.C: Local supply of automotive parts and local knowledge of automobile repair were already abundant in the developing-world context
options.D: International donors required the use of recycled materials in donated medical equipment

correct_answer_text: Local supply of automotive parts and local knowledge of automobile repair were already abundant in the developing-world context

question: Which pair of definitions best distinguishes reliability from maintainability as taught by the NeoNurture case?

options.A: Reliability is how easily a system is repaired; maintainability is how often it fails
options.B: Reliability is how often a system fails; maintainability is how easily it is repaired
options.C: Reliability is a design attribute; maintainability is an operational attribute unrelated to design
options.D: Reliability and maintainability are synonyms, both measuring time-to-failure

correct_answer_text: Reliability is how often a system fails; maintainability is how easily it is repaired

question: Designing the NeoNurture incubator for the developing-world context was described in the case as not just creating something that worked but also designing something that would break in what manner?

options.A: Break in a way that triggers automatic shutdown of all hospital systems
options.B: Break in a non-catastrophic way, because spare parts and trained repair technicians could not be guaranteed
options.C: Break in a way that requires factory return for repair to ensure quality control
options.D: Break only after a guaranteed minimum operating life of ten years

correct_answer_text: Break in a non-catastrophic way, because spare parts and trained repair technicians could not be guaranteed

question: The Meulaboh hospital received eight donated incubators after the 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami. By 2008, all eight were out of order. The case identifies which combination of root causes for these failures?

options.A: Counterfeit components, software defects, and lack of internet access
options.B: Power surges, tropical humidity, and the hospital staff's inability to read the English repair manual
options.C: Patient misuse, regulatory non-compliance, and substandard sterilization
options.D: Cybersecurity attacks, fuel shortages, and seismic damage

correct_answer_text: Power surges, tropical humidity, and the hospital staff's inability to read the English repair manual

question: Which Systems Engineering concept is most directly illustrated by the NeoNurture design choice to use parts already abundant in the operational environment?

options.A: Functional decomposition, by mapping functions to off-the-shelf hardware
options.B: Supportability, by ensuring the people, parts, tools, training, and logistics needed to sustain the system already exist in the operational environment
options.C: Verification and validation, by reusing previously qualified parts
options.D: Configuration management, by reducing the number of unique part numbers

correct_answer_text: Supportability, by ensuring the people, parts, tools, training, and logistics needed to sustain the system already exist in the operational environment

question: A $40,000 modern hospital incubator and a NeoNurture incubator are being compared as candidate solutions for a rural clinic in a low-resource setting. Which SE consideration best explains why NeoNurture may be the superior choice despite being less technologically advanced?

options.A: It has lower acquisition cost only; lifecycle cost is identical
options.B: It eliminates the need for any electrical power source
options.C: Its design accounts for the operational environment, supportability, and logistics constraints, so total lifecycle effectiveness is higher
options.D: It is certified under more stringent regulatory standards than the modern incubator

correct_answer_text: Its design accounts for the operational environment, supportability, and logistics constraints, so total lifecycle effectiveness is higher

question: Which of the following best characterizes the appropriate-technology principle that the NeoNurture case is intended to teach SE practitioners?

options.A: Always select the most advanced available technology to maximize performance margin
options.B: The best solution is the one whose technology level, supportability, and logistics fit the operational context, which is not always the most advanced
options.C: Reuse legacy designs unchanged to minimize development cost
options.D: Standardize on a single global technology baseline regardless of operating environment

correct_answer_text: The best solution is the one whose technology level, supportability, and logistics fit the operational context, which is not always the most advanced

question: The NeoNurture case is the handbook's flagship example for which set of cross-cutting SE topics (linked to RAM, logistics, and maintenance process discussions elsewhere in the handbook)?

options.A: Cybersecurity, zero-day exploits, and air-gap design
options.B: Human-machine teaming, explainability, and shared context
options.C: Maintainability, supportability, logistics, and lifecycle thinking
options.D: Stakeholder negotiation, interface management, and environmental requirements

correct_answer_text: Maintainability, supportability, logistics, and lifecycle thinking

question: Which statement most accurately reflects the relationship the NeoNurture case implies between concept-stage decisions and downstream sustainment outcomes?

options.A: Sustainment outcomes are determined almost entirely during the production stage, regardless of concept choices
options.B: Maintainability and logistics decisions made (or omitted) in the concept stage largely determine whether a deployed system can be sustained in its actual operational environment
options.C: Concept-stage decisions affect cost but have little effect on field maintainability
options.D: Sustainment is the responsibility of the operator and is decoupled from SE design decisions

correct_answer_text: Maintainability and logistics decisions made (or omitted) in the concept stage largely determine whether a deployed system can be sustained in its actual operational environment

question: An SE practitioner is designing a field-deployable diagnostic device for rural clinics. Drawing on the NeoNurture lesson, which design decision most strongly supports maintainability in the target environment?

options.A: Use proprietary, sealed-unit modules so that only certified vendor technicians can perform repairs
options.B: Use components and skills that are already abundant in the operational environment so local personnel can perform repairs without specialized training or imported parts
options.C: Maximize use of cutting-edge sensors to extend useful life before the first failure
options.D: Centralize all repairs at a single national facility to ensure quality control

correct_answer_text: Use components and skills that are already abundant in the operational environment so local personnel can perform repairs without specialized training or imported parts

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: Uber disabled the Volvo XC90's factory advanced driver-assistance system before the March 2018 fatal accident. From a Systems Engineering safety perspective, what is the primary lesson this decision illustrates?

options.A: Aftermarket sensors should always replace OEM equipment to avoid integration cost
options.B: Disabling layered/redundant safety systems eliminates defense-in-depth and increases overall risk
options.C: Radar frequency conflicts are unavoidable in modern vehicles
options.D: Software-only safety monitors are inherently superior to hardware interlocks

correct_answer_text: Disabling layered/redundant safety systems eliminates defense-in-depth and increases overall risk

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: The handbook states that designing a human and a machine that act autonomously together requires shifting the unit of analysis from individuals to what?

options.A: Software modules
options.B: Subsystems
options.C: Teams
options.D: Use cases

correct_answer_text: Teams

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: According to the Case 5 conclusions citing Cooke (2020), where does a team's intelligence reside?

options.A: In the most capable individual teammate
options.B: In the interdependent interactions among teammates
options.C: In the central decision-making algorithm
options.D: In documented procedures and checklists

correct_answer_text: In the interdependent interactions among teammates

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: When the Uber ADS detected an emergency, it suppressed any vehicle action for one second to avoid false alarms. What does this design choice illustrate as a Systems Engineering lesson?

options.A: False-alarm mitigation always outweighs latency in safety-critical control
options.B: Design choices intended to reduce nuisance behavior can introduce unacceptable safety risk and must be traded against hazard severity
options.C: Action suppression is required by NHTSA automated vehicle policy
options.D: One-second delays are within human reaction tolerance and therefore safe

correct_answer_text: Design choices intended to reduce nuisance behavior can introduce unacceptable safety risk and must be traded against hazard severity

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: Figure 6.1 (Timeline of vehicle impact) shows that each time the ADS reclassified the detected object, the system discarded the prior tracking history for that object. Why is this a critical SE flaw for an AI-based perception system?

options.A: It violates GPS pre-mapped route constraints
options.B: It breaks the detection-classification-prediction-response chain because path prediction cannot accumulate evidence across classifications
options.C: It increases storage requirements for the data recorder
options.D: It causes lidar and radar to operate on incompatible frequencies

correct_answer_text: It breaks the detection-classification-prediction-response chain because path prediction cannot accumulate evidence across classifications

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: For nearly 20 minutes before impact, the human-machine interface presented no requests for the operator's input. What human-factors principle from Human Systems Integration (Section 3.1.4) does this most directly violate?

options.A: Humans excel as passive monitors of highly reliable automation
options.B: Humans are poor passive monitors and complacency increases when the HMI does not engage them
options.C: Operators should never be required to intervene in automated driving
options.D: Audible alerts alone are sufficient to maintain operator vigilance

correct_answer_text: Humans are poor passive monitors and complacency increases when the HMI does not engage them

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: The Uber ADS did not assign a predicted track to pedestrians located outside of a crosswalk. From a requirements engineering perspective, this is best characterized as which type of failure?

options.A: An operational test failure detected during V&V
options.B: A requirements completeness failure—the operational design domain omitted a foreseeable real-world condition
options.C: A coding defect in the lidar driver
options.D: An environmental qualification failure

correct_answer_text: A requirements completeness failure—the operational design domain omitted a foreseeable real-world condition

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: NTSB cited Uber for inadequate safety risk assessments, ineffective real-time oversight of vehicle operators, and an inadequate safety culture overall. Which SE concept does this finding most strongly reinforce?

options.A: Technical failures alone determine accident outcomes
options.B: Organizational and cultural factors are major contributors to system accidents and must be addressed by SE
options.C: Regulatory submissions can substitute for internal safety processes
options.D: Operator training is sufficient to overcome AI limitations

correct_answer_text: Organizational and cultural factors are major contributors to system accidents and must be addressed by SE

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: The Case 5 conclusions argue that resolving uncertainty in an autonomous human-machine system requires, at a minimum, what state between the human and machine teammates?

options.A: Strict hierarchical command authority of the human over the machine
options.B: Shared interdependence to build context and adapt to rapid changes
options.C: Functional independence so failures cannot propagate
options.D: Identical sensor suites and decision logic

correct_answer_text: Shared interdependence to build context and adapt to rapid changes

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: The handbook concludes that AI machines acting as team members must be able to do what so the team can operate safely under uncertainty?

options.A: Operate fully independently of human input
options.B: Tell their human partners when they perceive a context change, and understand humans conversing with them (explainability)
options.C: Override human commands when sensors disagree
options.D: Suppress all action until certainty thresholds are met

correct_answer_text: Tell their human partners when they perceive a context change, and understand humans conversing with them (explainability)

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: Per the NTSB report cited in the case, the Uber ADS was functionally limited and unable to do which of the following until almost the moment of impact?

options.A: Maintain the vehicle within its lane
options.B: Correctly classify the object as a pedestrian, predict its path, or adequately assess its risk
options.C: Communicate with GPS satellites
options.D: Engage the operator-facing HMI tablet

correct_answer_text: Correctly classify the object as a pedestrian, predict its path, or adequately assess its risk

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: From a Human-Machine Teaming perspective, why did the handbook characterize the Uber vehicle as 'a poor team player'?

options.A: It had fewer sensors than competing autonomous vehicles
options.B: The operator and vehicle operated independently of each other rather than as interdependent teammates
options.C: It exceeded its 45 mph speed limit
options.D: It used both lidar and radar simultaneously

correct_answer_text: The operator and vehicle operated independently of each other rather than as interdependent teammates

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: The Case 5 conclusions argue that to reduce uncertainty in an autonomous system, human and machine teammates must be able to do what for each other?

options.A: Replace each other's role instantaneously
options.B: Explain their view of reality in causal terms (however imperfectly)
options.C: Share encryption keys for sensor data
options.D: Defer entirely to the machine's higher-rate processing

correct_answer_text: Explain their view of reality in causal terms (however imperfectly)

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: Both the operator (distracted by a personal cell phone) and the pedestrian (impaired and jaywalking) contributed to the accident. What broader Systems Engineering takeaway does the case make from these human factors?

options.A: Human factors are outside the SE system boundary and should be excluded from analysis
options.B: Human behavior is part of the sociotechnical system and the ADS must be designed for foreseeable human errors of both operators and other road users
options.C: Legal violations by third parties absolve the developer of design responsibility
options.D: Automation removes the need to engineer for human error

correct_answer_text: Human behavior is part of the sociotechnical system and the ADS must be designed for foreseeable human errors of both operators and other road users

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: The handbook's final SE message in Case 5 is that interdependence among parts of a human-machine team produces what additional benefit beyond simple reactivity?

options.A: It reduces the number of sensors required
options.B: It allows the team to emerge as a single unit, increasing the team's performance
options.C: It removes the need for explainability
options.D: It eliminates the need for safety culture

correct_answer_text: It allows the team to emerge as a single unit, increasing the team's performance

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: NTSB recommended that safety assessment reports submitted to NHTSA, which were voluntary at the time, be made what?

options.A: Optional but standardized
options.B: Mandatory and uniform across all states
options.C: Proprietary to each manufacturer
options.D: Replaced with self-certification

correct_answer_text: Mandatory and uniform across all states

section_title: Artificial Intelligence in Systems Engineering—Autonomous Vehicles
question: Which sequence best captures the AI perception pipeline that failed in this case, as described by Case 5?

options.A: Sense, store, transmit, log
options.B: Detection, classification, prediction, response
options.C: Plan, execute, verify, certify
options.D: Acquire, calibrate, fuse, ignore

correct_answer_text: Detection, classification, prediction, response

question: Per Section 6.6 of the INCOSE SE Handbook, where can SE practitioners find additional case studies beyond those presented in Part VI?

options.A: Only in proprietary INCOSE member-restricted archives not accessible to the public
options.B: In other sources, including the SEBoK, which maintains a set of case studies identified as 'implementation examples'
options.C: Exclusively in the IEEE Standards repository under ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288
options.D: Only in the NASA Systems Engineering Handbook NASA/SP-2016-6105

correct_answer_text: In other sources, including the SEBoK, which maintains a set of case studies identified as 'implementation examples'

question: According to Section 6.6, the SEBoK case studies referenced by the handbook are identified by what specific name?

options.A: Best practice exemplars
options.B: Reference applications
options.C: Implementation examples
options.D: Lessons-learned vignettes

correct_answer_text: Implementation examples

question: Per Section 6.6, which of the following domains is explicitly listed as being spanned by the SEBoK case studies?

options.A: Agricultural systems
options.B: Entertainment systems
options.C: Financial systems
options.D: Transportation systems

correct_answer_text: Transportation systems

question: Which of the following sets of domains is explicitly enumerated in Section 6.6 as being spanned by the SEBoK 'implementation examples' case studies?

options.A: Defense, information, management, medical, space, transportation, and utility systems
options.B: Aerospace, automotive, biomedical, chemical, and civil engineering systems
options.C: Manufacturing, retail, telecommunications, and consumer electronics systems
options.D: Agricultural, environmental, recreational, and educational systems

correct_answer_text: Defense, information, management, medical, space, transportation, and utility systems

question: What is the primary purpose of Section 6.6 'Other Case Studies' within Part VI of the INCOSE SE Handbook?

options.A: To present detailed narratives of three additional failed system case studies
options.B: To direct readers to additional case studies available in other sources beyond the five presented in Part VI
options.C: To provide a quantitative comparison of the five Part VI case studies
options.D: To define the criteria by which case studies were selected for inclusion in the handbook

correct_answer_text: To direct readers to additional case studies available in other sources beyond the five presented in Part VI

question: According to Section 6.6, which of the following domains is NOT included in the explicit list of domains spanned by the SEBoK case studies?

options.A: Medical systems
options.B: Space systems
options.C: Utility systems
options.D: Agricultural systems

correct_answer_text: Agricultural systems

question: Per Section 6.6, the additional case studies pointed to by the handbook are maintained by which organization/source?

options.A: The Systems Engineering Body of Knowledge (SEBoK)
options.B: The U.S. Government Accountability Office (GAO)
options.C: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
options.D: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB)

correct_answer_text: The Systems Engineering Body of Knowledge (SEBoK)

question: Per Section 6.6, which pair of domains is BOTH explicitly listed among those spanned by the SEBoK implementation examples?

options.A: Defense systems and management systems
options.B: Retail systems and consumer electronics systems
options.C: Agricultural systems and educational systems
options.D: Recreational systems and environmental systems

correct_answer_text: Defense systems and management systems
